The datagram payload **is** the AX.25 frame body (the FCS-less KISS-form bytes the listener already produces): send forwards them verbatim, or with the CRC-16 FCS appended (`includeFcs` — XRouter/AXIP-CRC; default off for LinBPQ BPQAXIP); receive surfaces each datagram as a `KissCommand.Data` `KissFrame`, **stripping + validating the FCS when the link uses one** (mandatory — `Ax25Frame.TryParse` rejects an S-frame with trailing bytes, so an unstripped FCS tail would silently drop every RR/RNR ack). CSMA setters are inert no-ops (UDP has no carrier to sense). The marquee proof: **two real pdn instances connect to each other over AXUDP on loopback** (a full SABM/UA + I-frame request/response over real UDP), so the C# node can be the AXUDP peer a remote node (e.g. the Rust Pico-W) dials.
- **Slow-channel defaults — a named, opt-in per-port profile, not a silent default.** The spec-default T1 (6 s) stalls connected-mode on a slow half-duplex AFSK channel (two equal phase-locked T1 timers collide forever — #292). Per §2 the engine's spec defaults are **unchanged**; tuning is decided at the node-host layer as a **named, opt-in** `profile:` field on a port (`slow-afsk1200`), resolved by a pure `ChannelProfiles.Resolve` that fills only the AX.25/KISS fields the operator left unset (explicit always wins; no profile = exact spec defaults). The slow profile lengthens T1 to 10 s — deliberately **above** the spec 6 s and common peer defaults so the two stations' timers run asymmetric and break the phase-lock (the #292 lever, pinned by the engine's `HalfDuplexContentionTests`) — plus N2=15 and the KISS-spec CSMA defaults (PERSIST=63/SLOTTIME=10, confirmed sufficient for two-station contention by the NinoTNC characterisation) and a generic AFSK TXDELAY=30. The profile deliberately does **not** set a TX tail: the need for a non-zero TXTAIL is a *modem + radio-audio-path* property (a software modem — Dire Wolf / samoyed — needs one, and so does a NinoTNC into a radio with a latency audio path, but a NinoTNC into a fully analogue path needs none, even on this exact channel), which the node can't infer from the channel — so `kiss.txTail` stays an explicit per-port operator setting. It is **not** an automatic blanket default because T1/TXDELAY are physical-channel properties and one node can host fast (AXUDP/9600/full-duplex) and slow ports at once — a node-wide default would be wrong for the fast ones. The validator rejects a typo'd profile name; a profile change is a single-port restart in the reconcile planner; the template documents it and shows the slow profile on the NinoTNC example (and explicitly *not* on AXUDP). Strict-by-default is intact.

**Tentative roadmap — slices 2–5 (shape only; sequencing + scope live in [#167](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/167)):**

- **Slice 2 — SQLite persistence (`pdn.db`), behind the same seams.** **Done — routing table:** the learned NET/ROM routing table is persisted to a consolidated `pdn.db` (raw SQL + Dapper, no EF) so a restart restores the topology instead of going blind until the next NODES broadcast — hydrate-on-start with downtime-aged obsolescence, save on the sweep tick / after a debounced ingest / on graceful shutdown, behind an `INetRomRoutingStore` seam; resilient (a store fault degrades to in-memory) — see [§17](#17-amendment-log). **Done — config-in-DB (#473):** `SqliteConfigProvider` now stores the live config as a single versioned JSON-blob row (`node_config`) in the same `pdn.db`, behind the unchanged `IConfigProvider`/`IWritableConfigProvider`/`NodeConfig` seam — so the reconcile path + every consumer are byte-for-byte the same; only *where config lives* changed. First boot migrates the legacy `/etc` YAML (else a seed/template) into the row, idempotently (the row gates re-import); writes (`PUT /config`, port CRUD) persist to the DB and raise the same `OnChange`; the `FileSystemWatcher`/debounce/echo machinery is gone (the DB is the single writer). The YAML conffile is **dropped from packaging** so dpkg stops prompting on upgrade; the vestigial `/etc` YAML is read once then ignored, edited now via the web UI / API / `pdn config export|import`. See [`docs/config-in-db.md`](config-in-db.md) + [§17](#17-amendment-log). **Deferred:** `packets.db` rolling capture (its own DB when built — keeping the hot path isolated, no sprawl).
- **Slice 3 — REST API + auth. 🟡 contract LOCKED + UI built; the read half + live feed SHIPPED, writes/auth pending.** The §7 API is locked as OpenAPI 3.1 in [`docs/node-api.yaml`](node-api.yaml) (derived from the built UI's typed client + the design handoff). The endpoints/SSE/auth shape: minimal-API endpoints (status, ports + lifecycle, sessions + connect/disconnect/send, netrom/routes, config + schema/raw/validate, links, log) + the `/events` SSE feed + Argon2id local users + WebAuthn/passkeys + JWT `read`/`operate`/`admin` scopes; first-start admin bootstrap + one-time `/setup?token=…`; the web server serves the SPA + stops being inert. **Done (read + live feed + config-write):** static-serve of the SPA + the read `/api/v1` endpoints (step 1), every screen rewired to the API client (step 1a), the live `/events` SSE feed + frame/byte telemetry making the monitor/frames-per-sec/link-stats live (step 1b), and config-write through the reconcile path — `PUT /config` (+ `/config/raw`) with validate-before-apply + a server-authoritative reconcile preview, wired into the editor's "Review & apply" (step 2). Port management — add/edit/remove + enable/disable over the same config-write path — landed too (step 3). The serialized direct-supervisor actions landed in step 4: sessions connect/disconnect/send + the port `restart` lifecycle, each run under the host's exclusive gate (the connect dial bounded outside it) so a web action never races a reconcile; the Sessions screen + Ports restart button are wired live. Step 5 added the **interactive sysop console**: a connect-out's live output is streamed to the web Sessions drawer over `GET /sessions/{id}/stream` (SSE) with type-in via the send endpoint. **Verified live against the real GB7RDG/LinBPQ node** (connect → banner + the `N` nodes-list streamed into the drawer; restart/send/disconnect exercised). Step 6 added connectionless **TEST ping** (`POST /ping` → `PingResult`, via a new `Ax25Listener.SendTestAsync` + the `AxPinger` correlation core) + the web ping tool — verified live (LinBPQ doesn't answer TEST → honest 100% loss). The **auth foundation** is now BUILT (default-OFF behind `management.auth.enabled`): Argon2id local users + a persisted-in-`pdn.db` HS256 JWT, `read`/`operate`/`admin` scope gates that pass through when the flag is off (so no regression) and enforce when on, login + the one-shot `/setup` bootstrap + admin-gated `/users`; backend only, pending the human's security review before it's ever enabled. **Still ahead:** wiring the UI's login/setup/users screens to the new endpoints (they stay on their mock gate today); WebAuthn/passkeys + TOTP/2FA + refresh tokens (named deferrals — the LAN-trust/naming problem passkeys hit on a node distributed to strangers, a candidate `B + mDNS` distribution pattern, and the simpler `localhost` flow to use for passkey dev *now*, are worked out in [`docs/passkeys-lan-trust-pattern.md`](passkeys-lan-trust-pattern.md)); a TEST *responder* (we DM an inbound TEST today — answering it is spec-compliance + enables pdn↔pdn ping), the per-`portId` connect dial (needs a per-port connector factory on the supervisor), the link-tuner/`tune` flow (held for a design pass on net-sim tuneables), beacons, then a self-hosted web-UI CI job + ship. See §17 (2026-06-09 auth-foundation / step-6 / step-5 / step-4 / step-3 / step-2 / step-1b / step-1 + 2026-06-08 Phase-5-UI entries).
- **Slice 4 — MCP + monitoring.** `Packet.Mcp` over stdio + SSE (read-mostly ops/diagnostics/network-exploration tools, write tools behind a separate scope); live packet monitor v2 + link troubleshooting beyond plain frame tracing (per-link RTT/retries/REJ-SREJ counts).
- **Slice 5 — React web UI.** Vite + React + TS + Tailwind + shadcn/ui (Decision §3); the routes in §5.5; zero-file-editing config.

**App platform — Slice 6: the app catalog ("Available apps") 🟡 (design 2026-06-13, [`docs/app-catalog.md`](app-catalog.md); build in progress).** Ship the *index*, not the payloads: a curated `catalog/apps.yaml` baked into the deb lets the owner browse available-but-not-installed apps and install them from the browser (URL-fetch of a sha256-pinned artifact, or `.pdnapp` upload) into the owner root, where the existing discovery + supervisor take over (install lands a disabled package; enable is the run-grant). Three artifact kinds (`assets`/`deb`/`pdnapp`); DAPPS moves out of the deb (~73→~40 MB); initial vetted set dapps/bpqchat/convers. A1 embedded now, A2 signed network refresh deferred (drop-in). See [§17](#17-amendment-log).

**NET/ROM L3+L4 🟡 (landed alongside the node host; full detail in [§5.9](#59-phase-9--plugin-api--netrom--hardening--post-v1-174) + [§17](#17-amendment-log)).** A node-level `NetRomService` subscribes every port's `Ax25Listener.FrameTraced` tap, hears NODES broadcasts (UI, PID 0xCF, dest `NODES`), and builds a routing table (in the new hand-written `Packet.NetRom` library) it surfaces in `Nodes` — the read-only awareness slice (on by default; harmless; cannot disturb a session). The **full vanilla L3+L4 body** then rode in behind it: NODES origination (opt-in `netRom.broadcast`), the L4 virtual-circuit transport + `CircuitManager`, interlink sessions, and `connect <alias>` routing across the network (opt-in `netRom.connect`). INP3 + real-BPQ-L4 interop are deferred. See §5.9.

### 5.5 Phase 5 — Web UI ✅ ([#170](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/170))

Vite + React + TS + Tailwind + shadcn/ui. **Built at [`web/packetnet-ui/`](../web/packetnet-ui) (2026-06-08)** — all ten screens (login, setup, dashboard, live monitor, sessions, routes, ports, config, users + link-tuner/ping tools) recreated from the converged Claude Design handoff, running against a typed mock backend (flip `VITE_API_MODE=live` to talk to the real Slice-3 API). `tsc`+`vite build` clean; a `vitest` render smoke test covers every screen (10/10). Awaiting the Slice-3 backend (`docs/node-api.yaml`) to go live against the node. See §17 (2026-06-08 Phase-5-UI entry).

### 5.6 Phase 6 — RHPv2 + AGW ⬜ ([#171](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/171))

Mirror RHPv2 framing from `/home/tf/src/rhp2lib-net/src/RhpV2.Client/Protocol/`. TCP + WS listeners. `--linbpq-compat` flag for the WS subset. AGW server **127.0.0.1 only** by default; `--listen-public --i-understand-agw-is-unauthenticated` required to expose.

### 5.7 Phase 7 — Packaging + self-update 🟡 ([#172](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/172))

The `.deb` build (`scripts/build-deb.sh`) + `node-v*` GitHub Releases are **done** (§9, §17). **The apt repo is no longer in packet.net's scope** — the Debian maintainer (**hibby**) already runs + signs the OARC reprepro repo and is handling distro packaging; packet.net's job on the packaged side is just to be a *well-behaved package* (never self-mutate) and a polite update notifier.

**Slice 1 (the apt-channel apply path) is shipped:** `GET /api/v1/system/info` + the channel-aware `POST /api/v1/system/update` (`PdnSystemApi`, admin-gated + audited), the `IInstallChannelProvider`/`ISystemUpdateLauncher` seams (`Packet.Node.Core/SelfUpdate`), and the packaging — the `install-channel=apt` marker, the `packetnet-update.service` oneshot, the `packetnet-apt-update` helper (targeted `apt-get install --only-upgrade` + health-gate + downgrade rollback), and the `49-packetnet-update.rules` polkit rule (`Depends: polkitd | policykit-1`). On `apt`, Apply dispatches the detached helper and 202s; self-contained 501s; unknown 409s.

**Slice 2 (the self-contained channel) is in progress:** `publish-node.yml` now emits per-arch self-contained `*.tar.gz` + a `latest.json` manifest + an extended `SHA256SUMS` (the download targets). Decided 2026-06-13: **checksum-only** integrity to start (cosign is a hardening follow-up); **migrations stay additive** (binary rollback doesn't roll back `pdn.db`); and — since packet.net is private — the self-contained **feed needs a public host** (operator's domain / OARC), the node's feed URL being config. Still to build: the node-side helper (download → sha256-verify → unpack `releases/<new>` → flip `current` → restart → `/healthz` rollback), `installers/install.sh`, and the API generalization (self-contained stops 501-ing).

Remaining beyond that: the **web UI** Apply button + version-poll completion UX; deepening the apt health-gate from `is-active` to a `/healthz` probe; **cosign** signing/verify; the available-version check feeding the UI. Full design + status: [`docs/node-self-update-design.md`](node-self-update-design.md).

### 5.8 Phase 8 — MCP + live monitor v2 + link troubleshooting 🟡 ([#173](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/173))

**Design AGREED 2026-06-13 — full spec in [`docs/mcp-design.md`](mcp-design.md).** `Packet.Mcp` over stdio + SSE (Streamable HTTP, which subsumes the deprecated raw-SSE the §5.8/§6 text names). Read tools: `list_ports`, `list_sessions`, `recent_frames(filter)`, `link_quality(remote)`, `network_topology`, `decode_frame(hex)`. Write tools: `send_ui_frame`, `reset_port`, `disconnect_session`, `set_kiss_param` — audit-logged. Link troubleshoot view: per-link RTT, retries, REJ/SREJ counts, T1/T3 graphs.

**Status (2026-06-13): the MCP server is built across both transports + monitor-v2 data is surfaced** — see the §17 build-step 1–4 entries. Done: the full §5.8 tool surface (6 read + 4 write) in `Packet.Mcp` behind `INodeMcpBackend`; `decode_frame` (pure, golden-tested); the in-process `LiveNodeMcpBackend` + the `/mcp` SSE transport (read-gated, operate for writes, audited); the `pdn mcp` stdio bridge (`RestNodeMcpBackend`, verified end-to-end); and monitor-v2's live **SRT + retry count** feeding `link_quality` + the REST `/links` (the hard-coded zeros retired). **Remaining:** the internet-peer/PIN-relay + mode-coordination halves of the link-tuner (the **SDM-coordinated deviation tuner shipped in PDN 2026-07-04** at `/tools/tuner` — node session API + web trend table + pause/guaranteed-restore; see §17) and (optionally) a DB-persisted write audit (the cross-cutting §6 item). *(The **T1/T3 graphs** web link-troubleshoot view shipped 2026-06-17, #467 — see the §17 entry.)* `set_kiss_param` has no KISS-modem write path yet (honest not-yet-wired). 

Kickoff decisions: **in-process** (the server reads live `NodeHostedService` state, not a REST re-projection — `Packet.Mcp` holds the transport-agnostic tool surface behind an `INodeMcpBackend` seam, with a live in-proc backend for SSE and a loopback-REST backend for the `pdn mcp` stdio bridge); auth is the **shipped `read`/`operate`/`admin` model** (read tools `read`, write tools `operate` + audit), *not* the `mcp:invoke`/granular scheme §6 once penciled in but never built (§6 corrected below); **full surface in the opening slice** (read + write + both transports), with an internal build order ending in the monitor-v2 piece. **Monitor v2** is the one part needing new instrumentation: SRTT/retries aren't derivable from the frame tap (`PdnReadApi.BuildLinks` honestly stubs them to 0), so it surfaces per-session T1-SRTT/retries/T3 from the AX.25 engine to feed `link_quality`, the REST `/links` stubs, and the T1/T3 troubleshoot view. No ax25-ts parity leg (not a parser flag / not the listener surface). The deferred link-tuner stays parked here.

### 5.9 Phase 9 — Plugin API + NET/ROM + hardening ⬜ (post-v1) ([#174](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/174))

**App extensibility — design AGREED 2026-06-10; the in-proc `.dll`-plugin model below is SUPERSEDED.** The full design is [`docs/app-extensibility.md`](app-extensibility.md): out-of-process, **language-agnostic** apps (not .NET-only), split into a **packet plane** (a pdn-native *local-session* seam for local apps like WALL + the **RHPv2 server** for *network* apps — making pdn an XRouter-compatible host so any [rhp2lib](https://rhp2lib.pages.dev/) app runs against pdn unchanged) and a **human plane** (a manifest + reverse-proxy + auth-gateway shell that surfaces an app's web UI under `/apps/{id}` without pdn knowing the app). The node owner owns trust; apps declare a capability manifest; the console is app #0 via a new `INodeApplication`. Slicing: (1) ✅ **DONE** — `INodeApplication` + the `applications:` registry + the `pdn-app/1` external-process stdio wire + WALL as a fully-separated out-of-process Python app (see the 2026-06-10 §17 Slice-1 entry; `examples/wall/` + [`docs/app-local-session-wire.md`](app-local-session-wire.md)) → (2) ✅ **DONE** — next rung of the local-session seam: a long-running Unix-socket daemon (`kind: socket`) for shared cross-user state; **LOBBY** worked example (`examples/lobby/`, live presence + broadcast); see the 2026-06-10 §17 Slice-2 entry → (3) ✅ **DONE** — app-gateway (manifest + reverse-proxy + auth shell + WALL web view; see the 2026-06-10 §17 Slice-3 entry + [`docs/app-gateway.md`](app-gateway.md)) → (4) `Packet.Rhp2.Server` → (5) BBS/Chat/DAPPS. WALL ships as a reference app *and* the documentation worked-example. **Superseded sketch (kept for history):** `Packet.Node.Extensions/IApplicationModule.cs` REST/MCP/frontend/AX.25 handler loaded from `*.dll` in an isolated `AssemblyLoadContext` — replaced because it was .NET-only + in-process (no blast-radius isolation, no XRouter interop); see the doc's "Why not in-proc .NET plugins". `Packet.NetRom` ships as a separate package. SharpFuzz harnesses + 72 h soak vs LinBPQ.

**NET/ROM — full vanilla L3+L4 stack 🟡 (read-only slice + the L3+L4 body + L3 transit forwarding shipped; INP3 deferred; see [§17](#17-amendment-log)).** The read-only "NET/ROM aware" slice was pulled forward off the Phase-9 body (a pure consumer of the existing `Ax25Listener.FrameTraced` tap that writes nothing) and the **full vanilla L3+L4 body** then rode in behind it. NET/ROM is hand-written, **not** through ax25sdl (no SDL figures, no single normative standard — BPQ is the de-facto reference); every divergence is a named flag/knob (`NetRomParseOptions`, `NetRomRoutingOptions`, `NetRomCircuitOptions`), never a silent BPQ-ism, and documented in the strict-vs-pragmatic audit. Shipped:

- **Read-only L3 ingest (the slice).** `Packet.NetRom` NODES wire parse + the L3 routing table (quality decay, obsolescence, 3-best routes, trivial-loop guard, MINQUAL floor, table caps); `NetRomService` above `PortSupervisor` hears NODES on every port and surfaces the table in `Nodes`. Incapable of disturbing a QSO.
- **L3 origination.** `NodesBroadcastBuilder` encodes the table → NODES info field(s) (≤11×21-byte entries per UI frame, multi-frame); a new `Ax25Listener.SendUiAsync` unproto send path; `NetRomService` originates our NODES broadcast on the NODESINTERVAL schedule with the OBSMIN advertise-gate + obsolescence decrement. Opt-in (`netRom.broadcast`, default off — TX is opt-in).
- **L4 virtual circuits.** A hand-written `NetRomCircuit` transport FSM (connect/ack + window negotiation, info/info-ack over the sliding window with choke flow-control + selective-NAK, disconnect/ack, retransmit/timeout, 236-byte fragment/reassembly) + a `CircuitManager` (circuit table indexed by index/id, inbound demux, mint/accept/refuse), `TimeProvider`-driven. The 15-byte L3 network header + 5-byte L4 transport header + opcode/flag wire types.
- **`connect <alias>` routing.** `NetRomOutboundConnector` (wired at the existing `IOutboundConnector` seam) resolves a distant alias/callsign via the routing table → opens/reuses an interlink AX.25 session (PID 0xCF) to the best neighbour → originates an L4 circuit end-to-end → bridges the inbound console ⇄ the circuit; falls back to the slice-1 same-port AX.25 dial when there's no NET/ROM route. An inbound circuit (a user routed to us) is bridged to a fresh node console. Opt-in (`netRom.connect`, default off).
- **L3 datagram forwarding (the transit/router role).** A node relays a datagram addressed to *another* node onward toward its destination instead of terminating it — the network-layer routing that makes the node a router, not just an endpoint. The pure `NetRomForwarding.Decide` (TTL decrement/cap, loop-back-to-origin drop, no-bounce-back next-hop, mirroring BPQ `L4Code.c`) + `NetRomService` interlink I/O. `netRom.forward`, default on under `Connect`. Validated by deterministic decision tests + a 3-node bridge transit test (§17 2026-06-06).

**Real-BPQ-L4 interop — done** (§17 2026-06-05, §7.1 row ✅): the transmitting NET/ROM stack (NODES origination + L4 circuits + `connect`-routing) is validated against a real LinBPQ over the docker stack both ways (BPQ learns pdn; pdn↔BPQ L4 circuits establish + carry data + disconnect). Surfaced + fixed one BPQ framing divergence — the Connect Request's proposed window + originating user/node live in the **info field**, not the transport-header TX byte (the canonical layout; new `ConnectRequestInfo` codec). **Deferred:** INP3 (the triggered/RTT routing overlay — a self-contained later increment; a full cross-stack implementation plan now lives at [`docs/netrom-inp3-plan.md`](netrom-inp3-plan.md) — default-off overlay, our-own-fleet as the first-class interop target, BPQ/XRouter best-effort). **Closed 2026-06-07 (the two small hardening follow-ups):** (1) the **link-down / quality-collapse failover signal** is now built — `NetRomRoutingTable.MarkNeighbourDown` drops a dead neighbour's routes at once (instead of waiting for the obsolescence sweep) and `NetRomService` raises it on an interlink **dial failure**, so a transit forward / `connect <alias>` re-routes around a dead next hop immediately (the connect path runs a failover loop over the destination's routes). (2) The **inbound-interlink Connect-Request race** was investigated: pdn's side is already robust — the 0xCF tap is attached synchronously before the next frame, a retransmitted Connect Request is deduped (`byPeerKey`), and a double-delivered datagram is absorbed by the circuit's sequence dedup; the only residual ("BPQ pipelines the Connect Request on a fresh lossy link and doesn't re-originate it") is a BPQ-side non-re-origination not fixable from our side without violating AX.25. Shipped a tap-hygiene cleanup en route (a re-established interlink no longer accumulates duplicate 0xCF taps). See [§17](#17-amendment-log).

### 5.10 Phase 10 — Hardware ecosystem & adaptive RF ⬜ (post-v1) ([#175](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/175))

The differentiator no other TNC stack does well: treat the radio + modem as first-class telemetry sources, and use their signals to drive AX.25's parameter knobs in real time. Unlocks a class of "you can see your link degrading and recover before it dies" UX that doesn't exist today.

**Workstreams:**

- **Tait 8100 / 8200 CCDI integration** — serial control channel to the radio surfaces SNR, signal-quality, MAS/SQ status, busy detect, channel programming. Three existing PoC repos by Tom (will be rewritten/folded into Packet.NET, not vendored):
  - [`M0LTE/tait-ccdi`](https://github.com/M0LTE/tait-ccdi) — protocol layer.
  - [`M0LTE/TaitMaster`](https://github.com/M0LTE/TaitMaster) — management UI.
  - [`M0LTE/taitctrl`](https://github.com/M0LTE/taitctrl) — CLI control.
  - Spec reference: [CCDI manual](https://wiki.oarc.uk/_media/radios:tm8100-protocol-manual.pdf) (PDF).
  - Target `Packet.Radio.Tait` library with `IRadioControl` abstraction so other radios can slot in (Yaesu CAT, ICOM CI-V, …).
  - 🟡 **First cut shipped 2026-07-02** (see §17): `Packet.Radio` (`IRadioControl` v0 + `RssiTaggingTransport`) + `Packet.Radio.Tait` (CCDI driver) + `tools/Packet.Tait.Spike`; per-frame RSSI/SNR and hardware-DCD events proven on the bench rig. Channel change / CCR frequency programming not yet modelled; Tom's PoC repos not yet mined for the management/CLI layers.
  - 🟢 **Node API surfacing shipped 2026-07-04** (see §17): the radio-control arc reaches `Packet.Node`'s `/api/v1` — per-frame RSSI/SNR on `/events`/`/monitor/recent`/`/traffic`/`/heard` (via a parity-safe node-owned inbound tap, kept off the AX.25 listener contract), per-port radio status+health on `/api/v1/radios` + `/ports/{id}/radio` (`TaitRadioHealthMonitor` wired per port), a `/radios/scan` discovery endpoint keyed by CCDI serial (+ `/dev/serial/by-id`), and `radio.serial:` stable config binding. Web UI + adaptive-parameter feedback still to come.
  - 🟢 **FFSK Transparent transport shipped 2026-07-04** (see §17): `Packet.Radio.Tait.TaitTransparentTransport` — a **TNC-less** AX.25 link over the radio's own internal FFSK modem (Transparent mode = an 8-bit-clean byte pipe), KISS-SLIP framed, with the transport owning the transmission so it stamps per-frame TX/RX airtime timing (`TxTiming` event + `ITxCompletionTransport`; inbound `ReceivedAt` + `RadioMetadata.EstimatedAirtime`). New `tait-transparent` PDN port kind (bind by CCDI serial or device path). The inherent trade-off vs NinoTNC+CCDI (`RssiTaggingTransport`): one device and no audio wiring, but **no signal telemetry** — RSSI/SNR/noise-floor/DCD are unavailable while the CCDI channel is a byte pipe. Hardware round-trip proven radio-to-radio on the rig.
- **Frequency-agile operation** — if the radio is CAT-controllable, schedule QSY across a frequency plan. Use cases: APRS digi tail on calling channel + drop to working channel for connected-mode sessions; per-link QSY when SNR drops below threshold; automatic channel hunting in poor band conditions.
- **NinoTNC mode agility / negotiation** — currently the operator picks a NinoTNC mode (0–15) at config. Goal: query NinoTNC capabilities at startup, negotiate the optimal mode for current channel quality (1200 → 4800 → 9600 based on SNR), renegotiate on degradation. Requires SETHW probe + mode-change handshake (open question: does NinoTNC firmware support runtime mode change?).
  - 🟡 **Coordination seed shipped 2026-07-03** (see §17): the mode-change handshake exists and is hardware-proven — `IRadioSideChannel` (`Packet.Radio`, radio-native small-datagram control plane; Tait SDM implementation) + the propose/confirm/commit/probe-verify/revert-to-home protocol (`ModeCoordinator`/`ModeResponder` in `Packet.Tune.Core`) + `packet-tune mode-coord`. The side channel is mode/channel-agnostic, which breaks the negotiate-over-the-link-being-changed chicken-and-egg. Node-side integration (ports, beacons, policy) not started — the map is [`docs/research/radio-side-channel-mode-agility.md`](research/radio-side-channel-mode-agility.md).
- **NinoTNC firmware upgrades** — port [`ninocarrillo/flashtnc`](https://github.com/ninocarrillo/flashtnc) flow into `packetnet ctl flash-tnc` so non-technical users can update firmware from the web UI. Bootloader protocol reverse-engineering needed.
- **Channel-quality scoring** — fuse radio telemetry (SNR, RSSI, busy %) with AX.25 telemetry (RC, REJ/SREJ rate, RTT) into a per-link "quality index". Surface in UI; feed back into adaptive parameter tuning.
- **Adaptive AX.25 parameters** — T1, RC, k, mod-8 vs mod-128 chosen dynamically from the quality score. Currently §C.4 leaves these fixed. Care needed — interop with non-adaptive peers must remain spec-compliant.

**Hardware test harness** ✅ (landed 2026-07-02): 2× NinoTNC + 2× Tait TM8110 on USB, TNC audio into the radios, radios coupled via ~100 dB attenuation, CCDI serial on both radios at 28800. First autonomous spike complete — capability dump in `artifacts/radio-capabilities/`, per-frame RSSI/SNR + DCD proven (see §17 2026-07-02).

**Exit criteria** (high-level):

- `IRadioControl` abstraction with at least Tait CCDI implementation. ✅ (2026-07-02, experimental — shape may still move; see OQ-011)
- Per-link quality index visible in web UI + persisted to time-series.
- At least one adaptive parameter (T1 or k) wired to quality feedback under a `--adaptive` flag.
- NinoTNC mode-change demonstrated end-to-end (manual trigger, no auto-negotiation needed for exit).
- `packetnet ctl flash-tnc` working from CLI; web UI integration optional.

### 5.11 Phase 11 — Close the loop: a self-measuring, self-reconfiguring network ⬜ (post-v1)

Phase 10 gave PDN **eyes** (per-frame RSSI/SNR, hardware DCD, radio health, the doctor) and **hands**
(mode change, TXDELAY, and — barely used — CCR frequency retune, all coordinatable over the SDM
side channel, which works precisely *because* it is independent of the packet modulation). Phase 11
is the **nervous system** connecting them: sense degradation, decide, act. Framing from the
2026-07-04 review with Tom (a captured direction, not committed scope; ordered by near-term
tractability).

- **Native DCD into the AX.25 medium-access layer (OQ-012) — foundational, in progress 2026-07-04.**
  The stack already has a **link-multiplexer** seam (`LinkMultiplexerSignal` — LM_SEIZE/RELEASE, "the
  medium-access arbiter that owns the radio"). Wire hardware carrier-sense (`IRadioControl` DCD;
  later Nino's KISS DCD extension) into the seize path so the AX.25 stack **natively** holds the
  medium while the channel is busy — i.e. emulate a TNC that exposes DCD, retiring the interim
  transport-level `CarrierSenseTxGate`. The correct home for the 0.5–1 s DCD head start, and the
  substrate the rest builds on.

- **Noise-floor-aware channel selection & (where legal) frequency agility.** CCR retunes RX in
  ≤20 ms; that plus an RSSI read is a fast **channel scanner** — pick the lowest-noise-floor /
  least-occupied channel, and (where band plans allow) QSY a link on congestion/degradation,
  coordinated over SDM with the same propose/confirm/commit machinery the mode-agility work ships.
  **Regulatory reality (Tom):** UK fixed packet stations have **only ~3 channels on 2 m** — no free
  rein — so full frequency-hopping is not a UK-station behaviour; but this is **global software** and
  applies where band plans are freer. The broadly-useful slice is **lowest-noise-floor channel
  *selection*** (worthwhile even among 2–3 channels), and it is **especially interesting for HF
  packet** (crowded, propagation-variable) — HF needs different radios, not these Taits, but the same
  software. Actuator + coordination exist; the missing piece is the scan-and-choose logic + band-plan
  guardrails.

- **Distributed spectrum & link-quality map (central stats).** Every PDN node is now an instrumented
  receiver (per-frame RSSI/SNR landed in Phase 10; the observability exporter carries it — see the
  metrics workstream). Aggregate across a network into a live **who-hears-whom-at-what-SNR** map +
  channel-occupancy view. Feeds: *real* NET/ROM routing quality (today a static config number → give
  it measured, propagation-tracking link quality), propagation monitoring, and network-scale
  antenna/site fault detection. Tom: "central stats could be good." The most novel dataset — a
  capability that emerges from the *network* of instrumented nodes, not any one.

- **The adaptive controller ("the brain") — future, density-gated.** Fuse the telemetry (RSSI, SNR,
  busy%, DCD duty, FEC-corrected-byte rate, RTT, TXDELAY estimate) into a link-quality score and drive
  the knobs (mode down on SNR loss, TXDELAY optimisation, k/window sizing, QSY on congestion) in real
  time — Phase 10's original "see your link degrading and recover before it dies." Sensors and
  actuators all exist; the controller does not. Tom: "could be good but requires a high density of
  PDN — one for the future." Explicitly density-gated; not near-term.

**Explicitly out of scope:** GPS / position-over-SDM (Tom: not interested), despite the radios
supporting GPS-SDM.

**The through-line:** channel selection, the central map, and the adaptive brain are three views of
one idea — *a packet network that measures itself and reconfigures itself*, which does not exist in
amateur packet today. The Tait CCDI/CCR channel makes it possible on VHF/UHF (calibrated RSSI + fast
retune + a mode-agnostic coordination bearer on one link); the HF story wants different radios but the
same software.

### 5.X Spike backlog 🟡 (issues [#176](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/176)–[#184](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/184))

Smaller, time-boxed exploration tasks. Not phase-sequenced — each can land
whenever it's the highest-leverage next thing. Listed in approximate order
of leverage-per-effort. Status: SP-001/004/005/006/009/010 are tracked +
closed as completed; SP-002 core landed (segmentation note in §17); SP-003,
SP-007, SP-008 remain open.

**SP-001 — LinBPQ MQTT frame feed ingestion** ([#176](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/176)) — ✅ tracked + closed as completed (see [#176](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/176)). (high leverage, low effort).
Tom operates a live LinBPQ node with four real RF ports and is willing to
expose an MQTT feed of every AX.25 frame sent/received (with or without KISS
wrapper). Build a `Packet.Replay.MqttIngest` tool that subscribes to the
feed, decodes frames, runs them through our parser, and emits structured
records (frame type, callsigns, control bits, payload size, decoded errors).
Acts as a 24/7 corpus of real-world frame traffic across four real RF
channels — vastly richer than synthetic test traffic. Specifically catches
parser edge cases that property-tests won't generate. Feeds the regression
corpus in SP-003.

**SP-002 — Direwolf-as-reference end-to-end harness** ([#177](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/177)) (high leverage, medium
effort). **✅ core landed 2026-06-03 (V5b real-peer leg) — `docker/direwolf/` + `DirewolfMod128Interop`.** Step 0 passed: Dire Wolf as responder answers SABME at mod-128 with no config knob (source + on the wire). The harness is the two-direwolf audio-loopback path (KISS-gateway + AGW-driven responder over a shared PulseAudio null sink — *not* net-sim, which can't bridge to an external Dire Wolf modem; the AGW echo app is a minimal Python helper since `Packet.Agw.AgwClient` is connect-initiator-only). mod-128 connect / bidirectional transfer / SREJ recovery / XID-up-to-128 are green vs Dire Wolf; segmentation is blocked by a Figure-6.2-vs-Dire-Wolf first-segment inner-PID divergence (§17). **History — corrected scope (2026-06-03 — full detail in the [#177](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/177) comment):** "containerise direwolf + expose KISS-TCP + point Packet.NET at it" is NOT enough — KISS is raw-frame passthrough that bypasses direwolf's connected-mode engine (`ax25_link.c`), which is reachable only via the AGW protocol + a registered client app (`dl_register_callsign`). The real harness is **direwolf + an AGW accept/echo app** (register callsign, accept the incoming connect, echo data). Step 0 was verifying on the wire that direwolf, as responder, answers an inbound SABME at modulo-128 (the BPQ-shaped unknown). For every transcribed transition, A/B our orchestrator's behaviour against direwolf's actual output. Direwolf is the closest-to-figure
implementation (see SI-* triangulation in [`docs/spec-issues.md`](spec-issues.md)),
so behavioural equivalence with direwolf is a strong correctness signal.
Strongest force multiplier on the SDL transcription work.

**SP-003 — Replay/record harness** ([#178](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/178)) (high leverage, medium effort). pcap-
style timestamped capture of KISS + AX.25 wire bytes with per-frame direction
+ port + source labels. Replay later to repro bugs, build a regression
library of weird-frames-seen-in-the-wild, and A/B real on-air behaviour
against the orchestrator's projection. Foundation for SP-001's persistence
+ for future "I saw a strange frame, replay this against the state machine"
debugging. Storage as JSON-lines or capnproto; both fine.

**SP-004 — AX.25 wire-format fuzzer (SharpFuzz)** ([#179](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/179)) — ✅ tracked + closed as completed (see [#179](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/179)). (medium leverage, low
effort). Plan §7 already promises this for nightly. Frame parser as target.
Mostly mechanical: a fuzz harness against `Ax25Frame.TryParse`, plus
`KissFrame.TryParse`. Likely surfaces real bugs in edge cases (oversized
fields, weird PIDs, malformed addresses). Cheap and overdue.

**SP-005 — Multi-Packet.NET-instance interop via net-sim** ([#180](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/180)) — ✅ tracked + closed as completed (see [#180](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/180)). (medium leverage,
low effort). Net-sim already in the interop stack. Spin up 2-3 Packet.NET
instances and let them talk to each other through net-sim's lossy channel.
Any drift between identical implementations exposes a state-machine bug
cheaper than against LinBPQ — and complements interop tests against
heterogeneous peers.

**SP-006 — Spec-prose-to-stub-test extractor** ([#181](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/181)) — ✅ tracked + closed as completed (see [#181](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/181)). (higher effort, payoff after
Phase 2). Parse the AX.25 v2.2 spec markdown, pull every "shall" sentence
into a stub xUnit test (initially `[Skip]`). Surfaces gaps where the SDL
transcription doesn't cover a prose mandate. Lower priority — the SDL
transcription itself already does most of the work, but this acts as a
backstop.

**SP-007 — NinoTNC KISS-side SNR proxy** ([#182](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/182)) (low leverage but useful fallback).
Even without Tait integration, can we infer link quality from frame-quality
bits / retry rates / error counts alone? If yes, gives `IRadioControl`'s
quality signal a fallback path for users without CAT-capable radios.
Investigate after SP-001 has produced enough real-world data to baseline.

**SP-008 — Full APRS encoding/decoding library** ([#183](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/183)) (large scope; significant
ecosystem value). The SDL transcription work covers AX.25's link layer; APRS
is the application-layer protocol that rides on UI frames. A native
`Packet.Aprs` library covering position/weather/mic-E/messages/status/
telemetry/objects/items would slot in cleanly above the AX.25 stack and
make Packet.NET a credible APRS gateway / parser, not just a bare TNC.

Specs:
- [APRS101.pdf](http://www.ui-view.net/files/APRS101.pdf) — the original
  1998 specification.
- [`how.aprs.works/aprs101-pdf-is-obsolete`](https://how.aprs.works/aprs101-pdf-is-obsolete/)
  — community-maintained corrections, clarifications, and deprecations
  layered on top of the 1998 spec. Both are needed to model real-world
  APRS faithfully.

Approach mirrors the AX.25 SDL pipeline where possible:
- Trust-the-spec encode-then-verify discipline.
- Property tests for round-trip (decode → encode → assert byte-equal) on
  every payload type.
- A real-world corpus (the SP-001b APRS-IS feed already exists; the
  forthcoming SP-001 LinBPQ-MQTT feed will be richer) gives free fuzz
  coverage.

Likely sequenced after the LinBPQ-MQTT feed lands so the library can be
validated against real traffic from day one. Open question: do we want
to support APRS Messaging end-to-end (would need state for acks +
retries) or strictly the parsing/encoding surface? Decide before
starting.

**SP-009 — Visual state-machine display** ([#184](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/184)) — ✅ tracked + closed as completed (see [#184](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/184)); the static (Mermaid-emitter) rung shipped, the live-UI rung remains the deferred future-option below. (medium leverage, low effort for the static rung). Two rungs of increasing scope. **Static rung (near-term):** add a Mermaid emitter to the SDL codegen pipeline (`tools/Packet.Sdl.CodeGen.Mermaid/`) that walks the IR and produces one `stateDiagram-v2` per page, with transitions labelled by trigger + guard. Outputs land next to the existing AX.25 figures in `docs/`, render directly in GitHub markdown, and slot naturally into the existing 7-backend codegen pattern. ~1–2 hours. **Live rung (deferred):** expose a "transition occurred" event on `Ax25Session`, render the same graph in a small UI (Avalonia desktop, or the browser-terminal track if/when SP-N lands) and highlight the current state + flash the firing edge. ~2–3 days plus whatever UI host we pick. Pay the live rung's cost only when there's a concrete demo or debug-of-live-link need driving it; the static rung pays for itself immediately as documentation. The mermaid emitter is also the natural baseline for any future graph-style export (Graphviz dot, PlantUML), so even if a different live UI is chosen later the documentation track stands alone.

**SP-010 — Strongly-typed action verbs (kill the stringly-typed dispatch bug class)** ([#260](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/260)) — ✅ tracked + closed as completed (see [#260](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/260)). (high leverage, medium effort; cross-repo: ax25sdl → packet.net + ax25-ts). Every SDL action is a free **string** — the figure's verbatim text (`"DL-UNIT-DATA Indication"`, `"set_own_receiver_busy"`, `"Invoke_Retransmission"`) — that `ActionDispatcher` switches on, with an `ActionVerbAliases` map reconciling spelling variants (display vs snake_case) and ax25-ts carrying its own `normaliseSubroutineName` layer. Nothing links the verbs the tables *emit* to the verbs a dispatcher *handles*, so spelling drift is caught only at runtime — as a throw (the UI-reception bug: a switch case left in display form while the alias mapped it to snake_case → every UI frame crashed, see the 2026-06-02 fix entry) or, worse, a silent no-op (the ax25-ts subroutine miss: `"Invoke Retransmission"` vs `"Invoke_Retransmission"` did nothing until a recovery test caught it). It keeps biting. **Proposal:** make the verb set a *generated closed type*. ax25sdl already owns an IR + a 7-backend codegen pattern; add an emitter that canonicalises each distinct action's spelling once (figure text → one token) and emits a closed verb type per language — a C# `enum`/sealed type and a TS string-literal union — with `ActionStep.Verb` carrying the typed value instead of a raw string. The dispatchers switch over the closed type, and a non-exhaustive switch becomes a **compile-time** failure (C# switch-expression exhaustiveness, CS8509, is a build error here under `TreatWarningsAsErrors`; TS via union `never`-exhaustiveness) — so a new or renamed verb in a figure fails to build in *both* runtimes until handled, instead of throwing on-air or silently no-op'ing. The runtime `ActionVerbAliases` map and `normaliseSubroutineName` both **delete**: canonicalisation moves to the codegen, the single source of truth. **Scope:** first cut = action verbs (the repeat offender); the same stringly-typed shape covers **guard atoms** (`"own_receiver_busy"` + the `and`/`not` grammar) and **event/trigger names** (`"I_received"`, `"DL_FLOW_OFF_request"` — already bit ax25-ts once), both natural follow-on closed types once the verb pattern is proven. **Legs:** ax25sdl (canonicalisation + typed-verb emitter + release), then packet.net `ActionDispatcher` and ax25-ts `action-dispatcher` consume the typed verb and drop their alias layers; mostly mechanical (rewrite two switches, delete two maps) but spans three repos + an ax25sdl release. The leverage is eliminating an entire recurring on-air-failure class at compile time across both runtimes. (The behavioural transition-coverage ledger, 2026-06-02, is the runtime backstop that catches the same class today — a typed verb set makes most of it a build error instead.)

### 5.Y Hardware-arrival probe playbook 🟡 ([#185](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/185))

> 2026-07-02: rig landed; items 1 and 2 done (capability inventory in `artifacts/radio-capabilities/`, OQ-009 answered — see §17). Items 3–6 remain; note the rig couples the *radios* via an RF attenuator (not audio cross-wire), so item 3's "TX audio level" becomes a TXDELAY/deviation sweep and item 6's mistune test applies as written.
> 2026-07-03: item 3's tooling is now in place and hardware-validated — `Packet.Tune.Core` + the `deviation-sdm`/`deviation-remote` assistant (meter: decode-rate / FEC delta / clipping / CCDI RSSI; advice UP/DN/OK) and the `doctor` capability probes (see §17 2026-07-03). The actual pot-calibration sweep on this rig is the remaining human-in-the-loop step.
> 2026-07-03 (later): **item 4 ✅ done for this rig** — the scripted `packet-tune mode-survey` swept every IL2P+CRC mode × both channel widths (narrow 12.5 kHz / wide 25 kHz, radios switched live via GO_TO_CHANNEL — first hardware use, clean) at the rig's fixed ~38 dB SNR; full 36-cell table + findings in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §Wide-channel IL2P+CRC survey (see §17 2026-07-03 mode survey). Headlines: 9600/4800 GFSK dead on BOTH widths (no IL2P sync; deviation calibration now the prime suspect — re-survey after a TX-DEV session), wide stabilises the QPSK/AFSKPLL marginals, every marginal cell is A→B (TNC A's pot). The SNR-*floor* half of item 4 (varying attenuation) stays open — this rig's pad is fixed.
> 2026-07-03 (later still): **item 4 re-run post-retap** — the GFSK "dead" verdicts were the radios' post-voice-filter RX tap point (R5), not deviation: retapped R5 → R2 (pre-filter, post-PSD-normaliser), every GFSK/4FSK mode now decodes (44-cell table, incl. the newly-selected 4FSK IL2P+CRC modes 1/3, in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §The R5→R2 retap; see §17 2026-07-03 retap entry), and the deferred mode-2/wide `deviation-sdm` baseline is captured (5/5 both rounds, `AD:OK`, level ≈ −39 dB vs new R2 idle −34.4 dB). Remaining level-sensitive work is **TNC A's TX pot** (the A→B marginals + the 9600-4FSK-A→B-on-narrow dead cell).

Concrete first-day actions for when the 2× NinoTNC + 2× Tait rig lands on
the bench. Listed so the autonomous agent has a ready playbook the moment
hardware is available.

1. **Tait capability inventory** — query both radios via CCDI, dump full
   capability list (channels, modes, frequencies, audio levels, supported
   commands) into `artifacts/radio-capabilities/<radio-serial>.json`.
2. **NinoTNC capability probe** — SETHW iteration over modes 0–15 + the
   `+16` no-flash variant; record what KISS responses come back; observe
   whether mode change is immediate or requires re-init. Directly answers
   OQ-009.
3. **SNR vs TX-level baseline** — step the Tait TX audio level across the
   cross-wire; log SNR on the receiving Tait at each level. Builds a
   per-rig calibration curve usable by the adaptive estimator.
4. **NinoTNC mode-by-SNR survey** — for each NinoTNC mode that decodes on
   the cross-wire, find the SNR floor at which BER starts climbing. Lets us
   pick mode-switch thresholds.
5. **1000-iteration AX.25 soak** — SABM → UA → I-frames → DISC → UA round-
   trip, 1000 times, through both radios. Records frame error rate, retry
   distribution, T1 variance, mode-stability across the run.
6. **Cross-coupling degradation** — TX on radio 1, deliberately mistune
   radio 2. Plot decode success vs offset. Maps the "graceful degradation"
   shape of the audio path.

Outputs all land in `artifacts/hardware-probe/<date>/` and feed into Phase
10's adaptive-parameter tuning.

### 5.Z AX.25 v2.2 completion arc ✅ (mod-128 + SABME + XID + segmentation) — completes Phase 2 ([#168](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/168))

**✅ Arc complete — shipped end-to-end as the V5c coordinated release, 2026-06-04** (`ax25sdl` 0.10.0 → packet.net libs `lib-v0.5.0` + `@packet-net/ax25` 0.6.0 → packet-term-web / axcall / packet-term-tui cascade; both runtimes at full parity; the interop matrix is green on the released commit). Full detail in [§17 2026-06-04 (V5c)](#17-amendment-log). The V1–V5 narrative below is retained as the build record.

Phase 2 delivered the mod-8 connected-mode data-link state machine end-to-end (data transfer + REJ/SREJ loss recovery, both runtimes). The *v2.2-specific* feature set is only partly present, and this arc finishes it. **Why v2.2 matters:** SABME selects "version 2.2" mode, and v2.2 is a *bundle* — mod-128 extended sequence numbers (window up to 127, for throughput on high-latency / high-rate links where a 7-frame window stalls the pipe), **plus** negotiated SREJ and segmentation. SREJ and segmentation are gated on a v2.2 connection (§1419 / §1621), reached via SABME or XID. So "do v2.2 properly" = mod-128 framing + the SABME/XID negotiation that legitimately turns the feature set on with a peer.

**Current state (the starting line).** Context plumbing for mod-128 exists (`IsExtended`, `Modulus`, byte-width V(S)/V(A)/V(R), k defaults 4/32); the `AwaitingV22Connection` state + `SABME` / `Establish_Extended_Data_Link` verbs are wired; SREJ recovery works **in mod-8** but is forced on by a config flag (`SrejEnabled`), not negotiated; `Segmenter`/`Reassembler` exist as **standalone utilities, unwired** into the DL-DATA path; XID is **frame-plumbing only** — the MDL negotiation FSM is unimplemented ("for now we use defaults"); and the wire codec (`Ax25Frame.Control`) is **1-byte only**, with `ActionDispatcher` throwing (`RequireMod8`) on extended N(S)/N(R). The coverage ledger shows `AwaitingV22Connection` at 0/25 because nothing drives a SABME connect.

**Cross-cutting.** Every phase has an **ax25-ts parity leg** (the TS runtime mirrors the C# one — see `[[ax25-ecosystem-release-map]]`). **ax25sdl** involvement is small — figc4.6 (AwaitingV22Connection), XID handling, and segmentation are already transcribed; the one likely figure change is the connect-initiation SABM-vs-SABME branch (V2). And this arc adds verbs/events, so **SP-010 (typed verbs, [#260](https://github.com/packet-net/packet.net/issues/260)) is worth doing first or alongside** — it turns the spelling-drift risk of a big dispatcher change into compile errors.

- **V1 — Extended (mod-128) frame codec** ✅ C# done 2026-06-02 (#239; ax25-ts parity leg pending). Model the 2-byte extended control field in `Ax25Frame` — I-frame (7-bit N(S)/N(R) + P/F) and S-frame (7-bit N(R) + SS + P/F); U-frames stay 1-byte in both modes. Encode + parse. **Key architectural change:** control-field width isn't derivable from the bytes alone, so parse/classify must become **mode-aware** — the receive path tells the decoder the link's negotiated modulo (a parse-option / session-level decode, replacing the stateless-on-control-byte classifier). Implement the extended N(S)/N(R) read/write (the `RequireMod8` sites). *Exit:* a mod-128 I/S frame round-trips encode→parse→classify with correct 7-bit sequence numbers, both runtimes. *C# done:* `ControlExtension` octet + mode-aware `PollFinal`/`Nr`/`Ns`; `extended` flag on the I/S factories + `TryParse`; receive-path threading (`Ax25Adapter`, `Ax25Listener` two-pass demux); `RequireMod8` deleted; `Mod128_extended_window_transfer_converges` un-skipped + green. See §17.

- **V2 — SABME establishment + version negotiation** ✅ C# done 2026-06-02 (ax25-ts parity leg pending). Connect-initiation already emits SABME vs SABM correctly (the figc4.7 `Establish_Data_Link` subroutine branches on `mod_128`) — but figc4.2 routes the `Disconnected` DL-CONNECT path **unconditionally to `AwaitingConnection`** with no version branch (confirmed against the authoritative `DataLink_Disconnected.graphml`: version routing lives only on the *responder* SABM/SABME-received side). So a v2.2 connect sent the right first frame but parked in the mod-8 state, whose T1 retry **downgrades to SABM** (figc4.2 `t05` → `SABMPEqEq1`) and which has **no FRMR handler** (so the §975 v2.0 fallback couldn't fire). *C# done:* kept the SDL faithful and corrected it in the runtime with a new default-on quirk `Ax25Spec44Mod128ConnectRoutesToV22` (off under `StrictlyFaithful`) that rewrites just that one transition's **target state** to `AwaitingV22Connection` (figc4.6) when `IsExtended` at dispatch time — a new quirk shape (the existing #38/#40/#42/#43 rewrite *guards*; this rewrites the `next` state, in `Ax25Session.ResolveNextState`). figc4.6 then drives the handshake correctly: retry resends SABME, FRMR → set version 2.0 + re-establish (→ `AwaitingConnection`, mod-8), DM(F=1) → teardown, UA → Connected-extended. `TwoStationHarness` gained a `quirks`/`BuildStrictlyFaithful` knob and now drives a SABME connect end-to-end; `AwaitingV22Connection` behavioural coverage **0/25 → 15/25**, battery total 50 → 64 (floor 45 → 60). New `Mod128EstablishmentConformanceTests` (route-through-V22, retry-resends-SABME-not-SABM, FRMR→v2.0 fallback, DM teardown, StrictlyFaithful-reproduces-the-defect). A *second* figc4.6 defect surfaced and was **fixed in a follow-up**: `t14_frmr_received` lists `Establish Data Link` *before* `Set Version 2.0`, so the §975 fallback re-established with a **SABME** (still mod-128 at that point) rather than a SABM — direwolf's author independently reordered it (`set_version_2_0` "not in flow chart" before `establish_data_link`). Fixed with a second default-on quirk `Ax25Spec45FrmrFallbackReestablishesV20` that forces version 2.0 *before* the t14 actions run (in `Ax25Session.ApplyPreExecutionQuirks`), so the re-establish is a SABM; the FRMR conformance test now asserts the *complete* v2.0/mod-8 fallback. **Both figure defects filed upstream:** the routing defect as [packethacking/ax25spec#44](https://github.com/packethacking/ax25spec/issues/44), the t14 ordering defect as [#45](https://github.com/packethacking/ax25spec/issues/45). *Exit (met for C#):* two extended-capable stations connect mod-128; an extended station falls back to v2.0/mod-8 on FRMR with a SABM.

- **V3 — XID parameter negotiation (the MDL)** ✅ C# done 2026-06-02 (part 1 codec + part 2 MDL runtime; ax25-ts parity leg pending). Two parts: **(1) the XID info-field codec** ✅ (§4.3.3.7 / Fig 4.5 TLVs — the spec numbers this §4.3.3.7, not §4.3.4.3 as the arc text first guessed: FI 0x82 + GI 0x80 + GL + ordered PI/PL/PV for Classes of Procedures PI=2, HDLC Optional Functions PI=3 incl. modulo + REJ/SREJ + segmenter, I-Field Length Rx PI=6 (bits), Window k Rx PI=8, Ack Timer T1 PI=9, Retries N2 PI=10). Done as `Packet.Ax25.Xid.XidInfoField` (encode/parse) + typed `ClassesOfProcedures` / `HdlcOptionalFunctions` / `XidParameters`, strict-by-default with `XidParseOptions` leniency knobs. See §17. **(2) the MDL/XID negotiation FSM** ✅ C# done 2026-06-02 — route (b) taken. figc5.1/figc5.2 (Ready/Negotiating) were hand-authored upstream from the §6.3.2 + App-C5.3 *prose* (a deliberate Tom-directed exception to ax25sdl's graphml→YAML flow; figc5.x never redrawn, PNGs all-black) and ship in `Packet.Ax25.Sdl` 0.9.0 as a **prose-derived bootstrap marked `verification_pending`** (figc5.3–5.8 per-parameter subroutines collapsed to a single `Apply Negotiated Parameters` placeholder). *C# done:* new `Ax25ManagementDataLink` driver runs the generated `ManagementDataLink_Ready`/`_Negotiating` tables through the existing `Ax25Session`/`ActionDispatcher`/`GuardEvaluator` machinery (injectable MDL hooks added to the dispatcher; new verbs `XIDCommand`/`Start·StopTM201`/`ApplyNegotiatedParameters`/`MDLNEGOTIATEConfirm`/`MDLERRORIndicateB·C·D` got arms; `set_version_2_0` routed through a hook); `MdlNegotiateRequestSignal` (raised after the UA on a v2.2 connect) wired to `mdl.Negotiate()` in `Ax25Listener`; inbound XID-command → responder, XID-response/FRMR while negotiating → initiator (C/R-bit disambiguated). The §6.3.2 reverts-to merge (`XidNegotiator.ApplyNegotiated`: HDLC-optional-functions → lesser of REJ/SREJ + modulo; duplex → lesser; k/N1 → notification/min; T1/N2 → greater; absent → retain) **replaces the forced `SrejEnabled`/`IsExtended`/`K`/`N1`/T1V/N2 config with negotiated values** + sets a new `SegmenterReassemblerEnabled` flag (V4 will gate on it); the FRMR v2.0 fallback (`XidNegotiator.ApplyVersion20Defaults`) installs the **full §1436 set** (half-duplex, REJ, mod-8, N1=256, k=7, T1=3000ms, N2=10), not merely `IsExtended=false`; TM201/NM201 retry loop as the SDL drives it. New `XidNegotiatorTests` + `MdlXidNegotiationConformanceTests` (two-station XID exchange convergence, v2.0 FRMR fallback, TM201 exhaustion → MDL-ERROR C, error D/B paths). See §17. **Caveats (figc5.x backfill owes these):** the *responder* XID-command-reception path and error A (XID command without P=1) are un-transcribed figure detail, so the responder is a runtime helper (`RespondToXidCommand`, marked) deriving the reply from the same §6.3.2 rules; the whole machine is `verification_pending` pending the figc5.x graphml redraw. *Exit (part 2, met for C#):* SREJ + segmentation + modulo + window are enabled by an actual XID exchange, not a test flag. ax25-ts parity leg pending.

- **V4 — SREJ(mod-128) + segmentation integration** ✅ C# done 2026-06-03 (ax25-ts parity leg pending). Two parts. **(V4a) SREJ + REJ recovery in the mod-128 sequence space** — *finding:* **no recovery-path code change was needed.** The recovery logic in `Ax25SessionBindings` was already mod-aware — every sequence computation is `% context.Modulus` (the send-window check, the `Ax25Spec40` out-of-window discard guard, the N(S)/N(R) comparisons), and V1 (#239) made the frame's `Nr`/`Ns` read 7-bit values on extended frames — so SREJ/REJ recovery works at modulo-128 unchanged. Proven + regression-guarded with new `Mod128LossRecoveryConformanceTests` (single-frame REJ + SREJ recovery, multi-frame SREJ burst, and a window-wrap case that transfers across the 127→0 boundary) and by extending the generative `LossRecoveryProperties` with an `extended` parameter so the FsCheck loss-recovery sweep fuzzes *both* modulos (the three SREJ quirks `Ax25Spec40/41/42` hold at mod-128 too). **(V4b) segmentation integration** — a §2.4/§6.6 segmentation-reassembly shim (`SegmentationLayer`) at the DL-primitive boundary; the data-link SDL + session stay **unchanged** (segments are ordinary I-frames with PID `PidSegmented` 0x08). Send side: `SegmentationLayer.BuildSendRequests` segments an over-N1 payload into PID-0x08 `DlDataRequest`s (gated on the negotiated `SegmenterReassemblerEnabled` flag V3 set); an over-N1 payload with the segmenter *not* negotiated is rejected cleanly (throws — never truncated or oversized). Receive side: a per-session `Reassembler` consumes 0x08-PID DL-DATA indications and surfaces one reassembled indication on the last segment, passing everything else through unchanged. Wired into `Ax25Listener` (new `SendData(session, data, pid)` helper on send; the `SendUpward` fan-out runs every indication through the reassembler on receive — the dispatcher's `DLDATAIndication => sendUpward(BuildDataIndication(tx))` is untouched, the seam is at the boundary) and into `TwoStationHarness` (`segmenter`/`n1` knobs + `SubmitLarge`). **Inner PID on reassembly:** Figure 6.2 confirms the segment header carries the 0x08 PID + the F/X octet only — **no field carries the original L3 PID** through a segmented series, so the *figure-literal* reassembly delivers `PidNoLayer3` (documented in `SegmentationLayer`). *(Superseded by the default-on `SegmentFirstCarriesL3Pid` quirk — §17 2026-06-04 — which carries the L3 PID as a Dire-Wolf-format inner octet on the first segment and recovers it; the figure-literal `PidNoLayer3` behaviour is now the `StrictlyFaithful` path.)* *Exit (met for C#):* `SegmentationIntegrationConformanceTests.Over_N1_payload_segments_reassembles_and_SREJ_recovers_a_lost_segment_mod128` — a >N1 payload segments on send, reassembles intact on receive over a mod-128 link, and SREJ selectively recovers a dropped segment (asserts an SREJ on the wire, not merely a T1-timeout go-back-N). New `SegmentationLayerTests` + `Ax25ListenerSegmentationTests` cover the shim + the listener seam. See §17. **Note (pre-existing quirk, not introduced here):** the standalone `Segmenter`/`Reassembler` model a 6-bit remaining-count (max 64 segments), whereas Figure 6.2's F/X octet is a 7-bit count (max 128); this is the shipped utility's existing design (plan §5.2) and the shim uses it as-is — flagged for a future revisit but out of scope for V4 (changing it alters the utility's wire format). ax25-ts parity leg pending.

- **V5 — Conformance + interop + parity** (medium effort; packet.net + ax25-ts). **V5a (conformance/ledger) ✅ C# done 2026-06-03** (ax25-ts parity leg pending): behavioural coverage of `AwaitingV22Connection` (**15 → 20/25** — residual 5 are never-produced error inputs t08/t09 + the not-layer-3-initiated branches the responder never reaches), mod-128 bidirectional data transfer incl. the 127→0 window-wrap, mod-128 REJ+SREJ loss recovery, the TimerRecovery receive columns by injection (Connected **21 → 38/66**, TimerRecovery **6 → 40/90**), the MDL XID-negotiation machine (added to the ledger via a one-line `Ax25ManagementDataLink.TransitionFired` forwarding event — Ready 2/2 + Negotiating 5/5), and segmentation over a mod-128 link — all via the harness in extended mode. Battery total **64/243 → 127/250**, floor **60 → 122**; no real bug surfaced. See §17. *(V4 delivered the in-process harness coverage of mod-128 REJ+SREJ loss recovery — incl. window-wrap — and wired segmentation round-trip + loss recovery; V5a folded those into the ledger and extended them.)* **V5b (interop matrix) 🟡 partial (C# core; 2026-06-03)** — the separate infra effort: **every v2.2 feature on the wire, not just connect**. **Headline finding that reshapes this leg: LinBPQ is NOT a mod-128 stack.** The pinned `m0lte/linbpq` (6.0.25.23) rejects an inbound SABME with FRMR (`L2Code.c` ~687: *"Reject until we support Mod 128"*), only ever sends SABM outbound, and clamps XID to "Mod 8 and 256 Byte frames" — verified in BPQ's source **and** empirically on the net-sim KISS path (SABME → control `0x97` = `FRMR|PF`, info `7F 00 01`). No config enables mod-128 (`MAXFRAME=1..127` caps only the mod-8 window; the framing stays mod-8). So the earlier note that BPQ was a "source-confirmed mod-128 stack" was **wrong** — corrected here. **Consequence:** the SABME-connect / mod-128-windowed-transfer / mod-128-loss-recovery / mod-128-segmentation rows are **unreachable against LinBPQ** (🚫 in §7.1) and need a genuinely mod-128 peer = **direwolf (#177)**; **rax25** stays a v2.0-only fallback alternative (`sabme()` TODO). **What V5b landed (C#):** (1) the **version-negotiation fallback** row vs LinBPQ — `LinbpqViaNetsimExtendedMode` drives our extended-preferred connect (SABME) against the real BPQ, asserts BPQ's FRMR on the wire, and verifies our figc4.6 FRMR-fallback re-establishes mod-8 and the link then carries data (banner + `P\r`); LinBPQ is the *ideal* fallback peer because it actively FRMRs the SABME. (2) **the genuine mod-128 interop rows vs direwolf** — `DirewolfMod128Interop` (the #177 deliverable): SABME→UA at mod-128 ✅, bidirectional mod-128 transfer with 7-bit sequencing ✅, SREJ-128 loss recovery ✅, XID-up-to-128 ✅, and mod-128 **segmentation** ✅ (round-trip both directions behind the default-on de-facto-interop quirk `SegmentFirstCarriesL3Pid` — the repo now matches Dire Wolf's first-segment inner-PID format, the only known v2.2 segmenter; off under `StrictlyFaithful`, which keeps the figure-literal Figure-6.2 form. The inner-PID octet also fixes the `PidNoLayer3`-on-reassembly limitation. See §7.1 + §17 2026-06-04). **Headline of #177: Dire Wolf, as responder, answers SABME at mod-128 unconditionally** (no config knob — the opposite of BPQ). **Topology surprise:** Dire Wolf's connected-mode engine is AGW-only on a real radio channel and net-sim has no external audio ingress, so Dire Wolf is *not* on net-sim — the `docker/direwolf` container runs the two-direwolf audio-loopback path (shared PulseAudio null sink = the AFSK1200 RF). XID-vs-BPQ is mod-8-only (BPQ's clamp). The remaining V5b work is the **ax25-ts mod-128 parity** leg (its own interop via `interop.yml`); cut a coordinated release once that lands. **V5c ✅ done 2026-06-04** — the ax25-ts parity legs landed (the segment quirk + the SP-010 guard/event half, both runtimes at full parity) and the arc shipped as a coordinated release: `ax25sdl` 0.10.0 → packet.net libs `lib-v0.5.0` + `@packet-net/ax25` 0.6.0 → cascade (packet-term-web deploy, axcall + packet-term-tui `v0.2.7`); the packet.net interop matrix is green on the released commit (`32760e0`). See §17 2026-06-04 (V5c).

**Sequencing.** V1 is the prerequisite for data-carrying mod-128 (V4) and most of V5; V2 can run in parallel with V1 (U-frame handshake only); V3 is largely independent and feeds V4's "negotiated enable." So: **V1 ∥ V2 → V3 → V4 → V5**, with SP-010 ideally landing before/at the start to de-risk the dispatcher churn. The single biggest risk is V1's mode-aware decode (the codec/parse boundary currently assumes 1-byte control everywhere — KISS in, AXUDP, the classifier, both runtimes).

---

### 5.G GB7RDG (LinBPQ) replacement parity ⬜

The flagship cutover target: replace the live LinBPQ node **GB7RDG** (OARC, M0LTE) with PDN. A full subsystem-by-subsystem audit of PDN vs the 2026-06-11 GB7RDG snapshot is in `gb7rdg-config/GAP-ANALYSIS.md` (forwarding-specific detail in `gb7rdg-config/ANALYSIS.md`). **Headline:** the hard protocol work is done — NET/ROM (L3+L4+INP3+connect-by-alias), AX.25 mod-8/128, multi-port KISS-TCP+ACKMODE, and the BBS engine (B1F/B2F/LZHUF/BID/routing) are all present and live-wired; the gaps are *breadth*, and most need individual design before building.

Disposition legend: **DROP** (decided no), **PARK** (deferred / secondary — revisit later), **CONSIDER** (roadmap candidate, needs its own design pass). Dispositions are Tom's calls (2026-06-16) — see §17. **Nothing here is approved to build yet**; each CONSIDER item gets designed/scoped on its own before work starts.

| Item | Disposition | Owner | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Per-peer AX.25 capability cache** (adaptive interlink/CONNECT dialing) | ✅ SHIPPED (node-v0.15.0) | packet.net | Remembers per (port,peer) whether a neighbour does v2.2/SABME + answers a pre-session XID; skips probes a known non-answerer would stall on, re-probes negatives after ~30d. `src/Packet.Node.Core/Capabilities/`; console `CAP`/`CAP CLEAR` + `/api/v1/capabilities` + SPA panel. See §17 2026-06-17. |
| **BBS content filtering** (RejFrom / RejTo / RefuseBulls / FBB filters) | CONSIDER | pdn-bbs | Biggest BBS gap — GB7RDG dropped 219 filter + 131 BID rejects in 9 days; PDN does only the BID half. Highest daily impact. |
| **Prometheus metrics / exporter** | ✅ SHIPPED | packet.net | `GET /metrics` in Prometheus text-exposition format, derived from the SAME live state the `/api/v1/*` read surface projects (no second source of truth). `pdn_*` namespace; node-health + per-port (link byte/REJ/SREJ stats aggregated to the port) + NET/ROM forwarding + **per-port radio health (`pdn_radio_*`, attached radios only, from the `/api/v1/radios` projection)** buckets. Cardinality is port-bounded **except** the one deliberate `pdn_link_snr_db{port,peer}` per-partner SNR gauge (Tom's call: amateur-packet station counts stay naturally small — see §17 2026-07-04). Same listener + `read`-scope gate as the REST API (unauthenticated when auth off, the localhost-scrape posture). Hand-rolled formatter, no new dependency. `src/Packet.Node/Api/PdnMetricsApi.cs`; docs/observability.md. See §17 2026-06-17 + 2026-07-04. |
| **Chat federation: on-air validation** | CONSIDER | pdn-bpqchat | Wire protocol is done + docker-validated; needs a live RF link to GB7RDG's neighbours (GB7NDH-3 / GB7WOD-1 / GB7WOK-1), a tagged bpqchat release, and the inbound peer allow-list enforced (design.md §4.1). |
| **M0LTEMap reporting** (`EnableM0LTEMap` + per-port `M0LTEMapInfo` + events) | CONSIDER | packet.net | Puts the node on the UK packet map — operator-facing, Tom runs it. |
| **OARC API** (`ENABLEOARCAPI`) | CONSIDER | packet.net | OARC node-API integration. |
| **Per-port NET/ROM QUALITY** | ✅ SHIPPED | packet.net | `PortConfig.netRomQuality` (0..255) overrides the node-wide `defaultNeighbourQuality` for routes heard on that port → an accurate per-port advert on a mixed-grade node. Hot-reloadable (read-only NET/ROM awareness, no restart). Console/REST/YAML + the Ports editor. See §17 2026-06-17. |
| **Per-port PACLEN / N1 knob** | ✅ SHIPPED | packet.net | `Ax25PortParams.n1` (16..256) seeds the session's N1/PACLEN per port; XID can still negotiate it lower, never higher. Hot-reseed (new sessions only). YAML + the Ports editor. See §17 2026-06-17. |
| **Housekeeping lifetime defaults + MaxMsgno** | CONSIDER | pdn-bbs | Kill-by-age machinery exists but all-30-day; GB7RDG keeps bulls ~7d. Mostly defaults/tuning + renumber. |
| **L4Compress** (`L4Compress=1`) | CONSIDER | packet.net | NET/ROM L4 payload compression; efficiency on slow links. |
| **Connect-script fidelity** (`!` force-direct, `NC`, INTERLOCK, PAUSE) | CONSIDER | packet.net + pdn-bbs | Matters for GB7RDG's shared-HF-PA partners, not the direct GB7RDG↔PDN link. `NC` currently sent verbatim; INTERLOCK recognised-but-ignored. |
| **Receiver-side restart granting** (`FS !offset`) | CONSIDER | pdn-bbs | Sender honours it; receiver never persists partial inbound. RF-feed-only — doesn't bite the clean net-sim link. |
| **Inbound FBB forwarding over internet-TCP** (BPQ `FBBPORT`) | CONSIDER | pdn-bbs | Protocol complete but inbound rides AX.25/RHP only; no raw-TCP forwarding port for an internet partner. |
| **MHeard `MH` console verb + persisted heard log** | ✅ SHIPPED | packet.net | `MH` (node-wide) / `MH <port>` (per-port) console verb listing recently heard stations (callsign, last-heard, count, port[s]), sourced from the same telemetry tap that feeds `/api/v1/links`. Persisted heard log (`src/Packet.Node.Core/Heard/`: `HeardLog` over a resilient `SqliteHeardStore` in pdn.db) survives port teardown AND node restart; retention = 30-day age-out + 500/port cap (timer-free, re-applied every 2048 hearings + on startup). Per-port rows + a node-wide merge (per-callsign across ports). Also `GET /api/v1/heard[?port=]`. See §17 2026-06-17. |
| **White Pages (WP consume)** | CONSIDER | pdn-bbs | Already tracked as forwarding.md F-3; GB7RDG receives `WP@<bbs>` each cycle (PDN stores as ordinary mail). |
| **APRS** (encoder + position/object beaconing + digipeater + APRS-IS IGate) | PARK | packet.net | "Very much a secondary thing." Largest single chunk; `Packet.Aprs` is decoder-only today. Revisit after the cutover essentials. |
| **Application packet identity** (node-prompt command verb + node-owned callsign + opt-in NET/ROM alias) | ✅ **SHIPPED (slices 1–4)** | packet.net + pdn-bbs/bpqchat/convers/dapps | Spec + all four slices implemented and released — app-packages.md § Application packet identity. Three dimensions à la BPQ `APPLICATION` — `command` in the manifest (the app name, optional); `callsign` + `netrom.alias` node-owned in `packetnet.yaml apps[]`/`applications[]`; node resolves + injects `PDN_APP_CALLSIGN` (`AppCallsignResolver`). Bare command verb at the node prompt (service app ⇒ loopback connect, session app ⇒ attach); opt-in NODES alias advert (`AppNetromAdvert` → `NetRomService.AppAdvertSource`); `session.match` clean-replaced by `packet.command`. **NET/ROM alias opt-in, off by default.** Slice 4 — all four apps released: pdn-bbs v0.2.33, pdn-bpqchat v0.1.1, pdn-convers v0.1.3, DAPPS v0.34.2 — each binds `PDN_APP_CALLSIGN` + declares `packet.command`. Node in node-v0.13.23; lab-staged + slices 1/2/4 wire-verified (slice-3 advert staged, gated on `netRom.broadcast`). |
| **AX.25 digipeating** (`DIGIFLAG`) | DROP | — | "An antipattern for nodes anyhow." |
| **Multi-user telnet login** | DROP | — | "More of an admin interface than anything nowadays" — the telnet/SSH console stays a sysop interface, not a public multi-user login. |
| **BPQmon / MQTT node telemetry** | DROP | — | "We're not a BPQ port." Superseded by the Prometheus item above. |
| **AGW emulator** (`AGWPORT`) | DROP | — | Apps use RHPv2, not AGW (a loopback AGW server remains Phase 6, but not for app-hosting). |
| **IP gateway** (`IPGATEWAY`, IP-over-AX.25) | DROP | — | Non-goal (and GB7RDG's own AXIP port is commented out). |
| **SNMP** (`ENABLESNMP`) | DROP | — | Non-goal — PDN monitoring is REST + SSE (+ the Prometheus item). |
| **CMS / Winlink** | DROP | — | Explicitly deferred in pdn-bbs docs; separate product decision. |

A **GitHub issue sweep** to file/triage the CONSIDER items against the org's trackers is wanted "at some point" (not yet done — noted here so it isn't lost).
