The radios' Transparent mode (proven 2026-07-04 to be an 8-bit-clean FFSK byte pipe, ~1.8 kbit/s, the radio fragmenting/reassembling ≤46-byte over-air blocks itself, `+++` escape reliable) is now a first-class transport. **`Packet.Radio.Tait.TaitTransparentTransport : ITxCompletionTransport`** wraps a `TaitCcdiRadio`: it enters Transparent mode (re-clocking the port to the transparent baud if it differs from the command baud — same on this rig, so no switch, but the driver handles the general case via a new `ISerialIo.SetBaudRate` seam + internal `TaitCcdiRadio.SetSerialBaudRate`), frames AX.25 with **KISS SLIP framing** (reusing `KissEncoder`/`KissDecoder` — FEND-delimited so the receiver finds boundaries; the radio's own blocking means no host chunking), and de-frames the inbound `TransparentDataReceived` byte stream back into whole `Ax25InboundFrame`s. `DisposeAsync` escapes Transparent (`+++`) and restores the command baud — with a hard XML-doc WARNING that a radio programmed "Ignore Escape Sequence" ON cannot be escaped (recovery = power cycle, the lockout hit on the bench). **Timing (Tom's ask — start/end of each transmission for the AX.25 engine):** the transport OWNS the TX, so it times it directly — a `TxTiming` event + `ITxCompletionTransport.SendAwaitingCompletionAsync` give per-frame queued / on-air-start (submit + configurable FFSK lead-in) / on-air-end (start + airtime) / airtime (SLIP-framed bytes × 8 ÷ configurable FFSK baud, default 2400); inbound frames carry a precise `ReceivedAt` + `RadioMetadata.EstimatedAirtime` (engine derives on-air start = `ReceivedAt − airtime`). **RSSI trade-off (documented, inherent):** RSSI/SNR/noise-floor/carrier-rise/DCD are NULL — no CCDI control channel exists while the port is a byte pipe. That is the cost of a TNC-less link (one device, no audio wiring) vs NinoTNC+CCDI (`RssiTaggingTransport`: full telemetry, two devices). **PDN port kind `tait-transparent`** (`TaitTransparentTransportConfig`): bind by CCDI serial (scanned at bring-up, reusing `RadioSerialResolver`) or device path, with `baud`/`transparentBaud`/`ffskBaud`/`leadInMs`; wired through the YAML + JSON converters, the validator (exactly-one-of device/serial; a `radio:` block is rejected — the radio IS the modem), `TransportFactory`, and the config template + `docs/node-api.yaml`. Teardown exits Transparent (a port left in it is deaf to CCDI). **Hardware-validated on the rig (both TM8110, CCDI 03.02, USB0 s/n 19925328 / USB1 s/n 19925369):** re-scanned by serial, opened both as `TaitTransparentTransport`, **8/8 AX.25 UI frames intact both directions** through the FFSK pipe, SLIP boundaries found, timing metadata populated and sane (airtime scaled with frame size, e.g. 47 B → 156.7 ms at 2400 Bd; on-air start derived to within the decode/serial latency; RSSI null), clean Transparent exit both radios (post-exit CCDI MODEL query answered → Command mode confirmed). Tests: +11 `Packet.Radio.Tait.Tests` (119 total — SLIP round-trip, boundary reassembly across chunk splits, TX/RX timing, exit-on-dispose over the scripted `ISerialIo`) + node YAML/JSON round-trip + validator cases. **Deferred:** the Transparent-config doctor probes (separate queued task). **Parity note:** no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change (the transport reuses existing `RadioMetadata`/`TxCompletion` fields; TX on-air start/end surface via the transport-local `TxTiming` event, off the parity-tracked AX.25 surface) → **no ax25-ts leg**.

### 2026-07-04 — Node + web: interactive guided deviation tuning comes to PDN (SDM-coordinated flavour) — start a session, watch the numbers, turn the pot

Task #10 phase 2 (final) — the tuning surface itself, building on phase 1's doctor. Deviation tuning is a two-ended, operator-initiated, **transmitting** procedure: this node's radio-attached port is one end (`tuned` — the operator turns the TX-DEV pot here; the port keys frame bursts on request) and a peer radio is the other (`meter` — measures decode rate + RX-audio level and returns edge-bracketing advice). Coordination rides the radios' SDM side channel (mode/channel-agnostic, hardware-proven). Because it keys the radio and disrupts the channel, a session is admin-scoped, audited, and pauses the port's normal traffic — restored on every exit path. The `packet-tune deviation-sdm` CLI stays the lightweight standalone; this adds the node/web surface. Tom's product call: tuning belongs in PDN.

**(1) Node session state machine (`PortTuningSession`, in `Packet.Node.Core.Tuning`).** Rather than fork the console-oriented `TuningSession.Run{Tuned,Meter}Async` loop, the node runs it **unmodified** over an `ObservingTuningLink` decorator that taps the wire and decodes the same `HI`/`MS`/`AD` telegrams into a structured `TuningEvent` feed (armed → peer-connected → per-round {burstIndex, decoded/total, levelDb?, rssiDbm?, advice up/down/ok/sweep, note} → ended/error). The tuned role's between-rounds `ITuningPrompt` is wired to the operator's "next" signal instead of a console read (a per-wait `TaskCompletionSource`). Events fan out to SSE subscribers with **full-history replay** so a late/reconnecting client sees the whole trend. **Restore discipline:** the un-pause callback runs exactly once from the loop's `finally` (or, if it never started, from `DisposeAsync`) — clean end, link error, exception, operator stop, and node shutdown all funnel through it, so a port can never be left paused or a radio keyed.

**(2) Pause = quiesce; restore = rebuild (`PortTuningService` + `PortTuningRegistry`).** Pause is `Ax25Listener.StopAsync()` (the listener stops transmitting/accepting; the modem serial port stays open so the session can key bursts + meter). Restore is `PortSupervisor.RestartPortAsync` under the host's exclusive gate — a full teardown + fresh bring-up, the definitive guarantee nothing is left wedged. (`Ax25Listener.StopAsync` cancels a lifecycle CTS that `StartAsync` never resets, so it is a one-way stop, not a reversible pause — hence restore is a rebuild, not a resume.) One session per port (a second start → 409); the port stays claimed until its restore completes and it auto-removes, so a new session can't race an old one's rebuild. Fail-fast validation reuses the doctor's SDM-enabled probe (a wildcard SDM the radio 0/06-rejects when SDM is disabled) plus NinoTNC-present / Tait-radio-present / 8-char-peer checks.

**(3) Endpoints (`PdnPortTuningApi`).** `POST /api/v1/ports/{id}/tuning/session {role,peerSdmId,burstFrames?}` → `{sessionId,…}` (admin+audit; 404/400/409). `GET …/tuning/events` → SSE (read-scoped; named `tuning` events, camelCase `TuningEvent` JSON; mirrors `PdnEventsApi`). `POST …/tuning/next` → the tuned operator's "I've adjusted the pot" (admin+audit; 409 when no round awaits / meter role). `POST …/tuning/stop` and `DELETE …/tuning/session` → stop + restore (admin+audit). DI singleton (disposed at shutdown → stops+restores every session). `docs/node-api.yaml` gains the four paths + `TuningStartRequest`/`TuningSessionInfo`/`TuningEvent` schemas.

**(4) Web — `/tools/tuner` wired to the real API** (`web/packetnet-ui`). The existing `link-tuner.tsx` (a client-side simulation of a different KISS-TXDELAY flow) is replaced by the deviation workspace: pick port + role + peer SDM id → **Start** (`api.startTune`, `subscribeTune` SSE), a live **trend table** (round / decoded / level / advice + operator note) fed by the feed, a prominent **"Next round — I've adjusted the pot"** button (enabled only while awaiting-adjustment on the tuned role), a clear **"port is paused for tuning / transmitting" banner**, and **Stop**. A 409 re-attaches to the running session (reload-safe, via the SSE replay). `types.ts`/`api.ts`/`mock.ts` gain the tuning types + a mock backend (a converging scripted session gated by the mock "next") so it renders with no node.

**(5) Tests.** Node (`Packet.Node.Tests.Tuning` + `Integration`): `PortTuningSessionTests` (8) — full tuned lifecycle over an in-memory link pair against the real meter loop (event order + round-shape + level/advice), sweep advice, meter-role lifecycle, error exit, **restore-exactly-once on every path** (clean/stop/error/dispose-without-start/double-stop), next-gate rejection, late-subscriber history replay; `PortTuningRegistryTests` (4) — one-per-port claim, instance-scoped remove, stop-all-restores-all; `TuningPreflightTests` (role/state wire + CanArm messages); `PortTuningApiTests` (6) — 404 unknown/not-running, 400 bad role, SSE/next/stop 404. Web: a `screens.smoke` case drives a mock session (Start → paused banner → first round → Next → next round). Live hardware validation (a real SDM session on the bench rig) is a documented human follow-up — the sandbox can't open radios. `dotnet build` 0 errors (warnings-as-errors + XML docs); the tuning suites + web gate (`npm ci && npm run build && npm run test`, 101 vitest) green. **No ax25-ts leg** — a node surface, nothing on the parity-tracked AX.25 contract.

**Deliberately deferred (noted, not built):** hosting the PIN `RendezvousRelay` in the node + internet-peer tuning (the SDM-coordinated flavour is enough for v1 and needs no relay); the mode-coordination UI. The CLI (`deviation-sdm`/`deviation-remote`/`mode-coord`) is unchanged.

### 2026-07-04 — Node + web: the capability doctor comes to PDN — "Check radio setup" per port (safe by default; interrupt=true transmits)

Task #10 phase 1 (tuning + doctor in PDN; **doctor only** — the tuning UI is a later phase). The capability doctor existed only as the standalone `packet-tune doctor` CLI (`Packet.Tune.Core.TuningDoctor`, which OPENS its own serial ports). Tom's product call: the doctor belongs in PDN, run against the port's **already-open** handles so an operator clicks "Check radio setup" and gets a plain-language pass/fail/unknown checklist + remedies without tearing the port down. The standalone CLI stays as the lightweight tool.

**(1) One probe implementation, two entry points (`TuningDoctor`).** Extracted the probe bodies into `RunProbesAsync(NinoTncSerialPort?, TaitCcdiRadio?, TuningDoctorOptions, onProbe, ct)` operating on injected open handles; the CLI's `RunAsync(tncPort, ccdiPort, …)` now opens the ports and delegates to the same core (its stdout/JSON behaviour is byte-identical — the port-open failure path stays CLI-side). A new `TuningDoctorOptions.IncludeTransmittingProbes` (default **true**, so the CLI is unchanged) gates the three **transmitting** probes — TXDELAY software-control, the wildcard-SDM check, and TNC↔radio pairing — which key the transmitter / perturb TXDELAY. A `null` TNC handle (a serial-KISS modem) reports the TNC-diagnostic probes as `unknown` "not a NinoTNC"; a `null` radio skips the radio probes.

**(2) `RunningPort.NinoTnc` handle.** `PortSupervisor` now captures the raw `NinoTncSerialPort` on the `RunningPort` (before the pacing/RSSI-tagging decorators hide it — `ModemTransport` is a `PacingKissModem` once ACKMODE is on, so a downcast wouldn't do). Not owned there (the modem chain disposes it).

**(3) Endpoint — `GET`/`POST /api/v1/ports/{id}/doctor` (`PdnPortDoctorApi`, projection `PortDoctorRunner` in Node.Core).** `Packet.Node.Core` now references `Packet.Tune.Core` (Tom-approved; only the doctor probes are used). **Safe by default:** `GET` is **read**-scoped and runs only the non-transmitting probes — the transmitting ones report `unknown` "requires a brief transmit — rerun with interrupt=true". The transmitting form is `POST …?interrupt=true`, **admin**-scoped + **audited** (`port_doctor`, mirroring ping/lifecycle), with a documented warning that it briefly transmits and perturbs TXDELAY. Runs are node-wide single-flight. `DoctorReport { portId, probes:[{ name, status:"pass"|"fail"|"unknown", detail, remedy }], ranAt }` (camelCase). 404 for an unknown/not-running port; a no-radio port gets a `radio-attached` "no radio attached" row. `docs/node-api.yaml` gains the path (get=read / post=admin) + `PortDoctorReport`/`PortDoctorProbe` schemas.

**(4) Web — "Check radio" per port** (`web/packetnet-ui`): a `DoctorButton` on the Ports per-port action row opens a modal that auto-runs the safe check (`api.runDoctor(id,false)` → GET) and renders the checklist (pass=green / fail=red+remedy / unknown=grey), with a secondary **"Run full check (briefly transmits)"** action (`runDoctor(id,true)` → POST ?interrupt=true) carrying the transmit warning. `DoctorReport`/`DoctorProbe` types + a mock `doctorReport(portId, interrupt)` builder (vhf-1 healthy NinoTNC+radio with the gated-unknown transmitting probes; uhf-2 a DIP `fail`+remedy and no-radio; others the not-a-NinoTNC degradation) so it renders with no node.

**(5) Tests.** Tune.Core: `TuningDoctorTests` (5) — the probe-core degradation + null-safe gating + onProbe sink. Node: `PortDoctorRunnerTests` (6) — the DoctorReport projection, the safe-vs-interrupt gate via an injected probe seam, and the serial-KISS/no-radio/non-Tait degradation; `PortDoctorApiTests` (3) — 404 for unknown + not-running ports on both GET and the interrupt POST. Web: 2 new `screens.smoke` cases — the safe-form checklist renders (gated-unknown + full-check action) and the fail+remedy/no-radio rows render. `dotnet build` 0 errors; the affected .NET suites + the web gate (`npm ci && npm run build && npm run test`, 100 vitest) green. **No ax25-ts leg** — nothing on the parity-tracked AX.25 contract changed (a node-diagnostic surface, like the radio endpoints).

### 2026-07-04 — Web UI: the operator radio surface — attach a radio, then SEE the link quality

The web control panel (`web/packetnet-ui`) now consumes the radio-control HTTP API landed earlier today (the entry below). The UX thesis: an operator attaches a Tait radio to a port and then sees their link quality. Scope kept to **attach + link-quality + health** — the tuning/doctor UI (`link-tuner.tsx`) is left as-is for a separate task.

**(1) Typed API + client.** `src/lib/types.ts` gains `RadioConfig` (kind/serial?/port?/baud?/healthIntervalSeconds?) + `radio?` on `PortConfig`, the read models `RadioStatus`/`RadioIdentity`/`RadioHealth`/`RadioScanResult`/`HeardStation`, and the additive `rssiDbm`/`snrDb`/`noiseFloorDbm` on `MonitorEvent`. `src/lib/api.ts` gains `getRadios()`, `getPortRadio(id)` (404-aware), `scanRadios()` — mock-backed so every surface renders with no node.

**(2) PortEditor "Radio control" section** (`src/screens/ports.tsx`, serial-modem kinds only): toggle a radio on → kind selector (Tait CCDI) → a **"Scan for radios"** button (`scanRadios()`) listing each hit as **"{model} · s/n {serial}"** (subtitle byIdPath||devicePath) → selecting **binds by CCDI serial** (the stable key), with an advanced "bind by device path instead" fallback; baud defaults to 28800; inline exactly-one-of-serial/port validation mirrors the backend. **Bug fixed:** `saveDraft` reconstructed `PortConfig` field-by-field and OMITTED `radio`, silently dropping the block on any Forms edit of a radio-attached port — the reconstruction is now the exported `portDraftToConfig()` (radio included, dropped only when the transport is switched to a non-serial kind).

**(3) Link quality in the monitor** (`src/screens/monitor.tsx`): a new RSSI column (dBm + SNR when present) on the frame table, and RSSI/SNR/noise-floor rows in the FrameDecode panel. Null → em-dash, never a bare 0.

**(4) Radios health panel** (`src/screens/dashboard.tsx`): the payoff view — one glanceable card per radio-attached port with identity (model/CCDI/serial), a connection-state badge (healthy=green/faulted=red/unknown=grey), a live-ish channel-busy pill, a colour-coded RSSI hero (+ averaged, PA temp), and the forward/reverse detector figures under an explicit **"Antenna-health trend (not VSWR)"** label per the driver's uncalibrated-detector caveat.

**(5) Mock + tests.** `src/lib/mock.ts` seeds two attached TM8110s with health (`RADIOS`), a scan set (`RADIO_SCAN`, one shared-serial dongle with null byIdPath), radio blocks on the vhf-1/hf-300 config ports, per-frame RSSI on inbound vhf-1 frames, and heard-station RSSI (`HEARD_STATIONS`). Gate `npm ci && npm run build && npm run test` green — **95 vitest tests pass**, including 4 new `config.roundtrip` cases proving the radio block survives the `portDraftToConfig` reconstruction + wire round-trip (serial-bound, path-bound, dropped on non-serial transport, null when absent). No ax25-ts leg — web-UI-only, nothing on the parity-tracked AX.25 contract changed.

### 2026-07-04 — Node radio-control surfaced through the HTTP API: per-frame RSSI/SNR telemetry + radio status/health/scan endpoints + serial-binding config

The radio-control arc (`PortRadioConfig` → `RadioControlFactory` → `RssiTaggingTransport`) was wired at the transport but consumed nowhere above it — `Ax25InboundFrame.Radio` was populated then discarded, and `TaitRadioHealthMonitor` / `TaitRadioPortDiscovery` were never referenced by the node. This PR surfaces all of it through `Packet.Node`'s `/api/v1`, for the web UI (next) and operators.

**(1) Per-frame RSSI/SNR into telemetry — parity-safe.** Radio metadata is a node-telemetry concern, not an AX.25-protocol one, so it is kept entirely OFF the parity-tracked `Ax25Listener` / `Ax25FrameEventArgs` contract (verified against the interop `parity-check.mjs`, which compares only `Ax25ParseOptions`/`Ax25SessionQuirks`/`XidParseOptions` flags+presets, `Ax25ListenerOptions` members, and `Ax25Listener` public methods/events — not `Ax25FrameEventArgs`). A node-owned `InboundRadioTap` (`IAx25Transport` decorator, outermost — outer→inner: tap → `RssiTaggingTransport` → modem) captures each inbound frame's `RadioMetadata` and exposes it via `IInboundRadioSource`; `NodeTelemetry.Observe` reads it for RX frames only, race-free (the listener's single pump thread yields a frame then synchronously fires `FrameTraced` before pulling the next, so the tap's last value is still this frame's). `RssiDbm`/`SnrDb`/`NoiseFloorDbm` now ride `MonitorEvent` (additive init-only), the `TrafficRecord`/`TrafficFrame` (new nullable `rssi_dbm`/`snr_db`/`noise_floor_dbm` columns + an ADD-COLUMN migration), and `HeardEntry`/`HeardStation` (new nullable `last_rssi_dbm`, newest-frame-wins) — so `/events`, `/monitor/recent`, `/traffic`, `/heard` all carry them where present, null otherwise.

**(2) Radio status + health per port.** `TaitRadioHealthMonitor` is started per radio-attached port (interval configurable, 10 s default) behind a node seam `IRadioStatusMonitor` (`TaitRadioStatusMonitor` for Tait CCDI; `GenericRadioStatusMonitor` for any other `IRadioControl`), stored on `RunningPort`, disposed before the radio it polls. **`GET /api/v1/radios`** + **`GET /api/v1/ports/{id}/radio`** project `RadioStatus` { attached, kind, controlPort, serial, identity{model, ccdiVersion}, connectionState (healthy|faulted|unknown), channelBusy, health{ rssiDbm, averagedRssiDbm, paTemperatureC, forwardTrendMillivolts, reverseTrendMillivolts, reverseForwardRatio — a per-station TREND, never VSWR per the driver's Tait-service caveats, sampleAt } }. Degrades clean: a faulted radio → attached=false and the endpoint never 500s.

**(3) Discovery.** **`GET /api/v1/radios/scan`** → `RadioScanResult[]` via `IRadioScanner`/`TaitRadioScanner` (bounded + single-flight over `TaitRadioPortDiscovery.DiscoverAsync`), each keyed by the **CCDI serial** (the stable identity — `/dev/ttyUSB*` renumber, and the CP2102 dongles' shared USB serial makes `/dev/serial/by-id` ambiguous) plus model, ccdiVersion, baud, devicePath, and byIdPath (`SerialByIdResolver`: enumerate `/dev/serial/by-id`, readlink+canonicalize, unique-match else null; Linux-only).

**(4) Config binding by CCDI serial.** `PortRadioConfig` gains optional `serial:` (validated exactly-one-of `port`/`serial`) + `healthIntervalSeconds:`. At attach, a `serial`-bound radio is resolved to its current device by scanning (`RadioSerialResolver.Match` in `RadioControlFactory`); no match degrades cleanly (log + run without radio), the same path as a failed open. Serial-binding is documented as the robust choice in `NodeConfigTemplate`. (Documented follow-up, NOT implemented: bind NinoTNC ports by `/dev/serial/by-id` — MCP2221 dongles have unique USB serials, so by-id suffices there.)

**(5) Docs + scope.** `docs/node-api.yaml` gains the `/radios`, `/ports/{id}/radio`, `/radios/scan`, `/heard` paths + `RadioStatus`/`RadioIdentity`/`RadioHealth`/`RadioScanResult`/`HeardStation` schemas + the signal fields on `MonitorEvent`/`TrafficFrame`. Did NOT build the web UI, nor pull `Packet.Tune.Core` (doctor/tuning) into the node — both are separate later tasks. Tests: +38 node (telemetry RSSI flow; heard/traffic RSSI round-trip + ADD-COLUMN migration; config round-trip + exactly-one-of validation; serial + by-id resolvers; radio status via a live `PortSupervisor` + fake radio; the radio endpoints via the WAF with a fake scanner) — the affected node suites green (the sandbox's `/tmp`-write denial fails only the unrelated Tailscale-sidecar + app-package tests, environmental). Parity note: **no ax25-ts leg** — `Packet.Node*` is a C#-only surface and nothing on the parity-tracked AX.25 contract changed.

### 2026-07-04 — Radio-package READMEs finalized for publication

`Packet.Radio`, `Packet.Radio.Tait`, and `Packet.Tune.Core` READMEs polished to the house style (H1 / one-liner / capability + Types sections / real-API `## Usage` block / nuget.org cross-links / Status line) as their NuGet package descriptions, since all three are on the `publish-libs.yml` matrix. Docs-only; no code change. Corrected the Tune.Core README's GETRSSI framing (a NinoTNC firmware 3.41-era fast path removed in 3.44, enrichment only — the decode/clip edge-bracketing is authoritative), and flagged Tait's `UnsafeSendCcrOverSdmAsync` (CCR-over-SDM) as experimental/unsafe with a consent-gate pointer.

### 2026-07-03 — CCDI surface gaps closed (backlog #5): extended/binary/legacy SDM, FUNCTION extras, RING typed decode, KAUP8R — hardware-validated

Backlog item 5 — the documented-but-unmodelled CCDI surface in `Packet.Radio.Tait`, probed raw on the rig first, then shipped as driver surface and re-validated over air through the shipped API. **Extended SDM (SFI 04/05)**: the open question was who splits/reassembles — hardware answered *the radios do everything*: one `a` command (GFI 2/SFI 04) carries up to 128 chars; the sender splits into multiple over-air FFSK bursts (SFI 05 continuations, one FFSK-received PROGRESS each), the receiver reassembles natively and RINGs once with the complete message in its one-deep buffer (mid-reassembly reads harmlessly answer "no SDM buffered"), and the auto-ack receipt covers the whole message — proven at 100 and exactly 128 chars, `PDN00002`→`PDN00001`, receipts acknowledged, the radios' CCDI3+CCDI2-Text programming accepting SFI 04 without complaint. So: `SendExtendedSdmAsync` + `TaitSdmSideChannelOptions.EnableExtendedSdm` (`MaxPayloadLength` 32→128; >32-char payloads route extended; **default off** — airtime/fleet-programming tradeoff, documented) and no host-side reassembler. **Binary SDM (GFI 1)** `SendBinarySdmAsync` (SFI 00 ≤32 B / SFI 04 above; refuses CR/LF/XON/XOFF — CCDI line-framing hazards): bytes `01 42 7F FE` delivered verbatim over air, plus a 40-byte extended-binary leg; **legacy `s` SEND_SDM** `SendLegacySdmAsync` delivered/RINGed/acked identically (back-compat only, per the manual). **FUNCTION extras** (transaction-engine one-liners, unit-tested against the manual's own checksummed examples): volume enable+set, Selcall RING output, keypress progress (TM8200-only, documented), channel-change progress, SDM runtime controls (output-on-reception — pushed SDMs surface via the new `SdmReceived` event — caller-ID encode/decode), user-controls lockout (`TaitUserControls`), subaudible validation, keypress simulation (`TaitKey` + duration code). Undocumented **f 0/6 probed once**: qualifiers 1/0 prompt-OK silently, zero observable effect (two-digit qualifier → parameter error); left disabled. **RING TYPE4**: pypdf layout-mode extraction of MMA-00038-06 pp. 53–54 is fully legible and shows the manual defines **no TYPE4 value table** (format names the field; table stops at TYPE3) — `CcdiRingMessage` gains typed `CallType`/`IsEmergency`/`Addressing` + raw `Type4` documented as undefined-by-spec; also found §1.9.8's `…Hi4A` example is a checksum erratum (raw sum low byte, not two's complement). **KAUP8R**: `NinoTncCommands`/`NinoTncSerialPort` gain SETSERNO (0x0A + 8 ASCII) / CLRSERNO (0x0A + 8 zeros) / GETSERNO (0x0E; 8 raw bytes on 0xE0, all-zero → `null`) per upstream tnc-tools — GETSERNO bench-read on TNC A (fw 3.41, register unset); the write path ships unit-tested but deliberately bench-unexercised. **CCR-over-SDM (GFI 2/SFI 03)** is modelled behind explicitly-named `[Experimental("PKTTAIT001")]` members (`UnsafeBuildCcrOverSdmFrame`/`UnsafeSendCcrOverSdmAsync`, XML-warned: remote radio *control*, no consent handshake; receipt = delivery only), pinned to the manual's `a130520312345678M01D0E36` example, and — **with Tom's explicit authorization, run last after everything else was pushed, SAFE commands only (volume `J104`/pulse `QP`, never frequency/power/keying)** — **live-validated radio-to-radio**: the target (in CCR via its local CCDI) executed the SDM-carried CCR commands; the pulse's `Q01P…` response surfaced on the *target's* serial (not over air), confirming the response-routing model; both SDMs auto-acked; and CCR exit soft-reset the target with total recovery (identity/RSSI back, bidirectional SDMs with receipts, no wedge, rig verified channel 0). A working over-air remote-reconfiguration primitive — exactly why the send path stays `Unsafe`-named + `[Experimental]`; the consent/capability gate is a named follow-up (spike doc follow-up 12). THSD Transparent noted untestable on this rig (no THSD channel programming). Tests: Radio.Tait.Tests 109 green (+68: SDM wire forms incl. bench-captured literals, FUNCTION vectors, RING decode, side-channel budgets, manual checksum vectors), Kiss.NinoTnc.Tests 181 (+9), Tune.Core.Tests 119, build 0 errors. Found on the way: the hand-enumerated `ci.yml` test matrix was missing the four newer test projects (`Packet.Mcp.Tests`, `Packet.Radio.Tests`, `Packet.Radio.Tait.Tests`, `Packet.Tune.Core.Tests`) — added (13 → 17 legs, all four verified green in Release under the standard filter), so this PR's test suites are actually CI-enforced from now on. Details: [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §CCDI surface gaps closed. Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg** (C#-only surfaces).

### 2026-07-03 — NinoTNC firmware flasher: `BootloaderNinoTncFirmwareFlasher` behind the `INinoTncFirmwareFlasher` seam + `packet-tune flash-tnc` — hardware-validated, OQ-010 answered

The firmware-flash seam (backlog item 2) gets its real implementation: **`Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc.Firmware.BootloaderNinoTncFirmwareFlasher`** — a native C# port of the dsPIC bootloader protocol upstream `flashtnc.py` speaks, byte-identical with the wire sequence validated by seven successful upstream flashes on the bench rig. Protocol: drain-to-5 s-silence (abort after 15 s of chatter), stranded-bootloader probe (`'R'`→`'K'` resumes an interrupted flash without the KISS entry handshake), 3× payload-less GETALL fill-and-flush, bootloader entry `C0 0D 37 C0` (new `NinoTncCommands.BuildBootloaderEntryKissFrame`) awaiting `'K'` ≤15 s, `'V'` one-letter version → chip variant (lowercase = EP256 / uppercase = EP512; mismatch with the hex → `'R'` + refuse), then the image line-by-line (first line char-by-char at 100 ms — page erase) with per-line `K`/`Z`/`F`/`N`/`X` replies. `NinoTncFirmwareHexImage` validates and chip-classifies the image (the 7 known first-bootloader fingerprint lines) **before the modem is touched**, and — stricter than upstream — refuses images without the `:00000001FF` EOF record (they could never complete and would strand the modem). Progress per accepted line (`IProgress<NinoTncFlashProgress>` — the seam signature changed from `IProgress<double>`; only the in-repo stub implemented it); failures throw `NinoTncFlashException` with a classified `NinoTncFlashFailure` + lines-written/reply-byte/chip context; cancellation is honoured between steps/lines and **documented to strand the bootloader if fired after entry** (recoverable: re-run, the stranded probe resumes — unit-tested end-to-end); pre-transfer aborts write `'R'` to restore the running firmware, mid-transfer failures never do (old image partially erased). All timings in one internal `NinoTncFlashTimings` record pinned to the validated defaults. **CLI**: `packet-tune flash-tnc <tncPort> <hexFile> [--yes]` — prints image size/lines/target chip + GETVER, refuses if another process holds the port (`/proc/*/fd` scan), interactive confirm without `--yes`, percent progress, post-flash GETVER re-verify + mode-0 reminder (catalogue-backed `--latest` left as a follow-up; file-path flashing first). **Hardware (SAFE zero-change variant)**: TNC B `/dev/ttyACM1` reflashed with the same 3.41 it was running — GETVER 3.41 before and after, 17 535/17 535 lines in 193 s, bootloader `'d'`/EP256, first try, no retries; rig restored (SETHW mode 6 +16 RAM-only, SETBCNINT 0, GETVER round trip green; `/dev/ttyACM0` and the radios untouched). Tests: Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc.Tests 172 green (+22: 12 scripted-fake flasher protocol tests incl. stranded-recovery and cancel-then-recover, 8 hex-image, 2 wire-frame), build 0 errors. **OQ-010 answered** (§15 updated). Details: [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §flash-tnc. Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg** (`Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc` is a C#-only surface).

### 2026-07-03 — Radio-health trending: `TaitRadioHealthMonitor` (CCTM 063/047/318-319 sampling, idle-offset-corrected fwd/rev TREND) + `packet-tune radio-health` — hardware-validated

Periodic radio-health sampling for Tait-attached ports (backlog item 3), library-only for now (node wiring is a later optional element). **`Packet.Radio.Tait.TaitRadioHealthMonitor`**: samples on an interval (default 10 s) — averaged RSSI (CCTM 063; skipped while transmitting, own-RSSI reads the muted receiver), PA temperature (CCTM 047), and the raw forward/reverse power detectors (CCTM 318/319) — classifying detector readings by the radio's PTT reports (`TransmitterStateChanged`): idle readings feed rolling **idle-offset medians**, transmit readings become offset-corrected fwd/rev millivolts + a reverse/forward ratio, and a keying edge triggers an immediate extra sample (150 ms PA settle, so short keyings are captured between ticks); a mid-sample keying edge discards that tick's detector readings rather than corrupt either pool; per-read failures null the field and the loop carries on. Typed `SampleTaken` events + a rolling min/median/max window `Summarize()`; consumers alert on **change**. Per the Tait service docs (now summarized + URL-cited in the spike doc and the XML docs): the detectors are raw √P-scaled diode millivolts, Tait itself never computes VSWR (reverse detector = mismatch-protection foldback; go/no-go specced only at High power) — so the ratio is deliberately exposed as a per-station TREND, never VSWR. **CLI**: `packet-tune radio-health <ccdi> [--interval 10] [--duration 60] [--key-once [s]]` (live table + summary; `--key-once` keys ONCE via FUNCTION 9 at channel-programmed power, hard-capped 3 s). **Hardware**: exercised on the rig (TM8110 s/n 19925328): idle RSSI at the −128 dBm squelched floor, PA 27–28 °C/~470 mV, one 2 s VeryLow keying captured by the edge-triggered sample — fwd/rev 388/164 mV (session 2 read 388/172), ratio 0.423 trend row, plus a live 15 mV idle-forward blip (the reading idle-offset subtraction exists for); rig left unkeyed, both radios verified channel 0, TNCs untouched. Tests: +7 Radio.Tait (41 total; scripted `FakeSerialIo` extracted to a shared test helper), Radio 8 + Tune.Core 119 green, build 0 errors. Details: [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §Radio-health trending. Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg** (`Packet.Radio.Tait`/`Packet.Tune` are C#-only surfaces).

### 2026-07-03 — Mode coordination over the SDM side channel: `IRadioSideChannel` + propose/confirm/commit/revert protocol + `packet-tune mode-coord` — the §5.10 mode-agility seed, hardware-validated

The radios' FFSK side channel is mode- and channel-agnostic (proven across every prior session), which breaks the chicken-and-egg of renegotiating TNC modes over the very link being changed — this PR promotes it into the seam Phase 10's mode agility needs and validates the first coordinated mode/channel renegotiations on the rig. **`Packet.Radio` gains `IRadioSideChannel`** (radio-native small-datagram control plane: send/receive + async over-air `DeliveryReceipt` + `MaxPayloadLength`; XML-doc'd capability-gating intent — drivers advertise machinery via the new `RadioCapabilities.SideChannel` flag, consumers gate on a live probe because radio programming can still have the feature off; no Tait dependency) with the implementation moved down a layer as **`Packet.Radio.Tait.TaitSdmSideChannel`** (ex-`TaitSdmChannel`); `SdmTuningLink` now rides the abstraction (`ITuningLink` unchanged; payload budget now comes from the channel). **`Packet.Tune.Core` gains the mode-coordination protocol**: `MODE` telegram verb (`propose|confirm|reject|commit|sent|report|revert`, all inside the 32-char SDM budget), `ModeCoordinator`/`ModeResponder` state machines over any `ITuningLink` with hardware behind an `IModeCoordStation` seam (`NinoTncModeCoordStation`: SETHW+16 RAM-only + settle frame + GETALL verify; GO_TO_CHANNEL + verify + retry; tagged `PMODE a<tag>` probe frames). Shape: nothing switches until the **commit's delivery receipt** proves both ends hold it; the commit's seq **tags the verification probes** (stale probes can't validate the wrong attempt); N probes each way with decode counts exchanged over SDM; **any failure past commit reverts both ends to the session's home mode/channel** + re-verifies the home link; a **responder idle watchdog** (150 s) reverts unilaterally when coordination is lost — the recovery for a split channel, the one failure SDM can't coordinate across. All TM8110 auto-ack-wedge guards carry over. **CLI:** `packet-tune mode-coord --role coordinator|responder --tnc --radio --peer [--sequence m[@ch],…|--sweep] [--strict-bandwidth] [--channel-width]` — sweep walks the IL2P+CRC catalog, lenient by default (try everything, let probes speak), `--strict-bandwidth` skips the wiki-cited 25 kHz modes on narrow (new `NinoTncCatalog.WideChannelModes` = {0,1,2} + `RequiresWideChannel`); always ends at home (coordinated, else local restore). **Hardware (rig, both TNCs 3.41):** (1) full sweep on channel 0 — 8 modes coordinated propose→…→probes×2 at ~60–90 s each, reproducing the post-retap survey exactly (mode 1 dead → reverted + home verified 2/2; mode 3 dead A→B only → reverted; mode 7 the known 4/5 A→B marginal; rest solid), then a **live unplanned failure**: a lost DCD-clear PROGRESS latched `ChannelBusy` true (channel measured idle at −128 dBm afterwards) and every backstop fired in sequence — busy-gate refused to key, ambiguous commit answered stay-home + best-effort revert, responder watchdog reverted its end after 150 s, CLI local-restore converged the coordinator; both ends verified home with no shared state. (2) **Coordinated channel switch** `1@1,3@1,6@0`: mode 1 (19k2, dead-on-narrow) **solid both ways on wide** at 531 ms mean TX, mode 3 likewise, coordinated return to ch 0/mode 6 solid — exit 0. (3) **Failure-revert demo** `--sequence 1` on narrow: 0/5 both ways → SDM revert → home link alive 2/2, exit 0; take 1 of this demo found two real bugs (home-verify counter opened after a possibly-blocking home apply; 15 s settle-echo wait), both fixed + regression-tested. Tests: +38 Tune.Core (119 total; codec/state-machines/revert/watchdog over an in-memory two-station ether); Radio 8, Radio.Tait 34, Kiss.NinoTnc 142 all green. Docs: spike-doc §Mode coordination over SDM (verbatim tables + the failure narrative), new design note [`docs/research/radio-side-channel-mode-agility.md`](research/radio-side-channel-mode-agility.md) (how this feeds Phase-10 node mode agility: side channel on Tait-attached ports, propose/confirm/commit between nodes, periodic mode+quality beacons, doctor capability gating — **explicitly not implemented in Packet.Node yet**), §5.10 workstream note. New follow-ups filed: stale-DCD escape for the busy gate, session epochs for long-lived responders, the settle-echo miss rate under mode-coord. Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg** (`Packet.Radio*`/`Packet.Tune.Core` are C#-only surfaces).

### 2026-07-03 — R5→R2 RX-tap retap: the direct-FSK modes come alive; post-retap survey + 9600 baseline; `SW` advice state; 3.41 catalog fixes

**The hardware fix.** Both TM8110s' global RX tap point was reprogrammed **R5 → R2**: R5 sat *after* the 300–3000 Hz voice filters — the root cause of every direct-FSK/GFSK "dead" verdict of the last two sessions (a voice filter passes all audio-band modems and guts G3RUH-style near-DC energy, symmetrically, at any pot position); R2 is pre-filter, post-**PSD-normaliser** (the correct modem pair with TX tap T13), chosen over raw-discriminator R1 because the normaliser makes the tapped level invariant across the rig's narrow/wide channel switching (bench-confirmed: idle RX-audio ≈ −34.4 dB, unchanged from pre-retap). The diagnosis signature is recorded as a reusable doctor diagnostic (**audio-band modes pass + direct-FSK dead both directions ⇒ RX tap after the voice filters**; candidate `TuningDoctor` probe named in the spike-doc follow-ups). Also for the record: the radio reprogramming **re-enumerated the CCDI ports with swapped associations** (now `/dev/ttyUSB2` = s/n 19925328 = `PDN00002` = TNC A's radio; `/dev/ttyUSB3` = s/n 19925369 = `PDN00001` = TNC B's) — recovered via `TaitRadioPortDiscovery`'s CCDI-serial disambiguation; standing rule: re-run discovery after any cable/programming work. **Post-retap survey** (same `mode-survey` procedure, both TNCs 3.41, 5 rounds/direction — now 44 cells, because the catalog's two 4FSK modes joined the IL2P+CRC selection, below): mode 2 (9600 GFSK) went 0/5-everywhere → **5/5 in all four cells including the narrow channel**, mode 4 (4800 GFSK) likewise — the session-6 "occupied bandwidth"/"untuned pots" readings are retired; 19200 4FSK is dead-on-narrow/solid-on-wide (~554 ms mean — the fastest cells this rig has produced) and 9600 4FSK solid-on-wide, per their published bandwidths; the one hard directional cell (9600 4FSK A→B on narrow, 0/5) plus 4 of 5 marginals are A→B — **TNC A's TX pot remains the tuning target**, now with its tightest-margin reproducer; IL2P counters truthful in all 44 cells. **The deferred 9600 baseline ran scripted** (no human; `deviation-sdm`, both TNCs mode 2, radios channel 1, meter ACM1/USB3 ← peer PDN00002, tuned ACM0/USB2 ← peer PDN00001, two rounds, pots untouched): all telegrams attempt-1/receipts-acknowledged/zero retries; trend `5/5 | clip 0 | −89.7/−89.9 dBm | level −39.3/−39.5 dB | AD:OK`, ≈5 dB quieting vs the −34.4 dB idle — the "before pot tuning" record, sitting usefully between the AFSK ≈0 dB and undecodable-9600 9–12 dB quieting cases. Rig left on channel 0 / mode 6, verified, both runs. **Code (this PR):** `DeviationAdvisor` gains the **`Sweep` advice state (`SW` — "no decode — sweep the pot")**: 0/n decodes without clipping now advise SW instead of the directional-lie `UP` (session 6's dead-link capture sent `AD:UP` for cells no pot position could fix); clipping still wins at 0/n (it *is* directional); wire additive — UP/DN/OK unchanged, old peers parse SW as unknown advice (null), never a direction; `TuningSession` renders the token + legend on both ends. `NinoTncCatalog`: **firmware-3.41 mode-14 byte alias `0x90` → mode 14** (3.44 reports 0x23; closes session 6's "unrecognised firmware byte" quirk — verified resolving live in this survey), and the **4FSK mode names corrected to "19200/9600 4FSK IL2P+CRC"** per the OARC wiki mode table (Tom's citation; there is no AX.25 4FSK — the kissproxy-inherited bare names wrongly excluded modes 1/3 from every IL2P+CRC name filter, including the survey's). Tests: +5 Tune.Core (81 total), +5 NinoTnc catalog/status cases (142 total). Full narrative + tables in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §The R5→R2 retap. Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg** (`Packet.Tune.Core`/`Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc` are C#-only surfaces).

### 2026-07-03 — Wide-channel IL2P+CRC mode survey (`packet-tune mode-survey`) — §5.Y item 4 done for this rig

The radios' new channel-1 (wide, 25 kHz) programming got its first scripted workout, and §5.Y item 4's mode survey is done for this rig. **New CLI:** `packet-tune mode-survey <tncA> <tncB> <ccdiA> <ccdiB> [--channels 0,1] [--rounds 5] [--json [path]]` — for each channel (both radios switched via GO_TO_CHANNEL and verified via FUNCTION 0/5/2, with a both-to-channel-0 recovery path if they ever split) and each **IL2P+CRC** catalog mode (name contains `IL2P+CRC` exactly — modes 2/4/5/7/8/9/10/11/14; plain-IL2P 13 and legacy AX.25 excluded, per Tom's directive), SETHW both TNCs (+16 RAM-only, settle throwaway frame per the second-frame quirk, GETALL running-mode verify), then N short-UI-probe rounds per direction recording decode count, mean send→decode latency, busy-gated receiver CCDI RSSI, and the receiver's GETALL `IL2PRxPkts`/`IL2PRxUnCr` deltas; Markdown table + optional JSON; **the rig is left on channel 0 / mode 6 in a `finally`, always**. Plus two bench primitives: `set-mode <tnc> <mode>` (SETHW + settle + verify) and `radio-channel <ccdi> [n]` (report / switch-and-verify). Reusable logic in `Packet.Tune.Core.ModeSurvey` (mode selection, settle/probe frames, per-mode receive deadlines, busy-gated-median RSSI reduction, verdicts dead/MARGINAL/solid, Markdown/JSON rendering) + 13 unit tests (76 Tune.Core total). **Hardware run (both TNCs 3.41, 5 rounds/direction, 36 cells):** GO_TO_CHANNEL works cleanly live (first use — prompt-completed, unsolicited PROGRESS 21 on the retune, verify matches first try, radios never split). **Findings:** 9600 GFSK + 4800 GFSK are dead on **both** widths with carrier confirmed at −89 dBm and `IL2PRxUnCr` Δ0 (no IL2P sync at all) — the wide channel alone does not bring GFSK up, so the untuned TX-DEV pots are now the prime suspect (3600 QPSK being solid on *narrow* rules out pure occupied-bandwidth); the wide channel **does** stabilise the marginals (2400 QPSK A→B 4/5→5/5, 300 AFSKPLL A→B 3/5→5/5); **every marginal cell is A→B** — TNC A's TX deviation, the tuning assistant's target; IL2P rx-counter deltas matched decode counts in all 36 cells; latency scales with bit rate as expected. Mode-2-on-wide being dead means the planned deviation-sensitive-mode SDM baseline was **skipped as specified**; a two-round `deviation-sdm` session on the wide channel (TNCs in mode 2) ran anyway as the SDM-on-wide confirmation: **all telegrams delivered attempt-1 with acknowledged receipts, zero retries**, assistant honestly reported 0/5 + `AD:UP`, and the GETRSSI level field showed the undecodable GFSK carrier *quieting* the RX audio (12.4/9.2 dB) — live level signal in exactly the deviation-sensitive regime. Full table + quirks (sporadic settle-frame ACKMODE echo absence; 3.41 mode-14 firmware byte `0x90` unknown to the 3.44-locked catalog) in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §Wide-channel IL2P+CRC survey. §5.Y item-4 status updated (SNR-floor half stays open — fixed pad). Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg**.

### 2026-07-03 — Packet.Tune GETRSSI fast path (firmware 3.41-era), hardware-validated

`NinoTncBurstMeter` probes GETRSSI (KISS `0x09 A7` — the 3.41-only RX-audio RMS meter, removed in 3.44) once at session start; when it answers, it captures an idle baseline (median of 5) and samples the level on the RSSI cadence during each burst (DCD-gated median, max-quieting fallback). `MS` telegrams gain an optional `lvl:`/`l` field — absent-parses-as-null for old peers, and the compact SDM form drops the level first rather than busting the 32-char budget. `DeviationAdvisor.DescribeLevel` enriches the advice line/trend table ("level −31.7 dB, −1.4 dB quieting, level steady") while the decode/clip bracketing verdicts stay authoritative; doctor's getrssi PASS now reads "available (firmware 3.41-era) — deviation meter fast path active". Validated live: both rig TNCs back on **3.41 mode 6 for the test campaign** (3.44 stays production guidance), radios confirmed on the new narrow channel 0, doctor all-PASS, two-round `deviation-sdm` session with the level riding the SDM (`V1|1|MS|5/5|c0|r-89.7|l-31.7`, 28 chars) and zero receipt retries. Bench note: AFSK bursts read ≈ idle noise level (negative quieting) unlike the 440 Hz tone — the level *trend* is the deviation signal for data bursts. Details in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §The 3.41 fast path. Parity note: no parse-options/listener change → no ax25-ts leg.

### 2026-07-03 — Firmware 3.44 flash + Packet.Tune.Core: remote deviation tuning (SDM + internet flavours), capability doctor — hardware-validated

Closes the "internet/SDM-coordinated remote flavour" follow-up from the entry below. **Firmware flash:** both rig TNCs 3.41 → **3.44** (upstream flashtnc). Findings: **GETRSSI (0x09 A7) was REMOVED** (no reply; it was an undocumented 3.41 feature — `GetRssiAsync` stays with "firmware 3.41 only" docs, `measure`/`deviation` degrade to "n/a on this firmware", and nothing new depends on it); GETALL still answers the labelled form on demand; register 0B still doesn't count host traffic (echo-timing fallback stays); CQBEEP-N + `[TARPNstat` arming unchanged (4.02 s tone for SSID 4 verified); a fresh flash boots **mode 0** — which produced a false "pot override" verdict until `verify-control`/`doctor` learned to **pin a known-good mode first** (default 6, `--mode`/`--keep-mode`). **New packable library `src/Packet.Tune.Core`** (in the publish matrix): `TuningTelegram` (`V1|seq|verb|args`, verbs HI/RQ/MS/AD/BY; MS args `dec/<n>|fec:<Δ>|clip:<Δ>|rssi:<dbm>` with a compact wire form that fits the 32-char SDM budget), `ITuningLink` with **`SdmTuningLink`** (telegrams over Tait CCDI SDMs — the radios' own FFSK at factory deviation, independent of the pot under tune; delivery-receipt-gated retries ×3/2 s, seq dedupe, DCD-clear etiquette) and **`WebSocketTuningLink` + `RendezvousRelay`** (minimal TcpListener/RFC-6455 relay; two clients pair on a single-use 6-digit PIN, frames forwarded verbatim, session dies with either socket), the shared **`TuningSession`** assistant loop (meter requests bursts, measures decode-rate / FEC delta / lost-ADC clipping / CCDI RSSI — the GETRSSI-less meter — and advises UP/DN/OK; tuned end fires bursts, shows a trend table, prompts the operator), `TxDelayControlCheck` (now CSMA-pinned: persistence 255/slottime 0 for the measurement, 3 unkey-gapped samples per point, minimum kept — 0.298/0.301/0.299 s measured for the 0.300 s step across three runs; previously the p-persistence slot deferral and ACKMODE **keying-train chaining** made single samples scatter 0.001–0.793 s), and **`TuningDoctor`** (probes: GETVER, GETRSSI-present, DIPs 1111, mode sanity, mode-pinned TXDELAY control, CCDI identity, PROGRESS, SDM-enabled via error-0/06 classification, TNC↔radio PTT pairing — each pass/fail/unknown + one-line remedy; `--json` for machines). **CLI:** `doctor`, `deviation-sdm`, `deviation-remote` (tuned role generates + prints the spoken PIN), `rendezvous --listen`, `radio-reset`. **The big hardware find — TM8110 SDM auto-ack wedge:** keying the radio (data PTT or its own SDM send) while its auto-ack of a just-received SDM is in flight *wedges the ack engine* — the radio keeps receiving SDMs but never acks again until a CCR-enter/exit soft reset (`radio-reset`, bench-recovered) or power cycle. Avoided by construction: 2 s post-receive guard on every link send, 2.5 s pre-burst delay, and the meter never transmits unsolicited. **Hardware validation (rig, both TNCs 3.44 mode 6):** doctor all-PASS on both stacks; deviation-sdm full two-round session with zero receipt retries after the guards (5/5 decode, `AD:OK` steady, −90.1 dBm — and the retry×3 path demonstrated live beforehand, incl. honest failure to a wrong peer ID); relay + deviation-remote over loopback with the real generated-PIN flow, one full RQ→MS→AD cycle with RF stimulus. 45 unit tests in `tests/Packet.Tune.Core.Tests` (codec, advisor, SDM retries/dedupe/guard over a fake channel, relay pairing over real loopback sockets, fake-link assistant round trips). Driver: `NinoTncStatusFrame`/`Delta` gain `LostAdcSamples`, `NinoTncTxTestFrame` keeps `RawFields`. Full narrative in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §Firmware 3.44 flash. Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg**.

### 2026-07-02 — NinoTNC remote-diagnostics driver surface + Packet.Tune tuning CLI (hardware-validated)

Closes the §17 "tuning-CLI primitives" follow-up (entry below): the bench-verified NinoTNC remote-diagnostics commands are now first-class `Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc` surface, and a new `tools/Packet.Tune` CLI drives the tuning loop. **Driver:** `NinoTncCommands` (GETALL `0x0B/00`, GETVER `0x08/00`, STOPTX `0x09/00`, SETBCNINT `0x09/F0+mins`, GETRSSI `0x09/A7`; replies arrive on raw KISS command byte `0xE0`), `NinoTncStatusFrame` — the NUMERIC `=II:` register report (registers 00–11: fw version, KAUP8R identity, uptime, board id, DIP positions, config mode, AX.25/IL2P RX counters, TX packets, preamble words, loop cycles, PTT-on/DCD-on ms, RX/TX bytes, FEC-corrected bytes; the numeric sibling of the labelled `=FirmwareVr:` diagnostic) — plus `NinoTncStatusDelta` (two snapshots → per-register deltas, `PreambleSeconds` = words×16÷baud), `NinoTncRssiReading` (GETRSSI reply; bench-calibrated as **RX-audio RMS dB**, idle flat-tap ≈ −33 / quieted 440 Hz tone ≈ −62 — a remote level meter, not RF dBm), `NinoTncCqBeep` (`[TARPNstat` arming + `CQBEEP-N` beep-request factories, N∈1–15 = tone seconds), awaiting helpers on `NinoTncSerialPort` (`GetAllAsync`/`GetVersionAsync`/`GetRssiAsync`/`StopTxAsync`/`SetBeaconIntervalAsync`/`ArmCqBeepResponderAsync`/`SendCqBeepRequestAsync`), two new classifier events (status report, RSSI reading), and `NinoTncAirTestFrame` generalised from hardcoded `CQBEEP-5` to any `CQBEEP-N` (SSID exposed = commanded tone seconds). **CLI (`tools/Packet.Tune`, not packable):** `verify-control` (DIP/config-mode report + commanded-vs-measured TXDELAY check, settling frame discarded per the settings-settle-on-second-frame rule), `measure` (GETVER + GETRSSI baseline n=5 + optional Tait CCDI RSSI/noise floor via `Packet.Radio.Tait`), `deviation` (interactive TX-deviation loop, local bench flavour: arm the remote TNC, trigger CQBEEP-8 from the local one, meter the tone with GETRSSI, operator adjusts the remote's TX-DEV pot between runs, trend table; the internet/SDM-coordinated remote flavour is the named follow-up). **Hardware-validated on the rig (both TNCs fw 3.41, mode 6):** verify-control end-to-end — DIPs `1111` software control, ACKMODE echo timing moved 0.303 s for the 0.300 s commanded TXDELAY step; measure — idle RX-audio −32.6 dB, TM8110 noise floor −129.4 dBm (matches the spike sessions); deviation — arming worked first try, CQBEEP-8 produced a ~7.5 s metered tone window at −64.3 dB vs −32.5 dB idle. **Firmware 3.41 quirks found and worked around:** GETALL answers with the *labelled* diagnostic (mapped via `NinoTncStatusFrame.FromDiagnostic`, so `GetAllAsync` returns one shape on any firmware); the numeric report is instead a spontaneous 60 s status beacon (`TNC>USB` fake UI; SETBCNINT paces it; reg 01 is eight RAW bytes); **register 0B never increments** for host traffic (flat at TXDELAY 200/500/1000 ms, ACKMODE+plain, modes 6/7, both sides) so `verify-control` falls back to ACKMODE TX-completion timing; STOPTX does not cut a CQBEEP tone short. Full table in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §Packet.Tune CLI. 37 new unit-test cases (parsers/factories/driver round-trips over the scripted serial fake; fixtures are live captures). Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change → **no ax25-ts leg**.

### 2026-07-02 — Node: optional `radio:` block attaches a Tait CCDI radio to a port

PDN exposure of the radio layer (same PR as the waves below): a port config may carry `radio: { kind: tait-ccdi, port: /dev/ttyUSB0, baud: 28800 }` — at port start the node opens the radio, enables DCD reporting, and wraps the port transport in `RssiTaggingTransport` (with the NinoTNC bit-rate provider when applicable), so `Ax25InboundFrame.RadioMetadata` flows in production. Validation: known kind, non-empty control port, positive baud, serial-modem transports only. **Radio open failure degrades to a working untagged port** (log warning) — telemetry never blocks traffic. Radio edits are restart-class in the reconcile planner (with a corrected restart-cause summary); KISS-param apply targets the modem transport under the wrapper so CSMA knobs + ACKMODE pacing keep working. 12 new tests (yaml round-trip incl. the FsCheck property, validator, planner, supervisor integration over the real `PortSupervisor`). Web UI surface + duplicate-radio-device validation + live-rig attach smoke are named follow-ups. Also: bench-verified NinoTNC remote-diagnostics primitives for the coming tuning CLI (`[TARPNstat` arming; `CQBEEP-N` = N-second 440 Hz tone, N=7 measured 6.99 s; `GETRSSI` 0x09A7 → RX-audio dB meter, −33 idle noise → −62 quieted tone) — see the spike doc and task list (Packet.Tune).

### 2026-07-02 — Phase-10 wave 2: frame timing, CCR mode, CSMA TX gate, watchdog, discovery

Same-day follow-up to the entry below, driven by Tom's asks (frame-edge timestamps, excess-TXDELAY detection, both computer-control modes, a richer transport seam, hang-up watchdogs, port auto-detection). **Seam**: `RadioMetadata` grew additively (noise floor, RSSI min/max/count, `CarrierRiseAt`, `BurstIndex`, `EstimatedAirtime`, `PreDataCarrier`) — `RssiTaggingTransport` now tracks carrier windows from DCD edges, handles multi-frame trains (one window, indexed frames — bench-proven: 3 queued frames = one carrier, ~370 ms spacing), and computes the **excess-TXDELAY detector input** (`PreDataCarrier` tracked the TX TNC's set TXDELAY +40–75 ms constant across a 200/500/1000 ms sweep); `NinoTncSerialPort` exposes `CurrentMode`/`CurrentBitRateHz` to feed it. **Timing envelope measured**: CCDI RSSI poll RTT 14.4 ms median (σ<1 ms), DCD edge report latency ~27 ms (σ<1 ms). **Settings-settle-on-second-frame** (NinoTNC-specific, bench-confirmed at every TXDELAY step; multi-frame trains are the general-AX.25 part) → `TxDelayHillClimbEstimator` now discards the post-step settling outcome (harmless one-frame insurance on other TNCs). **CSMA**: new `Packet.Radio.CarrierSenseTxGate` — the LinkBench `ITxGatePolicy` seam in production form (defer-send-while-busy, bounded wait, fail-open). **Tait**: full CCDI message set decoded (RING/SDM/display/TDMA), channel ops, DIAL/CANCEL, SDM send/read (blocked on these radios: programming disables SDMs — error 0/06), Transparent-mode enter/exit + byte-pipe events, and a complete **CCR-mode client** (`TaitCcrSession`: Hz-resolution RX/TX frequency, TX power, bandwidth, CTCSS/DCS, Selcall, volume, pulse) — live-validated: entry/pulse/volume/power ACK/exit-reset all work, and **data-line PTT still keys the radio inside CCR**, so a CCR power-step SNR sweep would need no frequency knowledge — but the sweep is abandoned: the rig's attenuators are 2 W-rated, so nothing above VeryLow may be keyed (no upward headroom). Hardware-found protocol trap fixed: the radio prompts *before* a rejection's ERROR → `PromptErrorGrace` (100 ms) on prompt-completed commands. **Watchdog**: keep-alive probes (30 s default; CCR uses the manual's own 10 s-recommended pulse), consecutive-failure → `Faulted` + event with self-healing recovery; pump death faults immediately. **Discovery**: `TaitRadioPortDiscovery` (env override → ttyUSB scan → MODEL-query probe) — live scan identified both TM8110s by CCDI serial where their identical CP2102 USB IDs cannot; PDN/node wiring is the named follow-up. Tests 84+34+8 green across Kiss/Tait/Radio. Full data in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md) §Session 2.

### 2026-07-02 — Packet.Radio + Packet.Radio.Tait: per-frame RSSI + hardware DCD proven on the bench rig (Phase 10 first cut)

The §5.Y hardware-arrival playbook ran for real: the 2× NinoTNC + 2× Tait bench rig (TM8110s, CCDI on the mic-socket serial at 28800, NinoTNCs in software-control mode, radios coupled via ~100 dB attenuation) was probed, characterised, and the headline Phase-10 capability **works end-to-end**: AX.25 UI frames sent TNC→radio→RF→radio→TNC at mode 6, with **every received frame stamped with RSSI (−90.3…−90.7 dBm) and SNR (~38 dB against a live-tracked −129 dBm noise floor)**, and the radio's **hardware carrier-sense (PROGRESS "receiver busy/not busy") leading frame delivery by 559–1020 ms** (TXDELAY + airtime — the head start CSMA never gets from bare KISS). Two new libraries (both added to the `publish-libs.yml` matrix): **`Packet.Radio`** — the OQ-011 `IRadioControl` v0 ({RSSI-get, carrier-sense, PTT-set} + reserved capability flags; frequency/channel members deferred until a second implementation exists) and the generic `RssiTaggingTransport` decorator that populates the until-now-always-null `Ax25InboundFrame.RadioMetadata` by timestamp-correlating a busy-fast/idle-slow RSSI sampler against frame arrival; **`Packet.Radio.Tait`** — the CCDI driver (framing/checksum codec with manual-vector tests, prompt-disciplined transaction engine with unsolicited PROGRESS/ERROR demux, identity/RSSI/PA-temp/fwd-rev-power queries, `SetTransmitterAsync` with unkey-on-dispose, `TransactRawAsync` escape hatch; 29 unit tests over a scripted `ISerialIo`, same seam pattern as `Packet.Kiss.Serial`). Plus `tools/Packet.Tait.Spike` (`inventory` → `artifacts/radio-capabilities/<serial>.json` per playbook item 1, `dcd` monitor, `rf-rssi` the PoC loop). **Hardware findings recorded:** OQ-009 is answered — `SETHW(mode+16)` mode change is immediate, no re-init (both TNCs sat in different modes, were SETHW'd to mode 6 live, and passed traffic first try); the TM8110/CCDI-03.02 firmware **rejects the CCDI TX-power-set** (FUNCTION 0/7 → "unsupported command"), so `TxPowerControl` is not advertised; channel *report* works (FUNCTION 0/5/2), GO_TO_CHANNEL/CCR untested; PROGRESS output is per-session radio state (`SetProgressMessagesAsync(true)` after open); forced-TX ignores the radio's TX timer (driver unkeys on dispose). Spike write-up in [`docs/research/tait-ccdi-spike.md`](research/tait-ccdi-spike.md). Parity note: no `Ax25ParseOptions`/quirk/XID/listener change, so **no ax25-ts leg**. Follow-ups named there: wire `ChannelBusy` into a CSMA TX-gate seam, burst-level RSSI attribution for back-to-back frames, second `IRadioControl` implementation before freezing the abstraction, and `docs/releasing.md`'s package list is stale vs the workflow matrix.

### 2026-06-24 — RELEASE: node-v0.24.0 (runtime log-level switch)

Shipped the runtime log-level switch (the entry below, PR #524) as **`node-v0.24.0`** (`.deb` + GHCR; **additive, no behaviour change** with no overrides set → no `lib-v` tag / no downstream cascade). One of the two steady-state observability gaps Tom green-lit after the "is observability good enough?" assessment (the other — BBS `/healthz` + `/status.json` machine-readable health — ships in `pdn-bbs v0.2.38`; Tom declined the alerting pack, opting to query state on demand via the assistant + the kiss-collector MCP). Deployed to the GB7RDG CT alongside `pdn-bbs v0.2.38` so the cutover/steady-state debug switch is in place before go-live.

### 2026-06-24 — Runtime log-level switch (restart-free, API-driven)

Closes the gap the `node-v0.23.0` entry below names ("the node still has no live log-level switch"): an operator can now raise (or lower) a logging category to Debug/Trace **live, with no restart** — the node's `appsettings.json` is read-only under `ProtectSystem=strict` and a restart drops every session, so the previous answer (a systemd drop-in + restart) was unworkable during a held cutover. New singleton `DynamicLogLevelOverrides` (`src/Packet.Node/Api/`) holds a thread-safe category-prefix → `LogLevel` map and is wired into the MEL filter pipeline as **both** an `IConfigureOptions<LoggerFilterOptions>` (it appends one `LoggerFilterRule` per active override) **and** an `IOptionsChangeTokenSource<LoggerFilterOptions>`. A mutation fires the change token; MEL's `LoggerFactory` re-runs the configurators and re-applies the rebuilt rules to **every already-created logger** — proven by a focused test (`DynamicLogLevelOverridesTests`) that flips a category and watches `ILogger.IsEnabled` change on a logger created before the override. Category matching is MEL's own longest-prefix-wins. Two endpoints on the existing surface (`PdnSystemApi`): `GET /api/v1/system/loglevel` (effective default + active overrides, **read** scope) and `PUT /api/v1/system/loglevel` `{ category, level }` (set; `level` null/""/"clear" removes; bad level → 400; **admin** scope, audited — same gate as the other mutating system endpoints). Default state = no overrides → logging exactly as configured (strictly additive, behaviour-preserving). No new external deps (the host already references the ASP.NET shared framework). Caveat: with **zero** logging providers `IsEnabled` is unconditionally false, so the effect is observable only because the host always has the console provider — a non-issue in production, but the unit test adds a stub provider to exercise it. Integration coverage in `LogLevelApiTests` (round-trip + live flip through the real host + validation + admin-gating).

### 2026-06-23 — RELEASE: node-v0.23.0 (cutover-observability logging)

Shipped cutover-readiness logging for the GB7RDG cutover as **`node-v0.23.0`** (`.deb` + GHCR; **logging-only, no behaviour change**, so no `lib-v` tag / no downstream cascade). An adversarial logging assessment found the node-side AXUDP multipoint peer liveness was **invisible** (only frame drops logged, at Debug) — you couldn't see which of GB7NDH/GB7BDH were actually exchanging. PR #523 adds, transition-based (never per-frame): a Debug "AXUDP-multipoint {LocalPort}: sent to {Call} at {Endpoint}" (first-contact + after-silence), a Trace "heard {Source} from {Endpoint}", and a Debug "learned peer endpoint" line in `AxudpMultipointFrameTransport`; plus an Information "Inbound session accepted from {Peer} on port {Port}" in `PortSupervisor.OnSessionAccepted` (today only faults were logged). Both use the files' own `[LoggerMessage]` source-gen convention; rendered-output tests (capturing logger) prove the first-contact-not-per-frame transition + the heard/learned lines. The node still has no live log-level switch — during cutover raise these to Debug via a systemd drop-in (`Logging__LogLevel__Packet.Node.Core.Transports=Debug`) while the CT is HELD, then remove after (documented in the pdn-bbs cutover runbook). The pdn-bbs counterpart (a "forwarding ACTIVE: N partners, M queued" golive confirmation line) shipped as `pdn-bbs v0.2.37`.

### 2026-06-23 — RELEASE: node-v0.22.0 (control-panel UI for the GB7RDG features)

Completes the four GB7RDG-migration features by giving them structured **web control-panel UI** (PR #522) — they shipped in `node-v0.21.0` as backend + Raw-YAML only, and "a feature isn't done without its UI". Shipped as **`node-v0.22.0`** (`.deb` + GHCR image). **UI only — no .NET / no lib change**, so no `lib-v` tag and no downstream cascade. The React panel (`web/packetnet-ui`) now exposes: the **`axudp-multipoint`** transport kind with a Local-port field + a peers table (call/host/port/broadcast, add/remove, validation mirroring the backend); per-port **`netRomMinQuality`** + **`nodesPaclen`** number inputs in the port's NET/ROM section (blank → inherit / no-cap, `null` not `0`); and a **`netRom.compress`** toggle. The TS domain types (`lib/types.ts`) now declare all four, so the Forms editor reads+writes them — and a latent bug is fixed: a multipoint port authored in Raw YAML was previously **dropped** on a Forms load→save (the draft mapping didn't carry `peers[]`/the new fields), now it round-trips (new `config.roundtrip.test.ts` proves the PUT /config serialize→deserialize deep-equal; extended screen smoke test). Verified: `web-ui` gate (`npm run build && npm run test`) green, 91/91 vitest. The features were already usable via Raw YAML / `pdn config import`, so this didn't gate the GB7RDG cutover; it completes the feature surface.

### 2026-06-23 — RELEASE: lib-v0.17.0 + node-v0.21.0 (GB7RDG migration features)

Shipped the four GB7RDG-migration features (the three entries below — multipoint AXUDP, per-port MINQUAL + NODESPACLEN, NET/ROM L4 compression; PR #521) to the world as a lockstep **`lib-v0.17.0`** (the NuGet libraries) + **`node-v0.21.0`** (the deployable `.deb`). Gated on a genuinely green `main` merge commit (`79794a9`: `ci` + `interop` both success — the interop matrix includes the ax25-ts parity check, which passed, confirming the features are parity-invisible: none adds an `Ax25ParseOptions`/`Ax25SessionQuirks`/`XidParseOptions` named flag or widens the `Ax25Listener` surface, so **no TS leg** is required). **Library API surface that warrants `lib-v0.17.0`:** `Packet.Axudp` gains the public `AxudpMultipointSocket` (the many-peer UDP pump), and `Packet.NetRom` gains the L4-compression negotiation surface (`NetRomCompression`, `ConnectRequestInfo`/`ConnectAckInfo` compression bits, `NetRomOpcode.Compressed`, `NetRomCircuitOptions`). The per-port MINQUAL/NODESPACLEN work is `Packet.Node.Core` configuration (not a published library), so it rides the node release only. **`node-v0.21.0`** is the artifact the GB7RDG migration consumes: it wires all four features through the config seam (`axudp-multipoint` transport kind with a nested `peers:` table; per-port `netRomMinQuality` + `nodesPaclen`; `netRom.compress`) — the translated `GB7RDG` config (4 RF ports + 3 multipoint AXUDP ports, callsign GB7RDG/READNG/IO91lk) validates + round-trips clean against this build via `pdn config import`/`export`. **Downstream pin-bumps N/A:** `axcall`/`packet-term-tui` consume `Packet.Ax25`/`Packet.Kiss` only (untouched this cycle — their 0.17.0 is byte-identical behaviour to 0.16.0), and they use neither `Packet.Axudp` nor `Packet.NetRom`, so a bump would be a no-op version change. The pdn-bbs LinBPQ→pdn-bbs mailbox importer (the migration's other half) shipped separately in `pdn-bbs` (PR #59) and is not part of this packet.net cascade.

### 2026-06-23 — Multipoint AXUDP transport (the BPQAXIP analog)

Added a **multipoint AXUDP transport kind** to the node host — the BPQ `BPQAXIP` model: ONE UDP socket reaching MANY partners, each addressed by `callsign → ip:udpport`, with a per-peer broadcast flag (BPQ's `MAP <call> <ip> UDP <port> [B]`, where `B` = "fan NODES/ID/BEACON broadcasts to this peer"). Until now AXUDP was point-to-point only (one `host`/`port` remote, see `AxudpTransport`); this is the mesh form a real IP-gateway node needs to interlink with the OARC AXIP cloud (the commented `PORT 8` in `gb7rdg-config/bpq32.cfg`, UDP 10093–10095).

**Shape.** New transport kind `axudp-multipoint`:

```yaml
transport:
  kind: axudp-multipoint
  localPort: 10093            # the one shared socket (send + receive for all peers)
  peers:
    - { call: M0LTE-9, host: 10.45.0.185, port: 10093, broadcast: true }
    - { call: GB7OUK,  host: 10.66.66.3,  port: 10094, broadcast: false }
```

`broadcast` is opt-in (default false — the BPQ `B` suffix). Validation: `localPort` in 1..65535 and **non-zero** (no ephemeral bind — partners MAP back to a fixed port); each peer's callsign valid + host non-empty + port in range; **no two peers share a callsign** (the callsign is the routing key, so a duplicate MAP is ambiguous — `GB7OUK` and `GB7OUK-0` collide).

**Routing.** Outbound: read the AX.25 frame's DESTINATION address (first 7 octets — modulo-independent, so no session context needed); a configured peer → that peer's endpoint; a NODES/ID/BEACON broadcast (or UI to any unmapped pseudo-dest) → fan out to every `broadcast=true` peer; otherwise try a learned source endpoint, else drop (a directed frame is never blindly fanned to every peer). Inbound: accept from any sender on the one socket; surface the bare FCS-stripped body straight up (the `Ax25Listener` routes by callsign); learn `source-callsign → sender-endpoint` as a reply fallback, but a configured peer endpoint always wins on send. The 2-octet AX.25 FCS is appended on send + stripped/validated on receive, identical to point-to-point AXUDP (the de-facto wire form).

**Where.** New `AxudpMultipointSocket` (`Packet.Axudp` — the pure UDP pump: send to an endpoint, receive from anyone) + `AxudpMultipointFrameTransport` (`Packet.Node.Core/Transports` — the callsign-routing `IAx25Transport` adapter, with `AxudpMultipointPeerEndpoint` the resolved-endpoint form). Wired through the full closed-union seam: a new `AxudpMultipointTransport` record + `AxudpPeerConfig` (`TransportConfig.cs`, with hand-rolled value equality over the peer list via `ConfigEquality` so a YAML round-trip doesn't needlessly restart the port), the `AxudpMultipointValidator`/`AxudpPeerValidator` arm, the YAML converter (now reads a nested `peers:` sequence) + JSON converter arm, and the `TransportFactory` arm (resolves each peer host via DNS, like the point-to-point arm). Presents as a native `IAx25Transport` with no CSMA/TX-completion (a UDP mesh has neither), so `PortSupervisor` drives it unchanged.

**Parity.** Node-host transport config only — it touches no `Ax25ParseOptions` / `Ax25SessionQuirks` / `XidParseOptions` named-flag inventory and does **not** widen the `Ax25Listener` option surface, so it is ax25-ts-parity-invisible (no TS leg, no `parity-exceptions.json` entry needed). Tests: multipoint-socket FCS round-trip + bad-FCS drop + receive-from-many (`Packet.Axudp.Tests`); callsign→endpoint routing, broadcast fan-out (broadcast peers only), inbound-from-many, learned-reply two-transport round-trip, directed-to-unmapped-is-dropped, no-capabilities (`Packet.Node.Tests`); validator (port/callsign/host/port/dup-call), YAML + JSON round-trip, factory wiring. `dotnet test Packet.Axudp.Tests` 12 green; node suite 1446 green; full solution builds 0 errors under warnings-as-errors + CA1848. No node release in this change (config feature; ships with the next `node-v*`).
### 2026-06-23 — Per-port NET/ROM MINQUAL + NODESPACLEN (GB7RDG bpq32.cfg parity)

Added two per-port NET/ROM knobs GB7RDG's `bpq32.cfg` sets that pdn previously only had node-wide (or not at all), on `feat/netrom-perport`. **(1) Per-port MINQUAL.** New `PortConfig.NetRomMinQuality` (0..255) + `EffectiveNetRomMinQuality(globalDefault)` resolving explicit-per-port → node-wide `NetRomConfig.MinQuality` → canonical 0, modelled next to the existing `NetRomQuality`/`EffectiveNetRomQuality`. Applied at the **route-keep decision**: `NetRomRoutingTable.Ingest` gained a `minQuality` parameter that overrides `options.MinQuality` for the floor in `UpsertRoute` (threaded as an explicit `floor` so a re-advert below the per-port floor removes an existing route, exactly as the table-wide floor does). The service plumbs the per-port value through the frame-trace tap (`OnFrameTraced` → `Ingest(..., portMinQuality)`), so a node can keep only high-grade routes off a busy RF port (GB7RDG's MINQUAL 100) while staying permissive elsewhere. **(2) Per-port NODESPACLEN.** New `PortConfig.NodesPaclen` (28..256 octets; null = no cap = today's behaviour). `NodesBroadcastBuilder.Build` gained a `maxFrameBytes` overload that segments the NODES advert into multiple self-contained UI frames each ≤ the cap (whole entries only — an entry is never split; a cap can only *lower* the entries-per-frame below the structural 11, never raise it). `NetRomService.BroadcastNodes` now builds **per-port** (same entry list, per-port framing) so GB7RDG's `NODESPACLEN=160` (→ 7 entries/frame) fragments a large NODES table. Both knobs are **hot-reloadable** like `NetRomQuality`: the reconcile planner's `NetRomQualityChanged` class now also fires on a MINQUAL/NODESPACLEN edit, and the supervisor's `UpdatePortQuality(portId, quality, minQuality, nodesPaclen)` swaps the attachment live — no port restart, no session disturbance (NET/ROM awareness + advertisement is read-only). Both validated 0..255 / 28..256 (rejected, not clamped). **Default-off preserves byte-for-byte today's behaviour** on every path (null MINQUAL → table-wide floor; null NODESPACLEN → the 11-per-frame structural cap). Tests: per-port MINQUAL resolution + route-keep + fallback + override-both-ways (table-level + end-to-end through the supervisor on the real frame-trace tap); NODESPACLEN segmentation at the cap, the GB7RDG-160 case, sub-entry degradation, and null-equals-unlimited; validator ranges; YAML round-trip; reconcile hot-class for both. `dotnet test` green — `Packet.NetRom.Tests` 289, `Packet.Node.Tests` config/reconcile/integration subset 334. **ax25-ts-parity-invisible**: these are node-host `PortConfig`/`NetRomService` config additions, not `Ax25ParseOptions`/`Ax25SessionQuirks`/listener-surface — no named flag, no TS leg.
### 2026-06-23 — NET/ROM L4 negotiated payload compression (BPQ L4Compress / L2COMPRESS interop)

Added LinBPQ-compatible **negotiated NET/ROM L4 payload compression** (`Packet.NetRom`), to interop with the compression GB7RDG and other BPQ nodes enable. Feasibility was investigated against the real LinBPQ source (`/home/tf/src/linbpq`, M0LTE fork, tag `24.33-203`) FIRST. **Key finding: BPQ's "L2COMPRESS" is not AX.25 link-layer compression — both the `L4Compress` and `L2Compress*` config knobs drive the same NET/ROM L4 circuit compressor in `L4Code.c`; `L2Code.c` has no compression at all, and the L2-only send path (`sendL2Chunk`) is dead code never called. So the work belongs entirely in `Packet.NetRom`, not `Packet.Ax25`** (no AX.25 change — zero parity impact). The exact on-wire scheme, verified in source: (1) **negotiation** — the originator sets bit `0x40` in the high byte of the trailing T1 timer of an extended (17-octet) Connect Request (`L4DATA[16] |= 0x40`); the accepter, iff its own `L4Compress` is on, mirrors agreement with bit `0x80` of the TTL octet in an extended Connect Acknowledge (`L4DATA[1] |= 0x80`); both bits are masked off before the underlying value is read, so a non-understanding peer is unaffected. (2) **data** — a compressed Information frame is flagged `L4COMP=0x10` in the opcode/flags byte (with `L4MORE=0x20` on non-final fragments), and the payload is a **standard zlib stream (RFC 1950)** — BPQ decompresses with a plain `inflateInit`/`inflate` (`doinflate`, `DRATS.c`), and its own deflate (`Compressit`, `HTTPcode.c`) uses `deflateInit(Z_BEST_COMPRESSION)`.

**Feasibility verdict: real, byte-for-byte-interoperable compression was implemented — not merely safe-decline.** Priority 1 (clean negotiation, default OFF, never corrupt) is fully met: compression is enabled on a circuit ONLY when both ends advertised it, so a BPQ (or any) peer that declines transparently runs the link uncompressed. Priority 2 was achievable because the wire framing is fully specified and the data plane is standard zlib: pdn uses `System.IO.Compression.ZLibStream` (RFC 1950, the format `inflateInit` expects). Interop was **proven both directions against real zlib**: a tiny C harness using BPQ's verbatim `doinflate` decoded pdn's compressed output exactly (`Z_OK`, byte-identical), and pdn's decompressor decoded a BPQ-style `deflateInit(Z_BEST_COMPRESSION)` stream exactly. BPQ's `L2Compressit` (its compress-direction function) is **declared but undefined in the entire linbpq tree** — there is no byte-exact encoder to match; `doinflate` is the authoritative, stable contract, and matching the *format* (not the encoder's exact bytes) is what makes the stream decodable, which is verified. Encoder heuristics not reproduced (opportunistic multi-frame batching, recompress-on-overflow) are throughput-only and don't affect decodability; incompressible payloads fall back to raw exactly as BPQ does (`complen >= dataLen ⇒ just send`).

New: `NetRomTransportFlags.Compressed` (0x10) + `NetRomTransportHeader.Compressed`; `ConnectRequestInfo.BuildExtended`/`OffersCompression`; `ConnectAckInfo` codec; `NetRomCompression` (zlib (de)compress, fail-closed + a decompress cap against zip-bombs). `NetRomCircuit` carries the per-circuit negotiated result (`CompressionNegotiated`), compresses outbound logical sends then fragments (flag on every fragment), and reassembles+inflates inbound. Knobs default OFF: `NetRomCircuitOptions.CompressionEnabled`/`ProposedTimerSeconds` (lib) and node-wide `netRom.compress` (`NodeConfig.NetRom`) — additive config, **no schema bump**. +17 NetRom tests (negotiation offer/decline both directions, wire codec, zlib round-trip, the proven-zlib-format assertion, end-to-end compress over fragmentation + loss recovery, incompressible fallback) + a YAML round-trip + the config round-trip property now covers `compress`. 296 NetRom + 430 node NetRom/Config tests green; changed projects build 0-warning under warnings-as-errors + CA1848. **ax25-ts parity: NONE required — no `Ax25ParseOptions`/`Ax25SessionQuirks`/`Ax25Listener` change; this is a NET/ROM-only feature and `Packet.NetRom` has no TS parity counterpart.**

### 2026-06-23 — lib-v0.16.0 downstream cascade (axcall/packet-term-tui v0.2.15; bbs/convers tidy)

Ran the downstream cascade for the 0.14.0→0.16.0 library releases (Tom's call — the lib-v0.16.0 entry below judged downstream pin-bumps "N/A" for a docs-only release, but a full currency cascade was wanted, including the PDN apps). Outcome after inspecting **every** consumer, including the app-catalog manifest (`catalog/apps.yaml`): **axcall v0.2.15** + **packet-term-tui v0.2.15** — `Packet.*` pins 0.13.0→0.16.0, source-compatible (28 / 14 tests green), six-platform release binaries each. **pdn-bbs** — `Packet.Ax25`/`Packet.Kiss` 0.7.0→0.16.0, but these are consumed **only by its interop test harness** (`Bbs.Interop.Tests/Ax25Endpoint.cs` — a `KissTcpClient` fronting an `Ax25Listener`), which compiles clean against 0.16.0 (the transport refactor preserved that shape); the app `.deb` is unchanged → merged, **no app release, no catalog bump**. **pdn-convers** — its `Packet.Ax25`/`Packet.Kiss` 0.7.0 pins were **dead** (no reference/usage; it talks RHP via `RhpV2.Client`) → removed, no release. **dapps / bpqchat / whatspac** — not .NET-NuGet consumers → out of scope. **pdn node itself** — consumes the libs via **ProjectReference** (no NuGet pins) with **0 node-src commits since node-v0.20.0** → no node release (would ship an identical binary).
