Also surfaced + fixed an ecosystem-wide latent CI break: `GHSA-2m69-gcr7-jv3q` — a **no-fixed-version** SQLite-native advisory (transitive `SQLitePCLRaw.lib.e_sqlite3 2.1.11` via `Microsoft.Data.Sqlite 10.0.0`) that began failing every warnings-as-errors build since ~2026-06-18 — by mirroring this repo's existing `NuGetAuditSuppress` into pdn-bbs + pdn-convers.

### 2026-06-23 — RELEASE: lib-v0.16.0 — nuget package documentation (READMEs + shipped XML docs)

Documentation pass over all 11 published packages (Tom: "make sure all the nuget packages we publish have decent, up-to-date documentation"), shipped as a lockstep **`lib-v0.16.0`** (docs-only — no API/behaviour change; a new version is required because a package's README/XML-docs only reach nuget.org on publish). Two deliverables: (1) **a per-package `README.md` for all 11** — 9 created, 2 refreshed (`Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc`, `Packet.Rhp2`); each renders on the nuget.org package page (auto-packed by the existing `Directory.Build.props` rule) with a tagline, install, a quick-start example **grounded in real tests / verified public signatures** (no invented API), key types, and cross-links. Public-safe (placeholder callsigns N0CALL/Q0PDN only). The NinoTnc README's *previous* version had **fabricated APIs** (`AdaptiveNinoTncTransport`, `INinoTncModem`, `TxTestFrameReceivedEvent`) — all corrected to the real surface. (2) **XML doc comments now ship for IntelliSense** — `GenerateDocumentationFile` enabled (per published csproj, scoped to the packages that ship; setting it in `Directory.Build.props` is too early to know which projects are published, and `Directory.Build.targets` is too late for the SDK to wire the doc-file path, so it lives in each of the 11 published `.csproj`) so each package carries its `lib/net10.0/<Pkg>.xml` (e.g. NetRom 223 KB, Agw 25 KB, Core 15 KB). **The doc comments were fixed properly, not suppressed:** an initial pass relaxed the doc diagnostics to non-fatal — reverted at Tom's instruction ("fix the docs properly"). Only **CS1591** (missing-doc-on-a-public-member, the repo's pre-existing "don't mandate 100% coverage" policy) stays suppressed; every other doc diagnostic (CS1570–74 malformed/incomplete, CS1734 bad paramref, CS0419 ambiguous cref) is **enforced as an error** under warnings-as-errors, and all **28 real issues in the published libs were fixed** (Ax25 15, NetRom 10, NinoTnc 2, Kiss 1) — correct param tags added, broken crefs repointed to the real members (`EffectiveSendWindow`→`Ax25SessionContext.EffectiveWindow`, `SubroutineRegistry`→`ISubroutineRegistry`, `MaxCachedPeers`→`Ax25SessionParameters.MaxCachedPeers`, a factually-wrong `Stopwatch.GetElapsedTime`→`TimeProvider.GetElapsedTime`), ambiguous `ConnectAsync`/`TryParse` crefs disambiguated by signature, cross-namespace crefs fully-qualified. Scope kept to the published packages: doc-validation is intentionally **off** for the node host, the unpublished libs, tests and tools (they don't ship docs); the workflow's incidental fixes to non-published `Packet.Node*`/`Rhp2.Server` were reverted. **Follow-up (named, optional):** a separate hygiene pass could enable + clean doc comments for the non-published code too. Verified: full solution builds **0 errors with the doc diagnostics enforced as errors** (the 28 fixes hold); all 11 published libs emit `.xml`; tests/non-published emit none; each package pack-verified to contain both `README.md` and its `.xml`. No node release; no TS leg; downstream pin-bumps N/A.

### 2026-06-23 — RELEASE: lib-v0.15.0 — Packet.Aprs / Packet.Agw / Packet.NetRom / Packet.Rhp2 added to the published NuGet set

Published four more libraries to nuget.org (Tom's request, after a per-package assessment of every unpublished lib), as a lockstep **`lib-v0.15.0`** release — the published set grows 7→11: **+ `Packet.Aprs`** (APRS payload codec), **+ `Packet.Agw`** (AGWPE / SV2AGW client), **+ `Packet.NetRom`** (NET/ROM L3/L4 + INP3), **+ `Packet.Rhp2`** (RHPv2 wire codec) — alongside the existing seven, all at 0.15.0. Each is a self-contained, externally-useful protocol/codec/client library whose dependency graph resolves on nuget (Aprs→Core; NetRom→Core+Ax25; Agw + Rhp2 zero-dep; every dep is published in this same release or already on nuget). Changes: packaging metadata added where missing (NetRom had none; Aprs gained `PackageId`+`Title`; Agw tidied to the house shape — dropped the redundant `Authors`-override / license / repo / symbol fields `Directory.Build.props` already supplies; Rhp2 already had a full block), all four appended to the `publish-libs.yml` matrix (+ header comment), and the README package table updated (Aprs/Agw/Rhp2 → published links; a `Packet.NetRom` row added — it had been missing entirely; `Packet.Rhp2.Server` row marked node-internal). Each of the four pack-verified locally at 0.15.0 (nuspec id/version/metadata + dependency shape) before push. Released via the **`lib-v0.15.0` tag** push. **Deliberately NOT published:** `Packet.Mcp` (node-host MCP tool plumbing bound to the `INodeMcpBackend` seam — only pdn can implement it, no external consumer) and `Packet.Rhp2.Server` (hard-depends on `Packet.Node.Core`, the app core, in its public API — it's the wire-facing network plane consumed over TCP/9000, not as a .NET dependency; would need decoupling first). No node release (the node consumes the libs in-repo). No TS leg (parity-invisible). Downstream `axcall`/`packet-term-tui` pin-bumps are N/A — neither uses these.

### 2026-06-22 — RELEASE: lib-v0.14.0 — Packet.Axudp added to the published NuGet set

Published **`Packet.Axudp`** to nuget.org for the first time (Tom's request), as a lockstep **`lib-v0.14.0`** library release. The publish set grows 6→7: `Packet.Core`, `Packet.Ax25`, `Packet.Ax25.Transport.Abstractions`, `Packet.Kiss`, `Packet.Kiss.Serial`, `Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc`, **+ `Packet.Axudp`** — all at 0.14.0 (the other six re-published unchanged; the AXUDP code itself is unchanged — it was simply never in the publish matrix because no external consumer had needed it, not for any correctness reason). `Packet.Axudp` is `AxudpSocket`, a bidirectional AX.25-over-IP (RFC 1226) endpoint that UDP-encapsulates AX.25 frames with the mandatory 2-octet FCS. Changes: added `PackageId`/`Title`/`Description`/`PackageTags` to `src/Packet.Axudp/Packet.Axudp.csproj` (house style), appended `Packet.Axudp` to the `publish-libs.yml` matrix (+ refreshed its header comment), and flipped the README package table to the published link. Pre-publish pack-verified locally at 0.14.0 — nuspec id/version/metadata correct and the deps (`Packet.Ax25 0.14.0`, `Packet.Core 0.14.0`, `Packet.Ax25.Sdl 0.10.0`) match the shape of the already-published `Packet.Kiss`/`Packet.Ax25` nuspecs (the flattened `Packet.Ax25.Sdl 0.10.0` is normal and already on nuget). Released via the **`lib-v0.14.0` tag push** (the proper path — the tag exists this time, unlike the 0.13.0 dispatch). No node release (the node consumes the libs in-repo via ProjectReference). No TS leg (parity-invisible). Downstream `axcall`/`packet-term-tui` pin-bumps are N/A — neither uses AXUDP, so they stay on 0.13.0.

### 2026-06-22 — Follow-up: lib-v0.13.0 tag created retroactively + downstream 0.13.0 pins shipped

Closed the two tidy-up items the lib-v0.13.0 publish entry (below) flagged for "a machine with push rights." (1) **`lib-v0.13.0` git tag created on origin.** The `publish-libs.yml` dispatch path doesn't tag and the publishing sandbox couldn't push one, so origin had nuget.org 0.13.0 but no tag (`git tag -l 'lib-v*'` showed 0.12.0). Tagged the exact build commit `e9777a6` — verified against the four published 0.13.0 nupkgs' embedded SourceLink `<repository commit>` (all four agree), which is the node-v0.20.0 base `b0533d9` + the #514 docs-only ledger merge — annotated to match the existing lib-tag style. `git tag -l 'lib-v*'` now ends at 0.13.0; the next library release is 0.14.0+ as the entry warned. (2) **Downstream pins bumped to 0.13.0** (`docs/releasing.md` Step 3): `axcall` → **v0.2.14** (28 tests green) and `packet-term-tui` → **v0.2.14** (14 tests green), each built+tested against the published 0.13.0 NuGet first (source-compatible — 0.13.0 is API-unchanged vs 0.12.0), merged on green, tagged, and their `release.yml` produced the six-platform binaries (6 assets each). No TS leg (parity-invisible). Both items the lib-v0.13.0 entry left open are now resolved.

### 2026-06-22 — RELEASE: node-v0.20.0 (config value-equality fix + review pass)

Shipped the accumulated review work (PRs #511/#512/#513) to the world as **`node-v0.20.0`**. The node-facing behavioural change is the config value-equality fix (`ApplicationConfig`/`AppOverrideConfig`/`WebAuthn`/`Tailscale` records now compare collection members by value, so a YAML round-trip no longer reads as "changed"); everything else in the arc is behaviour-preserving (internal refactors, the `NodeConfig`/`NodeConfigValidator` per-area splits, repo-wide brace enforcement) plus substantial new test coverage (Kiss.Serial pump, NinoTNC ACKMODE, Mcp/Axudp/Agw edges, config round-trip). Gated on a genuinely green `main` merge commit (`b0533d9`: `ci` + `interop` both success). The release (3 arch `.deb`s + self-contained `.tar.gz`s + `latest.json` + `SHA256SUMS`) was produced by `publish-node.yml` via **`workflow_dispatch` (version 0.20.0)** rather than a `node-v*` tag push — the remote dev environment's git proxy 403s tag/`main` pushes, and `gh release create` in the dispatch path creates the `node-v0.20.0` tag itself, so the artifact shape is identical. **No `lib-v` tag**: the published NuGet libraries had no API/behaviour change this cycle (every Packet.Ax25/Kiss.Serial/Kiss.NinoTnc edit was `internal`/`private` — timer-name constants, the `DispatchInbound`/`BuildSession` extractions, the `ISerialPortIo`/`OpenForTest` test seams), so per `docs/releasing.md` § "When to release" a library publish wasn't warranted and the downstream `axcall`/`packet-term-tui` pin-bumps (Step 3) are therefore N/A. **No TS leg** — the whole arc is ax25-ts-parity-invisible (no named flag added/changed). Lab redeploy (optional Step 0.5) deferred — `deploy-node.sh` targets the LAN `pdn-lab` and must run from a box with LAN + SSH access.

**Amended same day — libraries published as `lib-v0.13.0` at Tom's request.** Notwithstanding the "no library behaviour change" judgement above, Tom asked to ship the libraries to NuGet, so the six published packages (`Packet.Core`, `Packet.Kiss`, `Packet.Kiss.Serial`, `Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc`, `Packet.Ax25`, `Packet.Ax25.Transport.Abstractions`) were published at **0.13.0** via `publish-libs.yml` **`workflow_dispatch` (override_version 0.13.0)** off green `main` — every job's `push to nuget.org` step ran (confirmed "Your package was pushed"; the `NUGET_API_KEY`-missing warn step skipped, proving the key was present). **Gotcha for the next releaser:** the `publish-libs.yml` dispatch path does *not* create a git tag (unlike `publish-node.yml`'s `gh release create`), and this environment's git proxy 403s tag pushes, so **no `lib-v0.13.0` tag exists on origin** — nuget.org has 0.13.0 but `git tag -l 'lib-v*'` still shows `0.12.0` as latest. The next library release must therefore bump to **`lib-v0.14.0`+** (computing from the tag list alone would collide), and someone with push rights should create the `lib-v0.13.0` tag retroactively for the record. Downstream `axcall`/`packet-term-tui` pin-bumps (Step 3) remain out of this environment's repo scope; bump them to `0.13.0` where wanted.

### 2026-06-22 — Review follow-on: refactor-unlocked coverage + validator split

A "what did the refactor unlock?" pass on `claude/review-unlocked-followups`, all behaviour-preserving and parity-invisible. (a) **NinoTNC pump now testable** — the new `Packet.Kiss.Serial` `ISerialPortIo` seam made `NinoTncSerialPort` reachable: added an internal `NinoTncSerialPort.OpenForTest(KissSerialModem)` (+ `InternalsVisibleTo Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc.Tests` on both `Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc` and `Packet.Kiss.Serial`), and new `NinoTncSerialPortTests` (5) drive a fake `ISerialPortIo` through `KissSerialModem.OpenForTest` to cover the previously-untested ACKMODE TX-completion correlation (`SendFrameWithAckAsync` resolve / timeout / duplicate-tag), inbound classification + fan-out, and the dispose-fails-pending-acks teardown (Kiss.NinoTnc 95→100). (b) **Config round-trip property now exercises the new equality** — `NodeConfigArbitraries` generates valid `Applications`/`Apps` entries (with `Args`/`Capabilities`/`Environment` collections) and `NodeConfigRoundTripProperties` asserts them, so the `ApplicationConfig`/`AppOverrideConfig` value-equality added earlier is actually tested through serialise→parse (it was a no-op before — both sides were always empty). (c) **`NodeConfigValidator.cs` split** (831 LOC → per-area partials matching the `NodeConfig.*` layout: `.Ports`/`.Beacon`/`.Apps`/`.NetRom`/`.Management`/`.Tailscale`/`.Integrations` + root) — mechanical, all 20 validators preserved, usings scoped per file. Full suite green (Ax25 952, Node 1423/+2 skip, Kiss.NinoTnc 100, plus the rest); builds warnings-clean.

### 2026-06-22 — Review refactor follow-up: the two deferred extractions (no behaviour change)

Completed the two readability-only extractions the pass below deliberately deferred, on `claude/review-refactor-followup-extractions`. (a) **`Ax25Listener.DispatchInbound`** (the ~215-line inbound dispatcher) split into `TryInterceptConnectionlessTest` (the §4.3.4.2 TEST intercept), `TryRouteToCachedSession` (the cached-session reparse + XID/FRMR MDL routing + reconnect-SABM), and `HandleNoCachedSession` (the pre-session XID responder + SABM-accept + transient fall-through), so the top-level method now reads as a four-step dispatcher; all the load-bearing comments moved with their code, every early `return` preserved. (b) **`BuildSession`** — extracted only the genuinely safe slice: the ~45-line pure, closure-free per-session context seeding → a `static SeedSessionContext(local, peer, allowAccept, sp)` helper. The concurrency-sensitive closure web (`SendBytes`/`SendAndRearmT1Async`/`SendUpward` over `ctx`/`scheduler`/`ackmodeUnsupported`/the `sessionRef` late-binding) was **left intact by design** — extracting it buys nothing behavioural and risks the T1-rearm timing. Pure internal refactor; no named-flag/listener-surface change (parity-invisible). Ax25 952 + Node 1423 green; full solution builds clean.

### 2026-06-22 — Codebase-review refactor pass (no behaviour change)

A read-through review of the two largest libraries plus a test-coverage sweep, landed as five behaviour-preserving phases on `claude/codebase-review-refactor-vrp9ak`. No library behaviour changed, no named flag / listener-surface change, so the whole pass is ax25-ts-parity-invisible. (a) **Timer-name scheduler keys centralised** (`Packet.Ax25/Session/Ax25TimerNames.cs`): `T1`/`T2`/`T3`/`TM201` were bare string literals spread across `ActionDispatcher`, `Ax25Listener`, `Ax25Adapter`, `Ax25SessionBindings`, `Ax25ManagementDataLink`, coordinated only by prose — the #385 delayed-ack correctness depends on `Cancel(T2)` naming the same timer the link-mux arms; now `internal` consts (still valid switch-pattern cases, byte-identical behaviour). Also fixed a malformed double-`<summary>` on `Ax25Session.ResolveDmDegradeMatch` (the `ResolveNextState` doc moved onto `ResolveNextState`). (b) **`NodeConfig.cs` split** (1483 LOC → 9 per-area partials: `NodeConfig`/`.Apps`/`.Ports`/`.Beacon`/`.Services`/`.Management`/`.Tailscale`/`.Integrations`/`.NetRom`); the three non-implicit usings are referenced only by `NetRomConfig`, so they travel with it. Mechanical move, all 31 types preserved. (c) **Value equality for config records with collections** (`Configuration/ConfigEquality.cs`): de-duplicated the hand-rolled `Equals`/`GetHashCode` boilerplate `WebAuthnConfig`/`TailscaleConfig` shared, and brought `ApplicationConfig` (Args/Capabilities) + `AppOverrideConfig` (Environment) into line with the "every config record with a collection member compares by value" invariant they silently violated (no active bug — nothing compares them by value yet — but a latent footgun + inconsistency). New `ConfigCollectionEqualityTests` (per-field completeness guards). (d) **`Packet.Kiss.Serial` read pump now tested**: its only tests were reflection/constant shape checks because it was hard-wired to a concrete `SerialPort`. Added a narrow internal `ISerialPortIo` seam (public `Open(...)` unchanged) + `OpenForTest`; new `KissSerialModemPumpTests` (12) cover framing, the timeout/zero-read continue paths, split-frame reassembly, terminal-exception fault, `IAx25Transport` Data-filtering + `ReceivedAt` stamping, all send/parameter encodings, and dispose idempotency/ordering. (e) **Braces enforced** (`.editorconfig` IDE0011 = warning ⇒ build-gated under TreatWarningsAsErrors): `dotnet format` added curly braces to every single-line control-flow body across src + tests + tools (254 files, mechanical). (f) **Further low-risk dedup**: `Ax25Listener.SendAndTraceAsync` (the send-then-trace pattern its three connectionless send paths repeated); `TailscaleStatusSnapshot.Starting`/`.Faulted` factories + a `CleanupChildAsync` helper in the sidecar supervisor (the snapshot ctor was repeated 5×, the post-exit cleanup twice); a `PortSupervisor.TryGetRunning(id)` accessor for the `lock (ports) { TryGetValue }` read (the mutation/query lock sites left as-is). (g) **More test coverage** for the review's other gaps: Mcp `FrameDecoder` edge paths (+7: explicit framing overrides, KISS-no-data rejection, mod-128 decode, unknown-PID/empty-info), Axudp `AxudpSocket` (+3: null-frame guard, too-short drop, valid-FCS-unparseable-body), Agw `AgwClient` (+4: `GetPortInfoAsync` parsing, connect timeout, duplicate-session). Full suite green throughout (Ax25 952, Node 1423/+2 skip, NetRom 280, Rhp2 248, Kiss 83, Kiss.Serial 19, Aprs 143, Agw 22, Mcp 33, Core 42, Axudp 7, Kiss.NinoTnc 95, Ax25.Properties 84). Two readability-only extractions deliberately *deferred* — `Ax25Listener.DispatchInbound` and `BuildSession` — as touch-the-concurrency-seam-for-no-behaviour-gain follow-ups (the reviewer flagged `BuildSession` as highest-risk); the deliberate exhaustive `ActionDispatcher` switch was kept as-is (its compile-time exhaustiveness has caught real bugs).

### 2026-06-21 — Guide achievability pass + README package-matrix sync

Follow-up to the `IAx25Transport` rewrite below, after a critical "could a reader actually build this from the guide alone?" review. (a) **NET/ROM achievability gap closed** (`guide/07-netrom.md`): the chapter showed the pure-NET/ROM pieces but hand-waved the three glue points to the AX.25 stack, so a reader could build the NODES routing observer but not close the forwarding/circuit loop. Added a *Wiring it onto your AX.25 stack* section with verified connective snippets — inbound `DataLinkDataIndication{Pid:0xCF}` → `NetRomPacket.TryParse` → `CircuitManager.OnPacket`; outbound `SendPacket` → `listener.SendData(interlink, packet.ToBytes(), PidNetRom)`; advertising via `routing.BuildAdvertisement` → `NodesBroadcastBuilder.Build` → `SendUiAsync("NODES")` — and a named caveat that interlink session lifecycle is the remaining work `NetRomService` owns. (b) **NinoTNC mischaracterised as a software modem** corrected in `guide/index.md` (it's a hardware TNC). (c) Small `using Packet.Kiss;` added to the ch.2 NinoTNC snippet so it compiles. (d) **`README.md` library matrix** synced to the transport refactor: the retired `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions` row replaced with `Packet.Ax25.Transport.Abstractions`, and `KissFrame` moved from the `Packet.Core` cell to `Packet.Kiss` (where it now lives). Docs-only; no library/behaviour change.

### 2026-06-21 — Developer guide rewritten against the `IAx25Transport` seam (`guide/`)

Rewrote the `guide/` series (originally built on `IKissModem`, PR #501) against the shipped transport-layer refactor (lib-v0.12.0). The guide now teaches `IAx25Transport` (ns `Packet.Ax25.Transport`, package `Packet.Ax25.Transport.Abstractions`) as *the* transport seam — `SendAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte>)` + `ReceiveAsync() → IAsyncEnumerable<Ax25InboundFrame>`, with the two optional capabilities (`ITxCompletionTransport`, `ICsmaChannelParams`) feature-detected via `is`. KISS is reframed throughout as *one transport family* (`KissTcpClient`/`KissSerialModem`/`NinoTncSerialPort`) behind the seam, alongside `AxudpFrameTransport` (no KISS at all); the KISS codec types (`KissFrame`/`KissCommand`/`KissDecoder`) are mentioned only where the guide discusses implementing/decoding KISS yourself. `02-transports.md` re-conceived around the neutral seam (the spine of the rewrite); `01`/`04`/`05`/`06` reworked to construct a concrete transport → `Ax25Listener(transport, options)` → `ReceiveAsync`/`Ax25InboundFrame` (the `KissCommand` filtering dropped — the transport pre-filters); `03`/`07`/`08`/`index` swept. `axcall` chapter aligned with the updated `IKissModem`→`IAx25Transport` axcall. All cited types/methods/namespaces verified against `src/` at the 0.12.0 surface. Docs-only; no library/behaviour change, no ax25-ts parity leg. See `guide/`.

### 2026-06-21 — Developer guide: building tooling on the engine (`guide/`)

Added a top-level `guide/` series (`index.md` + `01`–`08`) documenting how a .NET developer builds their own packet-radio tooling on the public engine surface, *without* `Packet.Node` — progressing from transports (`IKissModem`) up through frames/callsigns, a monitor, a connected-mode client, a hand-rolled node, and NET/ROM, then pointing at the `Packet.Node.Core` building blocks for those who want the full host. Prose + inline snippets only (no new sample projects); structured as standalone getting-started material anticipating an eventual mkdocs/material site. Signatures cited were verified against source at write time (`Ax25Listener`, `Ax25Session`/DL primitives, `Ax25Frame` factories + `Ax25ParseOptions`, `NetRomRoutingTable`/`NetRomForwarding`/`CircuitManager`). Docs-only; no library/behaviour change, no ax25-ts parity leg. See `guide/`. *(Superseded by the 2026-06-21 rewrite above — the original transport chapter taught the now-deleted `IKissModem` seam.)*
### 2026-06-21 — OARC reporter retry-backoff test flake fixed (determinism, not a timeout bump)

Closed the `OarcReporterTests.A_transport_error_is_retried_then_accepted` flake flagged in the `lib-v0.12.0` release entry below (the "OARC retry-backoff starvation tail"). Root cause was a **test-harness ordering race**, not the production retry logic: the `StartAsync` harness starts the reporter's background send loop *before* the test installed its seams, so under CI CPU contention the node-up POST could fire before `CapturingClient.Responder` was set (it then defaults to Accepted → no retry armed) and before `RetryBackoffArmed` was wired (a null `Action?` → the signal the test awaits never fires) — `await backoffArmed.Task.WaitAsync(30s)` then timed out with the exact "condition not met within 30s" CI symptom. Fix is **test-side only**: the `StartAsync` harness now takes an optional `configure(reporter, client)` hook that runs after construction but *before* the send loop starts, and the test wires both seams through it — closing the ordering race and giving the field write a happens-before edge to the loop. The sibling `A_rejected_payload_is_not_retried` had the same latent hazard (robust-by-luck, but could silently exercise the accepted-not-retried path instead of the rejected one it means to cover) and was converted too. Production `OarcReporter` is **untouched** (no timeout bump, no production change). Verified: the race was reproduced on the old code (a temporary `Thread.Sleep` between start and seam-install gave the exact 30s timeout, then removed); the fixed test passed **200/200** under `stress-ng --cpu 16` + 4 concurrent worker loops and an independent **84/84** under separate CPU saturation; full `Packet.Node.Tests` 1415 passed / 0 failed / 2 skipped; warnings-clean. Test-only change — no library/node behaviour change, so **no release cascade**.

### 2026-06-21 — RELEASE: AX.25 transport-layer decoupling shipped (lib-v0.12.0 / node-v0.19.0)

The transport-layer refactor (Steps 0–6 below) is released. **`lib-v0.12.0`** → the six NuGet packages on nuget.org (`Packet.Core`, `Packet.Ax25`, `Packet.Ax25.Transport.Abstractions` (new), `Packet.Kiss`, `Packet.Kiss.Serial`, `Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc`; `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions` **retired** — removed from the publish set); verified pushed + indexed. **`node-v0.19.0`** → the amd64/arm64/armhf `.deb` GitHub Release. **Breaking** (`IKissModem`/`AckModeReceipt` deleted), so downstream .NET consumers updated in lockstep: **axcall v0.2.13** (`IKissModem`→`IAx25Transport`; built + 28 tests green against the published 0.12.0) and **packet-term-tui v0.2.13** (source-compatible currency bump; 14 tests green). **No TS leg** — the refactor was parity-invisible (no `Ax25ListenerOptions`/method/event name changed) and `ax25-ts`'s transport was already neutral byte-currency, so nothing to publish there. Release gated on a genuinely green `main` merge commit (`6aa0bac`: ci + interop both green) — which first required fixing a standing GHCR pull-auth failure that had been blocking interop (a stale `ghcr.io` cred on a self-hosted runner; fixed runner-agnostically with a self-cleaning `docker/login-action` step in interop.yml + clearing the studybox runner's stale cred). Known unrelated flake re-run to green: `OarcReporterTests.A_transport_error_is_retried_then_accepted` (OARC retry-backoff starvation tail — worth a proper fix, tracked separately; not transport-related). Follow-up: the PR #501 developer-guide branch (built on `IKissModem`) needs a rewrite against the new seam.

### 2026-06-21 — AX.25 transport-layer decoupling, Step 6: retire `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions`; `IKissModem` deleted (clean break)

The refactor's final step — the clean major break. `IKissModem` and `AckModeReceipt` are **deleted**; the surviving codec types (`KissFrame`, `KissCommand`) move into `Packet.Kiss` (same `namespace Packet.Kiss`, so zero consumer churn); the dead migration shim `KissModemTransport` is gone; and the `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions` project is **retired** (removed from the solution + `publish-libs`). The three KISS transports (`KissSerialModem`/`KissTcpClient`/`NinoTncSerialPort`) are now pure `IAx25Transport` (+ `ICsmaChannelParams`, + `ITxCompletionTransport` for tcp/nino) — KISS is fully an implementation detail behind the seam. `KissTcpClient`/`NinoTnc` keep a public KISS-specific `SendFrameWithAckAsync` (the `sequenceTag` is KISS wire detail beyond the neutral contract) but it now returns the neutral `TxCompletion`; the caller-supplied-tag / out-of-order-echo correctness is asserted on the wire rather than via the result struct. `grep IKissModem|AckModeReceipt` over the tree is empty. Verified: Ax25 952, Kiss 83, Kiss.Serial 7, Kiss.NinoTnc 95, Node 1415 green; full solution builds warnings-clean in published libs. **This is a breaking change to the published `Packet.Kiss*` packages** — ships as the next major-cadence `lib-v0.12.0`, with `axcall` (the external `IKissModem` consumer) updated to `IAx25Transport` in lockstep. The arc is complete: KISS is no longer a fundamental property of the stack — `Packet.Ax25` doesn't depend on it, the listener consumes `IAx25Transport`, and AXUDP constructs no KISS objects. (Follow-up: the PR #501 developer-guide branch, built on `IKissModem`, needs a rewrite against the new seam — sequenced after this publishes.)

### 2026-06-21 — AX.25 transport-layer decoupling, Step 5: delete the AXUDP KISS synthesis (the headline smell)

`AxudpKissModem` → **`AxudpFrameTransport`**, a native `IAx25Transport` that constructs **no KISS object at all**: the old `yield return new KissFrame(0, Data, body)` synthesis (the proof the abstraction was at the wrong layer) is deleted — `ReceiveAsync` yields `Ax25InboundFrame` directly, `SendAsync` sends the body+FCS. It implements NEITHER capability (no `ICsmaChannelParams` no-ops, no throwing ACKMODE) — a UDP tunnel has no CSMA and no TNC echo, so a consumer feature-detects their absence and degrades. The factory returns it directly (no shim wrap). Runtime class named `AxudpFrameTransport` to avoid colliding with the `AxudpTransport` *config record*. The AXUDP test now asserts the neutral API + that it offers no capabilities (the smell-gone proof); the interop AXUDP tests + LinkBench unwrap the shim. `Packet.Node` 1415 green; full solution builds clean. The only `new KissFrame` left in the tree is the legitimate KISS decoder. Next: Step 6 — retire `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions` + the axcall lockstep.

### 2026-06-21 — AX.25 transport-layer decoupling, Step 4: transports + host wiring go native `IAx25Transport`

The concrete transports, decorators, factory, and host wiring now speak `IAx25Transport` natively, and the transitional shim wraps are removed except for AXUDP (still `IKissModem` until Step 5). The KISS transports `KissSerialModem` / `KissTcpClient` / `NinoTncSerialPort` dual-implement `IKissModem` + `IAx25Transport` + `ICsmaChannelParams` (and `ITxCompletionTransport` for the two that speak ACKMODE — NOT serial, which has none, so a listener's capability probe correctly skips it); each `ReceiveAsync` filters to KISS Data and stamps `ReceivedAt` from an injected `TimeProvider` (KissTcpClient uses explicit-interface impl to resolve the name clash with its existing list-returning `ReceiveAsync`). The decorators `ReconnectingKissModem`/`PacingKissModem` now decorate `IAx25Transport` (Pacing requires an inner `ITxCompletionTransport`; Reconnecting replays the CSMA params on reconnect and forwards capabilities via `is`-checks). `AdaptiveKissTransport` re-points at `ITxCompletionTransport` + `ICsmaChannelParams` (it now surfaces `TxCompletion` rather than the KISS `AckModeReceipt` — behaviour-equivalent, only the unread `SequenceTag` dropped). `ITransportFactory.CreateAsync` returns `Task<IAx25Transport>` (KISS transports native; AXUDP wrapped by the shim inside the factory); `RunningPort.Modem`→`Transport`, `PortSupervisor`/`KissParamWriter`/MCP dispatch CSMA via `transport as ICsmaChannelParams`. Node test fakes (`InMemoryRadio`, `SharedRadioBus`, `FakeTransportFactory`, `EchoStation`/`RemoteStation`, the decorator/param/adaptive fakes) converted to native `IAx25Transport`; `tools/Packet.LinkBench` migrated to the neutral seam. Behaviour-preserving: `Packet.Ax25` 952, `Packet.Kiss` 83, `Packet.Node` 1415 green; full solution builds warnings-clean in src/ + tests/. Parity-invisible. Next: delete the AXUDP synthesis (Step 5), then retire `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions` + the axcall lockstep (Step 6).

### 2026-06-21 — AX.25 transport-layer decoupling, Steps 2–3: shim + flip `Ax25Listener` to `IAx25Transport`

The load-bearing edge cut. `Ax25Listener` now consumes `IAx25Transport`/`ReceiveAsync()` (yielding the neutral `Ax25InboundFrame`) instead of `IKissModem`/`ReadFramesAsync()`(`KissFrame`): the pump's `if (kiss.Command != Data) continue;` filter is **gone** (the transport delivers only AX.25 frames), and **`Packet.Ax25` no longer references `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions`** at all — the host↔TNC wire protocol no longer leaks into the data-link layer. The ACKMODE path is now a capability probe (`transport as ITxCompletionTransport` at construction → `SendAwaitingCompletionAsync`) that KEEPS the runtime `ackmodeUnsupported` latch as the backstop (a wrapping adapter exposes the capability even when the underlying modem doesn't support it — so the probe decides whether to *attempt*, the latch handles a runtime `NotSupportedException`). A migration shim `KissModemTransport` (in `Packet.Kiss`) adapts any legacy `IKissModem` → `IAx25Transport` (+ both capabilities), pre-filtering to Data and stamping `ReceivedAt` from an injected `TimeProvider`; it wraps the modem at the one production construction site (`PortSupervisor`) and at the test/interop/bench call sites, so the concrete transports stay `IKissModem` until the later steps convert them natively. The listener's own test fake (`LoopbackModem`) is converted to a native `IAx25Transport` (its non-Data-KISS inject path retired — that filtering now lives in the transport). Behaviour-preserving: `Packet.Ax25` 952, `Packet.Kiss` 83, `Packet.Node` 1415 all green; full solution builds under warnings-as-errors. Parity-invisible (no `Ax25ListenerOptions`/method/event name changed). Next: flip the host wiring + decorators/factory natively, delete the AXUDP synthesis, rename + retire `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions`.

### 2026-06-21 — AX.25 transport-layer decoupling, Step 0: the neutral `IAx25Transport` abstractions package

First step of the approved refactor that demotes KISS from a fundamental property of the stack to one transport implementation detail (the diagnosis: `Ax25Listener` consumes `IAsyncEnumerable<KissFrame>` then immediately filters to `Command==Data` and reads only `Payload` — a host↔TNC wire protocol leaking into the AX.25 data-link layer; the proof: `AxudpKissModem`, with no KISS on its wire, must *synthesise* `KissFrame`s to satisfy that read model). New **dependency-free** package `Packet.Ax25.Transport.Abstractions` (ns `Packet.Ax25.Transport`): `IAx25Transport : IAsyncDisposable` (`SendAsync(ReadOnlyMemory<byte> ax25)` + `ReceiveAsync() → IAsyncEnumerable<Ax25InboundFrame>`, default empty-stream), the neutral `Ax25InboundFrame` record struct (bytes + PortId + capture-time `ReceivedAt` + nullable `RadioMetadata` so #363 RSSI/SNR lands as a field not a contract break), and exactly two optional capability interfaces — `ITxCompletionTransport` (the de-KISS-named ACKMODE need; `TxCompletion` timing, no wire tag; documents the timeout-vs-absent distinction) and `ICsmaChannelParams` (the 4 TXDELAY/PERSIST/SLOTTIME/TXTAIL knobs as a CSMA-radio facet). Purely additive — nothing references it yet, full solution still builds/tests unchanged. Added to the `publish-libs` set. **Clean major break** chosen for the eventual `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions` retirement (axcall updated in lockstep). Subsequent steps: shim → flip `Ax25Listener` → flip host wiring → delete the AXUDP synthesis → rename family + retire `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions`. No `Packet.Ax25`/parity behaviour change (additive package only).

### 2026-06-20 — Flakiness root-cause: connect-confirm raised before `CurrentState` settles (engine fix)

Bug-hunting the interop suite on the live docker stack (LinBPQ/XRouter/net-sim) under CPU load surfaced — on the *first* run — `NetsimListenerScenarios.Listener_Connect_Then_Disconnect_Across_AFSK1200_Sim` failing: after `ConnectAsync` resolved, `sessionA.CurrentState` was `"AwaitingV22Connection"`, not `"Connected"`. Root cause is in the **core session engine**, not the test: `Ax25Session.DispatchEvent` ran the matched transition's action chain — which raises the upward DL signals callers block on (`DataLinkConnectConfirm` unblocks `Ax25Listener.ConnectAsync`) — and only **afterwards** advanced `CurrentState`. So a caller resuming from `ConnectAsync` on another thread could read `CurrentState` a hair before the dispatch thread settled it and see the pre-transition state. The window is tiny on an idle box (the dispatch thread almost always wins) but opens under contention — and it's a **latent race behind every `await connect-confirm; assert Connected` assertion** (~10 interop tests use exactly that shape via `WaitForSignal<DataLinkConnectConfirm>`), not just the one that flaked.

Fix: advance `CurrentState` to the next state **before** executing the action chain, so a signal raised by the actions (and any synchronous observer) sees the settled state — when the confirm fires, `CurrentState` is already `"Connected"`. Crash-safety (#225) is preserved: a thrown action now rolls `CurrentState` back to the pre-transition value in the `catch` (alongside the existing timer restore). Safe because nothing in the guard/action path reads `CurrentState` mid-transition — guards are matched off the pre-transition state *before* this block, and SDL verbs operate on `Context`/`scheduler`. Internal dispatch-ordering only; no `Ax25ParseOptions`/`Ax25SessionQuirks`/listener-surface change, so no ax25-ts parity leg. Validated: `Packet.Ax25` 952, `Packet.Ax25.Properties` 84 (FsCheck conformance), `Packet.NetRom` 280 all green; interop connect tests soaked on the live stack under load. See `src/Packet.Ax25/Session/Ax25Session.cs` `DispatchEvent`.

Also in this PR: **`OarcReporterTests.A_transport_error_is_retried_then_accepted` made deterministic** (it flaked the PR's own CI run — `condition not met within 30s: the retry after backoff` — the same test triaged on 2026-06-19 as "extreme-tail starvation, non-actionable"; it was actionable). The test advanced the `FakeTimeProvider` in a hammer-Advance loop racing the background send loop's park; for the reporter's *one-shot* retry-backoff timer the loop could advance past the registration window and then burn its 30s wall budget without ever firing it. Fix: a minimal internal test seam on `OarcReporter` — `RetryBackoffArmed`, raised the instant `Task.Delay(Backoff, clock)` has registered the timer (and before the await) — lets the test gate on "backoff armed" and then `Advance` deterministically (no-op in production; the periodic-poll tests keep the continuously-re-arming `AdvanceUntil`, which is robust). 60/60 under 7-hog load. See `src/Packet.Node.Core/Oarc/OarcReporter.cs` + `tests/Packet.Node.Tests/Oarc/OarcReporterTests.cs`.

### 2026-06-20 — Flakiness soak follow-up 3: real AB-BA deadlock in `NetRomCircuit` teardown (dump-proven)

The whole-suite repeat-under-load intermittently **hung** (not failed) `Packet.Node.Tests` — `dotnet test` killed at the `timeout`, last output `NetRomL3L4IntegrationTests.Disposing_the_service_cleanly_DISCs_its_interlink…`. `--blame-hang` named that test; the hang dump (`dotnet-dump … syncblk` + `clrstack`) showed a textbook **AB-BA lock inversion** in the published `Packet.NetRom` library, hit ~1/5 under load:

- **Outbound** (circuit `Disconnect` on teardown): holds `NetRomCircuit.gate`, then `Emit` invokes the `SendPacket` sink → `NetRomService.SendNetRomPacket` → `Ax25Listener.SendData` → `Ax25Session.PostEvent`, blocking on the **session lock**.
- **Inbound** (interlink pump): holds the **session lock** inside `PostEvent`, raises a DataLink signal that dispatches up to `CircuitManager.OnPacket` → `NetRomCircuit.OnPacket`, blocking on the **gate**.

Outbound took `gate→session`; inbound took `session→gate` — deadlocks when a circuit disconnect races an inbound interlink frame (load widens the window). A genuine production hang (a node tearing a circuit down while receiving traffic could wedge), not a test artifact. **Fix:** `NetRomCircuit` no longer calls the `SendPacket` sink while holding the gate — `Emit` queues into `pendingSends` (under the gate) and every public entry point flushes via `FlushSends` *after* releasing it, breaking the outbound `gate→session` ordering so the cycle can't form. Sends still happen synchronously before each method returns (no sink-visible behaviour change); loopback sinks are now safer (no nested gates); `Disconnect`/`Tick` use `try/finally` so a Request queued before an in-lock early return is still flushed. `Packet.NetRom` 280/280; **0 hangs in 14 full-assembly runs under 2-core load** (pre-fix ~1/5). Shipped as a standalone PR (production concurrency fix, separate from the test-only de-flakes). See `src/Packet.NetRom/Transport/NetRomCircuit.cs`. *(The same soak also surfaced `OarcReporterTests` retry-backoff as an extreme-tail starvation event — 124 reproductions all green, not in CI history, left as-is.)*

### 2026-06-19 — Flakiness soak follow-up 2: NodeCommandParser property-test verb-exclusion gap (found by whole-suite repeat)

Continuing the whole-suite repeat-under-load, it broke on iteration 6 in the FsCheck property `NodeCommandParserTests.Random_non_command_text_never_becomes_a_spurious_Connect_or_Bye` — falsified by `"MH"` (`got MhCommand`). Seed-dependent, so it only fails when FsCheck happens to generate a real verb. The property's `couldBeVerb` exclusion list — the predicate that decides which random strings the parser is *allowed* to recognise — had drifted from the parser's actual verb set: it was missing the **MH** family (the MHeard log verb, #454, 2026-06-17 — the parser accepts a ≥2-char prefix of `MHEARD`) and matched `PORT` instead of `PORTS`, so the exact word `"PORTS"` (which the parser recognises) was a latent next-break. Fixed the predicate to mirror the parser exactly (add `≥2 && prefix-of MHEARD`; `PORT` → `PORTS`). Test-only — the parser is correct. 50 runs × MaxTest 3000 = ~150k random cases under 2-core stress, green. See `tests/Packet.Node.Tests/Console/NodeCommandParserTests.cs`.

### 2026-06-19 — Flakiness soak follow-up: TrafficLogService subscription-timing race (found by whole-suite repeat)

Ran the whole standard suite on repeat under CPU contention to flush out anything the targeted fixes missed; it broke on iteration 2 in `TrafficLogServiceTests.Frames_traced_through_telemetry_are_persisted` — `TimeoutException: condition not met within 30s: both traced frames reach the store`. Root cause: a subscription-timing race in the **test**. `TrafficLogService` is a `BackgroundService` whose `ExecuteAsync` does `await Task.Yield()` *before* `telemetry.Subscribe(...)`, so the base `StartAsync` returns to the test at that yield — before the subscription is live. `NodeTelemetry` keeps no backlog for the SSE/Subscribe channel, so the two `telemetry.Observe(...)` calls the test fires right after `StartAsync` could be delivered to **no subscriber** and never reach the store → the 30s wait. CI CPU contention (and the soak's 2-core hog) delays the post-yield continuation, opening the window. Fix is test-only: gate the `Observe` calls on `telemetry.SubscriberCount > 0` (already a public test seam) so the observe-then-persist sequence is deterministic. The production startup gap is sub-millisecond and frames rarely flow exactly then, so the service itself is left unchanged. 80/80 under 2-core stress, green. See `tests/Packet.Node.Tests/Traffic/TrafficLogServiceTests.cs`.

### 2026-06-19 — CI test-flakiness pass: T2 coalesced-ack race + LOBBY connect race (reproduced off the self-hosted runners)

Mined ~90 recent `ci.yml`/`interop.yml`/`fuzz.yml` runs for intermittent failures and reproduced the genuine ones in an **ephemeral cloud sandbox** (a fresh .NET 10 SDK, deliberately NOT the self-hosted runner box) — running each suspect test individually and repeatedly, several under all-core CPU-hog stress, to prove or disprove robustness before touching anything. Findings:

- **The historical worst flake — `Packet.Node.Tests` inotify/FD exhaustion — is already gone on `main`.** The pre-`bd8ad11` symptom was a cascade of 40–53 integration tests failing at once with `IOException: configured user limit (128) on the number of inotify instances` from `FileConfigProvider`'s `FileSystemWatcher`, leaked across `WebApplicationFactory` boots. **config-in-DB (#473, `bd8ad11`, 2026-06-18) removed that boot-path watcher** — and every red run with this signature predates it. Verified empirically: the 219 `Integration` host-boots in one process held box-wide inotify FDs flat at 5 (the same 128 limit as the runner). No code change — documented so the next person doesn't re-chase a fixed flake.
- **`Ax25ListenerT2CoalescedAckTests.Five_frame_burst_coalesces_into_one_cumulative_RR_after_T2` — a real `FakeTimeProvider` arm-after-advance race (test-only fix).** Same class of bug as the 2026-06-09 AxPinger de-flake. The Connected in-sequence-I-frame SDL transition (`t26_i_received_yes_yes_yes_no_yes_no_no`, confirmed by decompiling `Packet.Ax25.Sdl`) runs its actions in the order `V(R):=V(R)+1` … `LM-SEIZE Request` (this is what arms T2 via the listener's deferred-grant `sendLinkMux`) … `Set Acknowledge Pending` (**last**). The test gated its `time.Advance(T2)` on observing `V(R)==N`, which is set *before* the arm — so on the **second** burst (where the frame that brings `V(R)` to 6 is itself the burst's first frame, the one that arms T2) the test thread could advance the virtual clock before `Arm("T2")` ran, leaving the timer due relative to the already-advanced clock so it never fired → "only 2/3 items after 2s". (The first burst can't hit it: T2 is armed during frame 1, four frames before `V(R)` reaches 5.) Fix: gate the advance on `session.Context.AcknowledgePending` — the transition's final action, so observing it true *guarantees* the preceding arm has run. 240 executions under full-core stress, green; full `Packet.Ax25.Tests` 952/952.
- **`LobbyAppIntegrationTests.Two_users_share_state…` — a real bind→listen connect race in the shipped LOBBY daemon (example fix).** Symptom was a hard 30s `"both got the banner"` timeout: a faulted `SocketApplication.RunAsync` (the test polls `Output`, never observes the task fault) manifests exactly as the banner-wait timing out. Root cause in `examples/lobby/lobby.py` `make_server`: a Unix socket file appears at `bind()` but only accepts after `listen()`, with a `chmod`+log between them — and the test's readiness gate is `File.Exists(socketPath)`. A connect in that window gets `ECONNREFUSED` → the bridge faults → the banner never comes. Under CI CPU starvation the daemon is preempted in the gap, widening it. Fix: `make_server` now binds+listens on a temp sibling path and **atomically `os.rename`s** it onto the final path, so the final socket only ever appears already-accepting (also hardens any field readiness probe that keys on the socket file, e.g. on daemon restart). 30/30 green end-to-end; 232/232 `Applications` tests.
- **Left as out-of-scope for this pass (with reasons):** the interop `LinBpqConnectTests`/`XrouterConnectTests` 20s peer-connect timeouts are real-radio timing flakes against the dockerised BPQ/XRouter stack (`Category=Interop`, need the compose stack), and the `connect-disconnect`/`netrom-route` flakes live in `packet-net/ax25-ts`, not here. The fuzz/healthcheck reds were infra (container-unhealthy, 360-min ceiling), not test bugs; the `AppGatewayApiTests` SPA-shell red and the `AppCatalogYamlTests`/`SystemVersionService` reds were genuine (since-fixed) regressions/data-staleness, not flakes.

Test-only + example-script changes; zero `Packet.Ax25`/`Packet.Node` runtime behaviour change, so no ax25-ts parity leg. See `tests/Packet.Ax25.Tests/Session/Ax25ListenerT2CoalescedAckTests.cs` and `examples/lobby/lobby.py`.

### 2026-06-19 — LAN discovery: advertise `_pdn._tcp` over mDNS (for the pdn mobile app)

The node can now advertise itself on the LAN over mDNS / DNS-SD so the pdn mobile app (packet-net/pdn-mobile) discovers it without typing an address. New `management.mdns` config block (`enabled` default-OFF, optional `instanceName`) and a `Packet.Node.Core.Discovery` namespace: `MdnsAdvert` is the pure planner (`Plan(config, version) → AdvertPlan?` — gated on enabled, a non-loopback `management.http.bind`, and a valid port; emits the `avahi-publish` argv), and `MdnsAdvertiserHostedService` supervises an `avahi-publish -f -s -- <instance> _pdn._tcp <port> <txt>…` child (mirrors the Tailscale-sidecar discipline: event-driven off `IConfigProvider.OnChange`, value-`Signature` reconcile so unrelated config edits don't restart it, TimeProvider capped backoff, total surface). **Multiple nodes commonly share a LAN, so identity is the callsign**: it rides a `cs` TXT record (the canonical id the client keys on) and is the default service instance name; `Identity.Alias` → `name` TXT, node version → `v` TXT. Registration is delegated to the system Avahi daemon (the conflict-free path on the Linux deb target — no second responder fighting port 5353); where `avahi-publish` is absent it logs once and stays dormant (the node is unaffected; manual add-by-address still works in the app), self-healing if avahi-utils is later installed. `.deb` now `Recommends: avahi-daemon, avahi-utils`. Additive optional config → no schema bump. Built with the verify-constraints + adversarial-review discipline (the review caught a config-change lost-wakeup, the missing `-f`/`--` avahi flags, and an unvalidated `instanceName` injection — all fixed). +17 Discovery tests, the round-trip property test now covers the mdns block, 298 Configuration+Discovery tests green. See `src/Packet.Node.Core/Discovery/`.

### 2026-06-18 — Self-contained update channel completed: feed host (OARC pdn-dist) + install.sh (#470)

The self-contained update channel is now usable end-to-end. The pieces that already existed — the apply helper (`packaging/packetnet-selfupdate`: download→sha256→unpack→flip `current`→restart→rollback), the channel-aware `POST /api/v1/system/update` (it already dispatches self-contained; the 501 is only the no-systemd case), the feed-reading `SelfContainedFeedClient`/availability probe, and the version banner — were missing two things: a **feed host** and a **first-install path**. Both built here; **no C# change** (the runtime side was already done).

- **Feed host = OARC static hosting** (`pdn-dist`, folder 4858, `https://pdn-dist.m0lte.compute.oarc.uk/`, valid cert). Serves `latest.json` + the per-arch `packetnet_<ver>_<arch>.tar.gz` + the install support bundle + `install.sh`. `latest.json` is host-agnostic (relative `file` + sha256), so the same manifest published as a GitHub release asset works verbatim on the bucket. See the `oarc-pdn-dist-feed` note + `docs/node-self-update-design.md`.
- **`packaging/install.sh`** — the `curl -fsSL …/install.sh | sudo sh` first-install: detect arch → resolve `latest.json` → download + **sha256-verify** the app tarball → lay it out under `/opt/packetnet/releases/<ver>` + flip the `current` symlink (the shape the apply helper expects) → install the units + the privileged update helper + polkit rule + config template (from the support bundle) → create the system user + dirs (mirroring the `.deb` postinst) → write `/etc/packetnet/update.conf` (FEED_URL) + a `PDN_UPDATE_FEED_URL` systemd drop-in (so the node's available-version banner works) → enable/start + `/healthz`-gate. Every path is `PDN_*`-overridable for testing.
- **`scripts/build-selfcontained-support.sh`** — single-sources the support bundle from `packaging/` (the ONE transform: the unit's `ExecStart` retargets from the `.deb`'s `/opt/packetnet/app` to the self-contained `/opt/packetnet/current` symlink — no duplicate unit to drift). Used by both the publisher and the smoke test.
- **`publish-node.yml`** — a step now uploads `latest.json` + the 3 tarballs + the support bundle + `install.sh` to pdn-dist on every `node-v*` tag (gated on an `OARC_TOKEN` secret; skips with a warning if unset so a release never fails for a missing secret). Mirrors the `m0lte/packet-term-web` OARC publish pattern.
- **`scripts/install-smoke.sh`** (wired into `deb-smoke.yml`) — proves `install.sh` against a `file://` feed (layout flip, support-file placement, feed config, sha256 refusal), no network/root/systemd. Plus a **real end-to-end run against the live OARC feed** verified by hand (downloaded + verified the actual 0.18.1 tarball, laid out the tree).

Remaining to fully close #470: set the **`OARC_TOKEN`** repo secret so the feed auto-publishes on tag (until then the feed is hand-seeded — 0.18.1 is live), and a confirm on a real systemd box (the curl|bash install with services). Shell + CI + docs only — zero `Packet.Ax25`/parity change.

### 2026-06-18 — Node-alias unification + OARC log-volume fix (node-v0.18.1, post-#459 polish)

Two follow-ups to the OARC arc, found dogfooding it live on the lab.

**Unified the node alias (the first real schema migration).** The node had **two** alias fields that could silently diverge: `identity.alias` (a display label — the OARC map name, the `{node}` banner token, the console name, `PDN_NODE_ALIAS`) and `netRom.alias` (the on-air NET/ROM mnemonic encoded into the 6-octet NODES-broadcast field). You could set them differently — the map showing one name while the network advertised another — and `netRom.alias` was never length-validated, so a >6-char value was **silently truncated** on the wire (`NetRomCallsign.WriteAlias` keeps only the first 6). Unified to **one** concept (the BPQ NODEALIAS model): `identity.alias` is now THE node alias — used for display AND as the NODES-broadcast alias (`NetRomService` takes its alias from there via a new `nodeAlias` ctor arg) — validated ≤6, with longer friendly text belonging in `services.banner`. Dropped `netRom.alias`. This is the **first production use of the #488 forward-migration seam**: a v1→v2 migration (`NodeConfigSchemaMigrations.Registry[1]`) folds a pre-v2 `netRom.alias` into `identity.alias` (existing `identity.alias` wins), caps it to ≤6 (faithful — only 6 ever reached the air; also guarantees the migrated config passes v2 validation, so no node fails to boot on a legacy over-long alias), and drops the dead field. `NodeConfig.CurrentSchemaVersion` → 2. UI: the NET/ROM-section alias input is gone; the Identity "Alias" field is the single source (`maxLength=6`, auto-uppercased), as is the setup wizard's. Named edge: a node jumping straight from a pre-0.17 YAML to 0.18.1 (skipping 0.18.0) loses `netRom.alias` (the YAML→DB import deserialises via the v2 typed model, which has no such field, and `IgnoreUnmatchedProperties` drops it — not fatal, just re-enter the alias); the incremental path (0.16→0.18.0→0.18.1, e.g. the lab) migrates losslessly via the DB blob.

**Quieted the OARC ingest HTTP logging.** The default `IHttpClientFactory` request logging emitted ~4 Information lines per POST, so the 60s link/circuit-status cadence steadily spammed the journal. `appsettings.json` now pins `System.Net.Http.HttpClient.OarcIngestClient` to Warning (other HttpClient logging untouched).

Node-host only — zero `Packet.Ax25`/parity change. 1395 node tests green (+ alias-validation, v1→v2-migration, and config-import-migration coverage). Ships as **node-v0.18.1**.

### 2026-06-18 — OARC network-map reporting (#459): the node reports itself onto the OARC packet map

pdn nodes can now appear on the [OARC packet-network map](https://node-api.packet.oarc.uk/) alongside the BPQ/XRouter estate. **Outbound reporting only** (the consume side — `/network`, `/history`, `/mcp` — is a named follow-up), **default-off**, with each telemetry category an independent UI toggle. Full design + the validated wire format live in [`docs/oarc-reporting-design.md`](oarc-reporting-design.md).

**Wire format validated against the real collector, not inferred.** The OpenAPI is sparse + untyped, so the format was probed live (synthetic callsign `Q0PDN`, reported up across every event type then marked down) AND cross-checked against the collector's open source ([`M0LTE/node-api`](https://github.com/M0LTE/node-api)). Key facts that shaped the build: open ingest (no auth); `202` is async queue-accept (validation happens off a RabbitMQ queue, so a malformed datagram is accepted-then-dropped); `time` is unix-epoch seconds; **`direction` must be `incoming`/`outgoing`** (a probe with `out` ingested but never surfaced — the single most important correction); locator is a strict 6-char Maidenhead (`^[A-R]{2}\d{2}[A-Xa-x]{2}$`) and **required** for node events; the L2-trace dialect differs (`dirn`=`sent`/`rcvd`, `l2Type` BPQ vocab so `SABM`→`C`/`DISC`→`D`, `ilen` only on I/UI, no `tseq` on supervisory). Typed routes need no `@type` (the generic/batch envelope's `@type` discriminator was mapped but is a deferred optimisation).

**What landed (node-host only — zero `Packet.Ax25`/parity change, so no ax25-ts leg):**
- **`NodeConfig.Oarc`** — master `enabled` (off) + per-category toggles (node-status/links/circuits default on once enabled; per-frame **traces off**, opt-in, RF-only by default), `baseUrl`, `publishExactPosition`, the two intervals. Validator (absolute http(s) URL + positive intervals, checked even when disabled), arbitraries, round-trip + validator tests.
- **`Packet.Node.Core/Oarc/`** — the 10 typed event DTOs (`OarcEvents`); `IOarcIngestClient`/`OarcIngestClient` (thin HTTP, never-throws, classifies 202→accept / 400-422→reject-no-retry / 429-5xx-network→retryable); the `IOarcStateSource` snapshot seam; `OarcReporter` (a `BackgroundService`: poll-diff loop emitting link/circuit up/down/status by stable id, node up/status/down on the master edge + cadence, the trace stream→l2trace mapping, a bounded **drop-oldest** queue with counted drops + a capped-backoff sender, hot-reload, best-effort node-down on shutdown); `NodeOarcStateSource` (captures live `ActiveSessions`/circuits/counters off `NodeHostedService`, tracks inbound via `SessionAccepted`/`IncomingCircuit`); `MaidenheadLocator` (validate + grid-centre lat/lon for exact position). Registered unconditionally in `Program.cs` (self-gates, so a hot-enable needs no restart).
- **Web UI** — an "OARC Network Map" config panel (toggles + base URL + cadence) with a locator-required warning when reporting is on but the node's grid is unset/invalid.
- **Tests** — 61 deterministic OARC/Maidenhead tests (client JSON shape + status classification; reporter behaviour via a fake state source + capturing client + `FakeTimeProvider`; the trace dialect; retry-vs-reject) + an env-gated (`OARC_LIVE_TEST=1`) live `Q0PDN` round-trip that posts via the real client and asserts read-back. 1385 node tests green; full live round-trip green.

**Named v1 limitations** (not silent — see design §9): circuit segment/byte counters report 0 (`NetRomCircuit` doesn't expose them yet); link `FramesResent`/RTT not surfaced; the RF-only trace filter is currently pass-through (all pdn transports are RF); batch ingest deferred. Closes #459.

### 2026-06-18 — Config-schema forward-migration seam (#488): the load-bearing follow-up to config-in-DB

Config-in-DB (#473) gave the persisted `NodeConfig` blob a `schema_ver` column but no way to *act* on it — an older-schema blob written by a prior build would have been deserialised straight through the current typed model (silently dropping a renamed field, or throwing → degrade-to-reseed and losing the operator's edits) on the first real shape change. #488 builds the seam so the first schema bump is one entry, not a fire drill.

**`NodeConfigSchemaMigrations`** (new) is a forward-only registry of JSON transforms keyed by from-version: `Registry[N]` rewrites a v`N` blob to v`N+1` as a `JsonObject` mutation — *before* deserialisation, so a rename/restructure/new-required-default is expressible without the old C# record still existing. `Migrate(root, from, to, registry)` is the dispatch core: **equal** → returns the blob untouched (idempotent); **less** → walks N→N+1→…→to applying each step and stamping `schemaVersion` after each (so a partial chain can't be misread as current, and a later step sees the prior step's version); **greater** → throws `NodeConfigSchemaException` (the fail-safe: an operator rolled the binary back onto a DB a *newer* build wrote — never run on, nor clobber, a future schema); a **gap** (missing step) throws rather than silently skipping. `NodeConfig.CurrentSchemaVersion` (new `const`, =1) is the single source of truth: `SchemaVersion` defaults to it, the store targets it.

**`SqliteConfigStore.Load`** now branches on the stored version: at-target → deserialise as-is (the common path); below-target → `ParseObject` → `Migrate` → `Deserialize(JsonNode)`, log the rendered `v{from}→v{to}` line, and report the *current* version (the row is lazily re-stamped on the next write); above-target → the `NodeConfigSchemaException` propagates to the boot fail-safe. A corrupt blob still degrades to `null` (re-seed) — distinct from a schema mismatch (throw), so `NodeConfigSchemaException` deliberately is **not** a `JsonException`. An `internal` ctor takes an explicit target + registry so the migrate-and-log path is exercised end-to-end with a synthetic v1→v2 — the mechanism is proven, not stubbed, even though the production `Registry` is still empty at SchemaVersion=1. `NodeConfigJson` gained `ParseObject` (string→`JsonObject`, rejects a non-object root) and a `JsonNode` overload of `Deserialize` sharing the canonical options.

**Packaging tidy:** `packaging/postrm` dropped the dead `rm -f …/packetnet.yaml.migrated` (that pre-0.17 sibling never existed — the real first-boot import marker is `.config-migrated` in the StateDirectory, `SqliteConfigProvider.MigrationMarkerName`, already removed by the `rm -rf /var/lib/packetnet` purge).

Capturing-logger discipline on the migrate line (assert the *rendered* `v1→v2` string, not just behaviour — a `LoggerMessage` arg-swap would still load but render a garbled line). Node-host only — zero `Packet.Ax25`/parity change. 1324 node tests green (incl. the new schema-migration + store-pipeline cases). Closes #488.

### 2026-06-18 — App-catalog ops (#475): regen script + de-brittled catalog test; O3/O4 resolved, O2 flagged

The catalog (`catalog/apps.yaml`, [`docs/app-catalog.md`](app-catalog.md)) is the curated "Available apps" index baked into the node `.deb`. Two recurring pain points addressed, two assessed.

**De-brittled the catalog parse test.** `AppCatalogYamlTests.Parses_the_real_shipped_catalog…` had encoded the exact versions + sha256s of the live catalog, so a routine pin bump (the bbs 0.2.35 / bpqchat 0.2.1 bump for node-v0.17.0) broke CI twice in one session. The test now asserts pin **validity + structure**, not values: each app's `version` is semver-ish, each `sha256` is 64-char lowercase hex, each artifact url is well-formed/https and embeds the id + the entry's version, all three RIDs (linux-x64/arm64/arm) are present, the vetted set (dapps/bpqchat/convers/bbs) exists, and both real artifact kinds (assets + deb) are exercised. A version/sha bump now keeps it green; only a structural regression turns it red. Exact-value coverage stays in `The_real_catalog_validates_clean` (validates the live file each run). Renamed to `…_with_all_three_kinds_and_valid_pins`.

**O3 — regen script (`scripts/regen-catalog.sh` + `.py`).** A bump is now `scripts/regen-catalog.sh`, not hand-editing six hashes. For each entry it resolves the app's GitHub repo (from `homepage:`, falling back to a built-in id→repo map), runs `gh release view --json tagName,assets` for the **latest** release, maps each RID to the matching asset (assets-kind by `<tool>-<rid>` filename; deb-kind by Debian arch; pdnapp by `.pdnapp`), and rewrites `version`/`url`/`sha256` **in place**, preserving comments, ordering, and name/description/icon/capabilities/homepage. The sha comes straight from GitHub's per-asset `digest` (no bytes downloaded — same value `build-deb.sh` copied by hand), so "vetted = Tom ran regen against a chosen release" holds. Idempotent (verified: corrupt an entry → regen restores byte-identical). `--check` is a drift guard (nonzero if any entry lags its latest release), `--print` emits a paste-friendly block. Documented in `docs/app-catalog.md` § Catalog maintenance; the `catalog/apps.yaml` header now points at it.

**O4 — manifest version drift: RESOLVED (empirical).** The earlier drift (bpqchat manifest 0.0.1 vs release v0.1.0; convers 0.1.0 vs v0.1.2) is gone — as of 2026-06-18 every vetted app's in-repo `pdn-app.yaml version:` equals its latest release tag (dapps 0.34.2, bpqchat 0.2.1, convers 0.1.3, bbs 0.2.35), so the spurious-"update available" condition no longer occurs. The regen `--check` mode is the standing guard against recurrence. O4 marked ✅ in the doc.

**O2 — apt-as-store complementary CLI channel: still BLOCKED, flagged.** Depends on the OARC apt repo (maintainer-owned, not yet stood up) **and** a design decision on how apt-installed apps integrate with catalog/discovery (does an apt app appear in "Available apps" as already-installed / a read-only badge; does the catalog advertise an apt name; do the two channels share a trust root). Not built blind — scoped questions captured in `docs/app-catalog.md` O2 and posted to #475, which stays **open for O2**.

Node-host + scripts + docs only — zero `Packet.Ax25`/parity change (catalog work is `Packet.Node*`-only, no ax25-ts leg). 13 catalog tests green. Partially addresses #475 (test + O3 + O4); #475 left open for O2.

### 2026-06-18 — Release train: node-v0.16.0 + lib-v0.11.0 + node-v0.17.0 (+ pdn-bbs v0.2.35, bpqchat v0.2.1, downstream Packet.* 0.11.0)

The consolidated [`docs/releasing.md`](releasing.md) Step-5 release ledger for this session's whole cascade — one durable record of what shipped when. It **complements, and does not restate,** two sibling entries: the per-feature engineering detail lives in the entries below (each `#NNN` has its own), the config-in-DB design lives in its own 2026-06-18 entry directly below this one, and the **previous** cascade's downstream fan-out (libs-0.10.0 / node-0.15.0 / ax25-ts-0.15.0) is the 2026-06-17 "Tracker reconciliation + downstream release ledger" entry — this entry is the *release record* for the new train only.

**Tags cut, on green `main`, in order.** **`lib-v0.11.0`** + **`node-v0.16.0`** were tagged on the same backlog-burn merge commit — the libraries and node version in lockstep per the doc — followed by **`node-v0.17.0`** on the config-in-DB + catalog-refresh merge. `publish-libs.yml` published the **six** NuGet packages (`Packet.Core`, `Packet.Kiss.Abstractions`, `Packet.Kiss`, `Packet.Kiss.Serial`, `Packet.Kiss.NinoTnc`, `Packet.Ax25`) at **0.11.0**; `publish-node.yml` built + install-smoked + published the amd64/arm64/armhf `.deb`s + `*.tar.gz` + `latest.json` + `SHA256SUMS` GitHub Releases for both `node-v0.16.0` and `node-v0.17.0`, and `publish-docker.yml` pushed the multi-arch GHCR images (`ghcr.io/packet-net/packet.net:0.16.0` and `:0.17.0`, both also `:latest` in turn).

**What `lib-v0.11.0` / `node-v0.16.0` carry — the §5.G backlog burn** (each item's own §17 entry below has the engineering detail; this is the inventory): Prometheus `/metrics` exporter (#457), the `MH` console verb + persisted heard log (#454), kiss-tcp half-open auto-reconnect (#464 — the `Packet.Kiss` read-idle liveness timeout + KISS-param replay), per-port NET/ROM QUALITY + PACLEN/N1 (#455/#458 — the `NetRom` per-port quality threading + the parity-safe `Ax25SessionParameters` N1 reseed), implicit-0 TxTail sent on the config cadence (#465), the `set_kiss_param` modem write (#466), the web-UI-builds-on-`dotnet publish` wiring (#468), the per-link T1/T3 link-troubleshoot view (#467), the Radix accessibility pass for overlays/menus (#478), the self-deriving-app bare-verb routing (#476), `Packet.Rhp2` becoming a standalone publishable shared codec (#474), and baking standard-port mail (IMAP 993 / SMTP 465/587 bind grant) into the shipped unit (#469). The library-touching pieces (`Packet.Kiss` reconnect liveness, the `NetRom` per-port quality, the `Ax25` listener/session tweaks) are why this train moved `lib-v*` and not just `node-v*`; none added a parity-tracked `Ax25ParseOptions`/`Ax25ListenerOptions`/quirk surface, so `parity-check.mjs` stayed green with no new ax25-ts named-flag leg.

**What `node-v0.17.0` carries:** config-in-DB (#473 — the `SqliteConfigProvider`; node config moves out of the `/etc/packetnet/packetnet.yaml` dpkg-conffile into `pdn.db` with a seamless first-boot YAML→DB migration, ending the dpkg keep/replace upgrade prompt structurally — full detail in its own entry below) plus a catalog refresh (the `catalog/apps.yaml` pins bumped bbs 0.2.34→0.2.35 and bpqchat 0.1.1→0.2.1, so both now show as available/installable from the control panel). Node-only — no library change rode `node-v0.17.0` (`lib-v*` was not re-tagged for it).

**The app side (separate repos, their own release flows):** **pdn-bbs v0.2.35** ([`packet-net/pdn-bbs`](https://github.com/packet-net/pdn-bbs)) — White-Pages (network-directory) consume (#36), receiver-side restart-granting/`FS !offset` (#38), per-class housekeeping lifetime defaults + `MaxMsgno` renumber (#39), inbound FBB forwarding over TCP / `FBBPORT` (#40), SMTP DSN + non-Latin1 handling and the mail-to-standard-ports default (#48, the BBS half of #469), and the bare-`b` sign-off + webmail polish + CI auto-release plumbing (#49). **bpqchat v0.2.1** ([`packet-net/pdn-bpqchat`](https://github.com/packet-net/pdn-bpqchat)) — the decoupled pdn-UI arc: a slot-mode SPA, the multi-user claim flow, scope-gated writes + settings, a persisted allow-list with an admin editor, the federation panel, and DMs; shipped as 3-arch static `.deb`s installable from the node catalog. Both are surfaced through the node-v0.17.0 catalog refresh above.

**ax25-ts** (the TS leg): mod-128 (extended) end-to-end wire interop coverage merged ([PR #70](https://github.com/packet-net/ax25-ts/pull/70)). This is **interop test coverage only** — no behaviour/named-flag change in `@packet-net/ax25`, so **no npm release and no `packet-term-web` pin bump** this cycle (Step 4 skipped per the doc); the C# `interop.yml` job builds ax25-ts from source, so the coverage is exercised without a publish.

**Downstream .NET consumers** (`docs/releasing.md` Step 3, being cut in parallel with this ledger): [`packet-net/axcall`](https://github.com/packet-net/axcall) and [`packet-net/packet-term-tui`](https://github.com/packet-net/packet-term-tui) bump their four `Packet.*` pins **0.10.0 → 0.11.0** and re-release the next patch each (the tag after their current `v0.2.11` — i.e. `v0.2.12`); check their Releases for the exact tags once the cascade settles. No `packet-term-web` leg (ax25-ts unchanged for npm).

**Infra note:** a **5th self-hosted CI runner (studybox)** was added + provisioned this session on a separate box, continuing the runner-host oversubscription relief begun with the 2026-06-17 capacity entry (~5 GB/job × N runners on one 16 GB host was the OOM/swap-thrash re-running interop/crossgen jobs).

This ledger entry is docs-only (no code/parity change); a tag push is not itself a plan event, but the *release* is.

### 2026-06-18 — Config-in-DB: `SqliteConfigProvider` (#473) — Phase 4 Slice-2's deferred piece lands

The node's live config now lives in `pdn.db` as a single versioned JSON-blob row (`node_config`, `id=1` singleton, `schema_ver`/`format`/`payload`/`updated_utc`), behind the **unchanged** `IConfigProvider`/`IWritableConfigProvider`/`NodeConfig` seam — so the reconcile path (`NodeHostedService` → `ReconcilePlanner`/`PortSupervisor`) and every consumer are byte-for-byte the same; only *where config lives* changed. **Why:** the old `FileConfigProvider` rewrote its own `/etc/packetnet/packetnet.yaml` **conffile** through the management API, so every dpkg upgrade saw the runtime mutation and prompted keep/replace. Moving config into `pdn.db` (the writable `StateDirectory`, which dpkg never tracks) removes the conffile entirely and the prompt **vanishes structurally**.

**Storage:** one JSON blob, not shredded tables — `NodeConfig` is a deep, polymorphic, frequently-evolving record tree (a `kind`-discriminated transport union, many additive sub-records); a blob round-trips the *exact* model the provider produces (provably zero behaviour change), where structured tables would demand a migration per field add and re-implement the union in SQL. The blob uses the **same** `System.Text.Json` options the management API serialises with (`NodeConfigJson` = camelCase web defaults + `TransportConfigJsonConverter`), so the `PUT /config` body and the on-disk bytes are byte-identical — one canonical serialisation. JSON (not YAML) keeps comments/formatting out of the load-bearing bytes and let the file watcher's `lastText` echo-suppression machinery be **deleted** (the DB is the single writer — no out-of-band edit, no echo). `SqliteConfigStore` follows the resilient `pdn.db` discipline (meta-less `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` — no `user_version` fight; WAL; fresh pooled connection per call; every op `try/catch(SqliteException)`; upsert).

**Migration (seamless, the non-negotiable):** on first 0.17 boot (row absent) the provider resolves a source in order — `--config` YAML (the lab's hand-tuned `/etc` file) → `PACKETNET_CONFIG_SEED` → `/usr/share/packetnet/packetnet.yaml.example` → in-code `NodeConfigTemplate` — parses + validates + saves it into the row, so M0LTE/tom's real callsign/ports/auth/tailscale carry over with **zero operator action**. A present-but-invalid source **throws** (a node never boots on broken config). Idempotency is structural: the import runs *only* when the row is absent, so a postinst re-run / restart / downgrade-then-upgrade cannot double-import (proven by test: a second boot reads the DB, and a hand-edit to the YAML between boots is ignored). An informational `.config-migrated` marker is written (the row, not the marker, is the authority); the source YAML is never auto-deleted (rollback floor).

**Write path:** persist-before-advance — `TryApply` saves to the DB *before* advancing `Current`, so a DB write failure does not advance `Current` (the node never runs on un-persisted config; it surfaces a `(store)` error). `PUT /config`/`/config/raw` + all port CRUD persist to the DB and raise the same `OnChange` the watcher used to; `PdnConfigApi`/`PdnPortsApi`/`NodeHostedService` needed **zero changes** (already store-agnostic). The hot **hand-edit-the-file** flow is gone (documented behaviour change); config is now edited via the web UI / API, plus new headless CLI **`pdn config export [--out]`** / **`pdn config import <path>`** (short-circuit before the web host like `pdn mcp`) for the export → `$EDITOR` → import workflow. `GET /config/raw` still serves the YAML view of `Current`.

**Packaging:** `packaging/conffiles` emptied (no conffile shipped → no md5 compare → no prompt); `build-deb.sh` stages the template to `/usr/share/packetnet/packetnet.yaml.example` instead of `/etc`; `packetnet.service` drops `/etc/packetnet` from `ReadWritePaths` (the `--config` arg stays — read-only import/inspect anchor); `postinst` keeps the chown-if-exists on a pre-existing `/etc` YAML for one cycle (so the importer can read it), creates nothing; `postrm` purge removes `/etc/packetnet/packetnet.yaml*` explicitly (no longer dpkg-managed). **Tests:** +21 (`SqliteConfigStoreTests` full-tree round-trip property incl. the transport union + `AllowedOrigins`/`Tags` sequence-equality; `SqliteConfigProviderTests` migration proof — YAML→DB import, second-boot-reads-DB, invalid-source-throws, seed fallbacks, `TryApply` persists + raises once, DB-failure-does-not-advance, faulty-subscriber isolation — asserting the **rendered** migration log line, not just the value). Four integration suites (Auth/Totp/WebAuthn/AppGatewayChallenge) updated: flipping auth on between boots now goes through the live write seam (`PUT /config/raw`, ungated while auth is off) instead of an inert YAML rewrite; AppPackages persistence asserted against the DB row. Full Node suite green (1311). Not a cross-stack parity item (a pdn/C# host concern; zero `Packet.Ax25` change). See [`docs/config-in-db.md`](config-in-db.md).

### 2026-06-17 — Backlog burn batch: set_kiss_param write (#466), self-deriving-app verb (#476), UI-build-on-publish (#468), Packet.Rhp2 shared codec (#474)

Four node-host/build issues landed together from the 2026-06-17 backlog burn (assembled as a local commit stack, then pushed). **#466** — the `set_kiss_param` MCP tool, previously a no-op, now writes the four standard KISS CSMA knobs (TXDELAY/PERSIST/SLOTTIME/TXTAIL) to the modem via a unit-testable `KissParamWriter` with range validation, gated through the supervisor's exclusive lock. **#476** — a service app's bare command verb (e.g. `BBS`) now reaches the app even when it bound a self-derived SSID that differs from the node-resolved `PDN_APP_CALLSIGN`, via a read-only `ILocalAppRegistry` seam with safe fallback for migrated/ambiguous cases. **#468** — the `web/packetnet-ui` Vite build is wired into `dotnet publish` (MSBuild targets, live API mode, stale-output cleanup), so a plain publish, `build-deb.sh`, the docker image, and CI all emit the current UI with no hand-staging. **#474** — `Packet.Rhp2` becomes a standalone publishable shared codec (the rhp2lib convergence) with convergence tests; the original issue's "Whatspac support in the RHPv2 server" half was a mis-filed gap and was dropped (WhatsPac is delivered by the separate-repo `whatspacd` client speaking to the existing RHPv2 server, per `docs/whatspac-client-design.md`). All node-host/build/MCP; zero `Packet.Ax25`/parity change.

### 2026-06-17 — KISS TXTAIL: implicit-0 default, sent to the modem on the config cadence + on change (#465)

TXTAIL becomes a **deterministic, always-sent** KISS param instead of an opt-in one. Until now `kiss.txTail` was unset/null by default and `PortSupervisor` skipped sending it (`if (kiss.TxTail is { } tail)`), so the modem kept whatever tail its firmware booted with — non-deterministic across modems. #465 settles the default at an **implicit 0** and sends it to the modem **unconditionally** on every apply: port bring-up, the regular KISS-param apply cadence, and a hot config change / reload — alongside TXDELAY/PERSIST/SLOTTIME. So a port that sets no tail (or no KISS block at all) still gets an explicit `0` on the wire.

**The taxonomy (settled by M0LTE, supersedes the 2026-06-07 "explicit-only, no default" framing below).** The need for a non-zero tail is a **modem + radio-audio-path-latency** property, NOT a channel/baud one: **0 is correct for most paths** — a NinoTNC into a fully analogue audio path, *even on a slow AFSK1200 channel*; a **non-zero** tail is needed only for a software modem (samoyed / Dire Wolf) or a NinoTNC driving a radio with a non-zero-latency audio path (NinoTNC re-added TXTAIL for a specific radio — see its release notes). The node can't infer audio-path latency, so the **per-port non-zero override is preserved** (`kiss.txTail: 5`) and wins over the implicit 0.

**Where the 0 resolves.** `ChannelProfiles.OverlayKiss` now resolves `TxTail = explicit ?? profile ?? 0`, so a profiled port carries an explicit 0 (the regression test that pinned `slow-afsk1200 → null` now expects **0**); a channel profile still must NOT *set* a non-zero tail (it can't know the audio path — the `slow-afsk1200` profile leaves it unset). `PortSupervisor.ApplyKissParamsToModemAsync` resolves `kiss?.TxTail ?? 0` and sends it unconditionally, which also covers the no-profile/no-kiss-block path the resolver passes straight through as null.

**Composes with #483's reconnect replay.** `ReconnectingKissModem` replays the last-set KISS params (incl. TXTAIL) onto a freshly re-dialed modem; now that the supervisor always sets TXTAIL, the replayed set carries the explicit 0 too — a new connection that starts at the modem's defaults is brought back to the deterministic tail the config implies. A new test pins that an explicit 0 is replayed (not skipped as "unset").

**Node-host/transport config only — zero `Packet.Ax25` surface change**, so the parity inventory + `parity-check.mjs` are untouched. Docs: the config template (`packaging/packetnet.yaml`) documents the taxonomy (0 for most; non-zero only for software modems / a latency audio path). Tests: `ChannelProfilesTests` (slow-afsk1200 → 0; explicit non-zero override survives the resolve), `ReconfigDeltaIntegrationTests` (TXTAIL=0 sent on bring-up with no KISS block; non-zero override sent + re-applied live on a hot change), `ReconnectingKissModemTests` (explicit-0 replay), plus an `InMemoryRadio` send-count to prove the always-send cadence. Full `Packet.Node.Tests` green (1270). Closes #465.
